name: Vincent Aleven
title: Professor
bio: Vincent Aleven is a professor studying intelligent educational systems, cognitive modeling, and metacognitive skills development. His research focuses on creating advanced educational technologies and tutoring systems that improve student learning outcomes through personalized, AI-based interventions.

name: Tutaleni Asino
title: Faculty
bio: Tutaleni Asino is a faculty member at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute who focuses on learning sciences and educational technologies. As Co-Director of the Learning Sciences for Innovators Program, their research explores how people learn and innovate through technology-enhanced environments.

name: Jeffrey Bigham
title: Associate Professor
bio: Jeffrey Bigham is an Associate Professor who develops human-centered AI systems and assistive technologies to improve accessibility for people with disabilities. His research spans augmented reality, virtual reality, crowdsourcing, and innovative tools that enable blind and visually impaired individuals to interact with digital and physical interfaces.

name: Patrick Carrington
title: Associate Professor
bio: Patrick Carrington is an Associate Professor at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute who researches accessibility and assistive technology, ubiquitous computing, and the design of physical interfaces. His work spans interaction and experience design, with particular expertise in sensing, haptics, and fabrication technologies.

name: Paulo Carvalho
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Paulo Carvalho is an assistant professor investigating how cognitive, metacognitive, and motivational processes of learners can inform the design of more effective, practice-first learning technologies. He designs, deploys, and evaluates AI-driven adaptive systems and studies how to scale them effectively into real classrooms.

name: Albert Corbett
title: Associate Research Professor Emeritus
bio: Albert Corbett develops and evaluates computer-based learning environments, particularly cognitive tutor technology that supports student understanding of mathematical and scientific concepts. His research applies computational modeling and data mining to advance the science of learning through integrated classroom and computer lab curricula.

name: Laura Dabbish
title: Professor
bio: Laura Dabbish studies the social effects of digital transparency, connecting social and organizational psychology with technology and design. Her research explores how digital transparency in collaboration tools, social networks, and workflows impacts privacy, identity management, and team interactions.

name: Sauvik Das
title: Associate Professor
bio: Sauvik Das directs the SPUD Lab and conducts research at the intersection of HCI, AI, and cybersecurity, focusing on how to design systems that empower people with improved agency over their personal data and experiences online. His work includes understanding and mitigating privacy risks from emerging technologies, using emerging technologies to address longstanding privacy and security risks, and creating systems that shift power towards people and away from threat actors.

name: Motahhare Eslami
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Motahhare Eslami's work investigates users' behavior around socio-technical systems using HCI, social computing, and data mining techniques to identify misinformed behavior and redesign systems for better user interaction. She focuses on algorithmic opacity as a cause of misinformed behavior and develops methods to add transparency into opaque algorithmic systems.

name: Jodi Forlizzi
title: Herbert A. Simon Professor in Computer Science and HCII
bio: Jodi Forlizzi is a pioneer in establishing design research as a legitimate form of research in HCI, with 20 years of advocacy for its importance in the field. Her current research focuses on designing educational games, robots, autonomous vehicles, and healthcare technologies that use AI and ML to adapt to people's needs.

name: Sarah Fox
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Sarah Fox is an Assistant Professor in the Human-Computer Interaction Institute at Carnegie Mellon University, where she directs the Tech Solidarity Lab. Her research examines how technological artifacts challenge or propagate social exclusions.

name: Mayank Goel
title: Associate Professor
department: Software and Societal Systems Department (S3D) and Human-Computer Interaction Institute
bio: Mayank Goel's research focuses on designing, implementing, and testing new sensing systems by repurposing and extending the capabilities of sensors and devices in our daily lives. He collaborates with medical professionals and designers to develop end-to-end solutions for health sensing, global development technologies, and novel interactions that can be evaluated in real-world environments.

name: Jessica Hammer
title: Associate Professor
bio: Jessica Hammer is an Associate Professor jointly appointed in the HCI Institute and Entertainment Technology Center whose research focuses on transformational games that change how players think, feel, or behave. She is an award-winning game designer exploring how game design and immersive experiences can drive learning, social change, and meaningful behavioral outcomes.

name: Erik Harpstead
title: Senior Systems Scientist
bio: Erik Harpstead is a Senior Systems Scientist at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute who focuses on games, play, and learning sciences. His research explores how interactive systems can enhance educational outcomes through human-agent and human-robot interaction.

name: Chris Harrison
title: Associate Professor
bio: Chris Harrison directs the Future Interfaces Group, which investigates novel sensing technologies and interaction techniques that enable powerful and natural human-computer interactions. His research spans emerging modalities including mobile computing, touch interfaces, and gestural interaction.

name: Ken Holstein
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Ken Holstein is an Assistant Professor at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute studying human-centered AI, responsible computing, and social computing. His research explores how to design AI systems that are fair, transparent, and aligned with community values through human-algorithm collaboration and participatory design approaches.

name: Jason Hong
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Jason Hong is a Professor Emeritus at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute who focuses on usable privacy and security, social computing, and ubiquitous computing. His research spans mobile systems, prototyping tools, and responsible computing practices to empower users in digital environments.

name: Scott Hudson
title: Professor
bio: Scott Hudson is a Professor and Associate Director for Academics at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute, where he conducts research on accessibility, physical interfaces, ubiquitous computing, and human-robot interaction. His work spans sensing, haptics, fabrication, and the development of tools that bridge physical and digital worlds.

name: Alexandra Ion
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Alexandra Ion directs the Interactive Structures Lab, which investigates and develops interactive design tools that enable digital fabrication of complex shapes and structures for novice users. Her research lies at the intersection of human-computer interaction, digital fabrication, metamaterials, and computational design.

name: Geoff Kaufman
title: Associate Professor
bio: Geoff Kaufman studies the psychological and social impact of fictional narratives, games, and computer-mediated interactions, focusing on how people simulate character perspectives through experience-taking. His work develops games and technologies as interventions for social change, including strategies to reduce bias, encourage pro-environmental behavior, and increase empathy.

name: Sara Kiesler
title: Hillman Professor Emerita of Computer Science and Human Computer Interaction
bio: Sara Kiesler is a Hillman Professor Emerita who studies the social and behavioral aspects of computers and computer-based communication technologies. Her research focuses on how digital systems affect human interaction, privacy, and personal information management in an increasingly transparent digital world.

name: Aniket Kittur
title: Professor
bio: Aniket Kittur is a Professor at Carnegie Mellon's Human-Computer Interaction Institute whose research on AI-augmented cognition explores how human and machine intelligence can be partnered to accelerate knowledge acquisition and innovation. His work spans social computing, visualization, collective intelligence, and cognitive psychology, with over 100 publications including 17 best paper awards.

name: Roberta Klatzky
title: Charles J. Queenan, Jr. University Professor of Psychology and Human-Computer Interaction
bio: Roberta Klatzky is an expert in cognition whose research examines the relationships between human perception and action, with a focus on spatial and haptic perception. Her work has significant implications for interface design and applications in medical visualization and virtual environments.

name: Ken Koedinger
title: Hillman University Professor
bio: Ken Koedinger is a Hillman University Professor of Computer Science who develops data sharing and analytics infrastructures for supporting innovations in learning, including DataShop and LearnSphere. His research spans cognitive modeling, intelligent tutoring systems, and learning science, with extensive work in bringing AI-enabled educational technology to schools through initiatives like the PLUS project and Carnegie Learning.

name: Robert Kraut
title: Herbert A. Simon University Professor Emeritus
bio: Robert Kraut is a social psychologist who studies the design and social impact of information technologies in small groups, homes, and organizations. His research examines how people use technology for communication, collaboration, and support, with particular focus on online communities and digital health applications.

name: David Lindlbauer
title: Assistant Professor
bio: David Lindlbauer is an Assistant Professor whose research focuses on understanding how humans perceive and interact with digital information, and building technology that goes beyond flat displays to advance capabilities in virtual world interaction. He creates and studies enabling technologies and computational approaches that control when, where, and how virtual content is displayed to increase the usability of Mixed Reality interfaces.

name: Marti Louw
title: Director, Learning Media Design Center
bio: Marti Louw is a design-based researcher who uses design as a systematic inquiry method to create technology-enhanced learning environments that are socially co-constructed, personally relevant, and emancipatory. Her work focuses on applied learning sciences for EdTech, disciplinary observation skills, citizen science, documentation in creative contexts, and emerging technologies in informal learning settings.

name: Nikolas Martelaro
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Nikolas Martelaro is an Assistant Professor at CMU's Human-Computer Interaction Institute whose lab focuses on augmenting designer capabilities through new technology and design methods. He blends expertise in product design, interaction design, human-robot interaction, and mechatronic engineering to create tools and methods that help designers understand people better and create more human-centered products.

name: Bruce M. McLaren
title: Professor
bio: Bruce McLaren is a professor and head of the McLearn Lab who researches how technology can support education through intelligent tutoring systems, digital learning games, and collaborative learning platforms. His work investigates how students learn more effectively when presented with educational games, worked examples, and AI-powered feedback mechanisms.

name: Amy Melniczuk
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Amy is an HCI instructor and researcher committed to designing equitable, evidence-based learning experiences through tangible and embodied technologies. Her work advances the theory and practice of tangible computing in education while championing inclusive, data-driven approaches that respect linguistic and cultural diversity.

name: Dominik Moritz
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Dominik Moritz is an Assistant Professor at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute focused on human-centered machine learning, data visualization, and interactive systems. His research emphasizes making data exploration and visualization tools accessible and usable for diverse audiences through interaction design and assistive technologies.

name: James Morris
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: James Morris is a Professor Emeritus at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute whose research spans distributed personal computer systems, software engineering, and functional programming. His work focuses on the intersection of systems design and user interface development.

name: Raelin Musuraca
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Raelin Musuraca is an Associate Teaching Professor and Director of the MHCI program who has spent over 30 years at the intersection of human-centered design, product strategy, and organizational decision-making. Her work focuses on aligning HCI education with product development realities and developing adaptive, risk-based approaches to innovation through frameworks like Assumption Artifacts.

name: Brad Myers
title: Professor
bio: Brad Myers is the Charles M. Geschke Director of the Human-Computer Interaction Institute and a renowned researcher in user interface design, programming environments, and human-centered AI. His work focuses on making programming more accessible through innovative interaction techniques, demonstrational interfaces, and developer experience research.

name: Meg Neumann
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Meg Neumann is an Assistant Professor at CMU's Human-Computer Interaction Institute whose research focuses on accessibility, inclusive design, and design for marketplaces and social impact. She combines deep industry experience from companies like Indeed, Apple, and Dropbox with entrepreneurial insights to develop frameworks for equitable and user-centered design at scale.

name: Amy Ogan
title: Associate Professor of Learning Sciences
bio: Amy Ogan is an educational technologist focusing on making learning experiences more engaging, effective, and enjoyable through the design and deployment of educational technologies. Her research spans learning sciences, human-centered AI, and games, with particular emphasis on international contexts and emerging economies.

name: Adam Perer
title: Associate Professor
bio: Adam Perer is an Associate Professor at Carnegie Mellon University whose research integrates data visualization and machine learning to create visual interactive systems for making sense of big data. His work focuses on human-centered data science and extracting insights from clinical data to support data-driven medicine.

name: Steve Rathje
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Steve Rathje is an incoming Assistant Professor whose research focuses on how digital technologies such as artificial intelligence and social media shape important psychological outcomes, including polarization, intergroup conflict, mental health, and well-being. His work has been published in top venues including Nature, Science, and PNAS, and has been supported by the NSF and Templeton World Charity Foundation.

name: Carolyn Rosé
title: Kavčić-Moura Professor of Language Technologies and Human-Computer Interaction
department: Human-Computer Interaction Institute, Language Technologies Institute
bio: Carolyn Rosé's research focuses on understanding the social and pragmatic nature of conversation and building computational systems to improve communication efficacy between people and with computers. She directs the TELEDIA lab and has made substantial contributions to automated analysis of collaborative learning processes and dynamic support for collaborative learning through intelligent conversational agents.

name: Dan Saffer
title: Assistant Professor of the Practice
bio: Dan Saffer is a product design leader with extensive experience designing devices, apps, websites, and robots across major technology companies. His research focuses on human-centered AI, human-robot interaction, and experience design, with particular expertise in microinteractions and service design innovation.

name: Julie Saunders
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Julie has over 25 years of experience in human-centered design, research and strategy, with a passion for understanding people's perspectives, behaviors, and unmet needs to conceive innovative experiences. She works as an independent consultant with companies in Web3, retail, and banking, and previously established digital user experience teams at Highmark Health focusing on healthcare experience design.

name: Skip Shelly
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Skip is a design leader with 30+ years of experience leading strategic projects across Fortune 100 companies, startups, and civil agencies in sectors including healthcare, manufacturing, and defense. At HCII, he teaches interaction design, service design, and directs the Master of Human-Computer Interaction program while mentoring students and facilitating industry partnerships.

name: Hong Shen
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Hong Shen is an Assistant Professor at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute whose research focuses on social computing and human-centered AI with an emphasis on responsible computing and ethics. Her work combines research through design methodologies with the development of tools and programming approaches to address the societal implications of AI systems.

name: Hirokazu Shirado
title: Associate Professor
bio: Hirokazu Shirado's research focuses on the role of social interactions and technology in the emergence of social order and collective problem-solving, particularly studying cooperative behaviors through social network interactions. He investigates hybrid systems of humans and machines, examining how artificial agents can facilitate human cooperation and address social dilemmas.

name: Daniel Siewiorek
title: Buhl University Professor Emeritus of Computer Science and Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Daniel Siewiorek is a leading researcher in ubiquitous computing and assistive technology, with a focus on wearable devices and accessibility solutions. His work includes emotion recognition systems, virtual rehabilitation assistants, and applications designed to improve health outcomes for users with disabilities.

name: John Stamper
title: Associate Professor
bio: John Stamper is an Associate Professor and METALS Program Director at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute who focuses on learning sciences and educational technologies. His research applies human-centered AI and data visualization to improve student learning effectiveness in online courses and educational systems.

name: Danielle Thomas
title: Systems Scientist
bio: Danielle Thomas is a Systems Scientist at HCII who leads the PLUS project, a hybrid human-AI tutoring system designed to improve math learning outcomes for middle school students. Her research focuses on uncovering insights into teaching and learning, developing effective tutoring systems, and leveraging AI to scale learning interventions while enhancing educator training.

name: Laura Vinchesi
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Laura is a human-centered design leader with over two decades of experience in visual and product design across B2C and B2B environments, with expertise in design thinking as a driver of innovation. Her work spans interaction design, service design, design system creation, and design thinking education, with a proven track record of establishing design infrastructure and delivering world-class training to professionals globally.

name: Yi-Chia Wang
title: Principal Research Scientist
bio: Yi-Chia Wang is a Principal Research Scientist at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute whose research spans Natural Language Processing, Computational Social Science, and Artificial Intelligence with emphasis on human-human and human-AI interactions. Her work focuses on developing technologies that enhance user experience, promote well-being, and advance trust and safety in interactive systems.

name: Randi Williams
title: Assistant Research Professor
bio: Randi Williams is an Assistant Research Professor at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute whose research creates sustaining bridges between communities and technology to drive innovation. Her work focuses on the intersection of AI for education, human-robot and human-agent interaction, and responsible computing.

name: Sherry Tongshuang Wu
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Sherry is an Assistant Professor in the Human-Computer Interaction Institute whose research lies at the intersection of HCI and NLP, aiming to support humans interacting with imperfect AIs through interactive debugging and correction. She has authored award-winning papers in top-tier NLP, HCI, and Visualization conferences and brings experience from internships at Microsoft Research, Google Research, and Apple.

name: Nesra Yannier
title: Senior Systems Scientist
bio: Nesra Yannier is a Senior Systems Scientist passionate about creating innovative educational technologies and learning experiences at the intersection of physical and virtual worlds to improve children's learning and engagement. Her work spans learning sciences, educational technology, and AI-powered systems like Intelligent Science Stations that have been shown to improve learning outcomes and are used in museums, schools, and after-school programs across the US and Europe.

name: Haiyi Zhu
title: Associate Professor
bio: Haiyi Zhu is an Associate Professor at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute who studies human-centered AI, social computing, and responsible computing with a focus on community engagement and policy design. Her research examines how AI tools can be developed and evaluated in partnership with affected communities, and how online communities can be shaped through thoughtful design of rules and norms.

name: John Zimmerman
title: Tang Family Professor of Artificial Intelligence and Human-Computer Interaction
bio: John Zimmerman is an interaction designer and researcher who investigates how to blend human and machine intelligence, with a focus on designing intelligent systems that people love and trust. His work spans interaction with AI systems, product attachment theory, public service innovation through social computing, and research through design as a scientific inquiry method.

name: Charlie White
title: Professor of Art
bio: Charlie White is the Regina and Marlin Miller Head of School of Art at Carnegie Mellon University, where he has led transformative initiatives including MFA program restructuring, undergraduate curriculum overhaul, and expansion of the school's facilities and enrollment. His leadership has focused on pedagogical innovation, faculty diversity, and strengthening the school's financial stability while maintaining CMU's artistic vision.

name: Lyndon Barrois Jr.
title: Assistant Professor of Art
bio: Lyndon Barrois Jr. is an artist and filmmaker who uses cinema as a means to explore temporal and geographical concepts including anachronism, simultaneity, and reanimation. His current work examines heist film and museum contexts to engage with legacies of colonial extraction, while navigating questions around color, control, taste, waste, and information layering.

name: Kim Beck
title: Professor of Art
bio: Kim Beck is an artist whose work spans drawing, print, installation, and sculpture, with pieces exhibited internationally at venues including the Walker Art Center, Carnegie Museum of Art, and the High Line in NYC. Her practice engages with the built environment and fiber-based materials, and her work is held in major museum collections including the Minneapolis Museum of Art and Denver Art Museum.

name: Elizabeth Chodos
title: Associate Professor of Curatorial Practice
bio: Elizabeth Chodos is the founder and director of the Institute of Contemporary Art Pittsburgh and serves as the Johnson Family Public Art Curator for Carnegie Mellon University. She specializes in curating major contemporary art exhibitions and surveys, with a focus on contemporary artists and investigative artistic practices.

name: Johannes DeYoung
title: Associate Professor
bio: Johannes DeYoung is an internationally recognized artist whose practice explores themes of animism at the intersection of computational and material processes. His works have been exhibited internationally in galleries and museums across multiple continents and featured in major publications.

name: Isla Hansen
title: Assistant Professor of Art
bio: Isla Hansen is an artist creating systems and objects for play, performance, sculpture, interactive installation, animation, and video, combining soft and hard materials with digital fabrication and real-time media. Her work explores cultures surrounding personal technologies, play, labor, children's media, and the relationship between bodies and technological progress.

name: Ranee Henderson
title: Assistant Professor of Art
bio: Ranee Henderson is an Assistant Professor of Art whose multi-disciplinary work focuses on representational, figure-based scenarios that explore experience-driven decision-making. Her artistic practice combines painting, illustration, and fine art to create diverse bodies of work exhibited across Canada and the United States.

name: Jongwoo Jeremy Kim
title: Professor
bio: Jongwoo Jeremy Kim is a historian of modern and contemporary art specializing in representations of the male body in painting from the 19th century to the present. His research examines artists such as John Singer Sargent, Francis Bacon, and David Hockney, with particular attention to queer visual culture and masculinity.

name: Sujin Kim
title: Associate Professor of Art
bio: Sujin Kim is an award-winning experimental filmmaker specializing in animated documentary, known for her experimental use of cutting-edge animation techniques and 3D visualization tools. Her work explores deeply personal narratives and collective traumas, often focusing on experiences shaped by historical and systemic violence.

name: Golan Levin
title: Professor of Art
bio: Golan Levin is an artist, engineer, and researcher interested in new intersections of machine code, visual culture, and critical making. His work combines creative twists to digital technologies that highlight human relationships with machines, expand the vocabulary of human action, and apply computation as a medium for critical inquiry and cultural innovation.

name: Clayton Merrell
title: Dorothy L. Stubnitz Professor of Art
bio: Clayton Merrell is a painter and printmaker whose work has been exhibited internationally and is held in collections including the Smithsonian Museum and the U.S. Department of the Interior. His practice spans traditional fine art mediums and large-scale public art installations, including a 69,000 square foot terrazzo floor at Pittsburgh International Airport.

name: Paolo Pedercini
title: Associate Professor of Art
bio: Paolo Pedercini is a game developer, artist, and educator who creates videogames addressing social issues such as environmental justice, religion, labor, and alienation under the project name molleindustria. His work examines the relationship between electronic entertainment and ideology, spanning digital art, academia, game industry, media activism, and internet folk art.

name: Rich Pell
title: Associate Professor of Art
bio: Rich Pell works at the intersections of science, engineering, and culture, with expertise spanning documentary video, robotics, bioart, and museum exhibition. He is the founder and director of the Center for PostNatural History, an organization dedicated to collecting and exhibiting life-forms altered through domestication, selective breeding, tissue culture, or genetic engineering.

name: Melissa Ragona
title: Associate Professor
bio: Melissa Ragona teaches and researches sound design, film theory, and new media practice, forging approaches from film studies, art history, and new media technologies to analyze contemporary time-based arts. Their current work examines Andy Warhol's recording aesthetics and explores the nexus between sound and image in experimental film.

name: Britt Ransom
title: Associate Professor of Art
bio: Britt Ransom is an artist whose practice explores the relationships between humans, animals, and environments through sculpture and installations created using digital fabrication processes. Her systematic work reflects observed microcosms and examines the entanglements between humans and other species through digital mesh development and material exploration.

name: Sharmistha Ray
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Sharmistha Ray's art practice explores the complex inheritance of multiple cultures through the lens of queer identity, immigration, and modes of abstraction in both Western and non-Western traditions. Working primarily in painting and drawing while also engaging in sculpture, installation, curation, and criticism, they co-founded the feminist art collective Hilma's Ghost as a collaborative model for research, artistic production, pedagogy, and community.

name: Jon Rubin
title: Professor of Art
bio: Jon Rubin is an interdisciplinary artist who creates interventions into public life that reimagine individual, group, and institutional behavior. His projects include Conflict Kitchen, The Last Billboard, and The Independent School of Art, and have been exhibited internationally at major museums and biennales.

name: Yoko Sekino-Bové
title: Assistant Teaching Professor of Art
bio: Yoko Sekino-Bové is a ceramic artist whose porcelain work has been exhibited nationally and internationally, including prestigious venues such as the NCECA Invitational Exhibition and the Icheon World Ceramic Biennale. Her practice combines technical expertise in glazing and form-making with a commitment to teaching ceramic arts, having conducted residencies and workshops across multiple countries.

name: Suzie Silver
title: Professor of Art
bio: Suzie Silver is a Professor of Art who has been creating queer performance and video art for over two decades, employing digital collage and appropriated media to explore themes of desire, sexuality, and social disruption. Her work celebrates exoticism, ecstasy, and camp while investigating divergent desires and imagining new futures through collaborative video projects and installations.

name: Rachael Starbuck
title: Visiting Professor of Art
bio: Rachael Starbuck is a sculptor and video artist who creates fluid feeling objects and videos sensitive to their environment, exploring connections through touch, desire, intimacy and communication across distance. She is a co-founder of the nomadic curatorial project Partial Shade and a founding member of MATERIAL GIRLS, a non-local artist collective of female-identifying sculptors and digital artists.

name: Maria Elena Versari
title: Visiting Professor
bio: Maria Elena Versari is an art and architectural historian whose research focuses on the 19th and 20th centuries, particularly at the intersection of art and technology, the international avant-garde, and totalitarian aesthetics. She has published extensively on Italian Futurism, avant-garde internationalism, Fascist aesthetics, and has curated major exhibitions on totalitarian architecture and early modernist sculpture.

name: Charlie White
title: Professor of Art
bio: Charlie White is an artist and academic whose work pushes the boundaries of representation across photography, film, animation, and digital media. With over 20 years of exhibition experience and 14 years in academia, he bridges art making, pedagogy, and school administration.

name: Jamie Wolfe
title: Assistant Professor of Art
bio: Jamie Wolfe is an animation director and artist whose practice centers on avant-garde approaches to character and narrative, exploring unconventional forms, movement, and storytelling through hand-drawn animation and bold visual palettes. Her work spans experimental animation, drawing, painting, and comics, with films screened internationally at major festivals and collaborations with notable musicians and media brands.

name: Alisha B. Wormsley
title: Assistant Professor of Art
bio: Alisha B. Wormsley is an interdisciplinary artist and cultural producer whose work explores collective memory and the synchronicity of time through the stories of women of color. Her practice spans video art, public installation, performance, and archival projects, with significant commissions and exhibitions at major institutions including the Carnegie Museum of Art, Mattress Factory, and Andy Warhol Museum.

name: Imin Yeh
title: Associate Professor of Art
bio: Imin Yeh is an interdisciplinary artist working in sculpture, installation, and participatory events that use print media as both a technical and conceptual tool. Her projects examine unseen labor and production through repetitive handcraft and explore alternative economies based on exchange and craft-based practices.

name: Shikeith
title: Visiting MFA Core Faculty
bio: Shikeith is a multidisciplinary artist whose practice interrogates sociopolitical structures that oppress marginalized communities, drawing on hauntology and African diasporic spiritual traditions to create magical realist photographs. His work challenges gender norms and engages viewers with invisible forces shaping perception.

name: Frankmarlin
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Frankmarlin is an artist whose practice explores themes of erasure, lineage, surveillance, healing, and the beauty of mundane Black experiences by looking into the invisible and asking critical questions. They hold an MFA from Carnegie Mellon School of Art (2025) and a BFA in Studio Arts from Kutztown University (2020).

name: Julia Betts
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Julia Betts creates multimedia works at the intersection of sculpture, performance, and installation, characterized by intentional unpredictability, unstable materials, and orchestrated situations of controlled disarray. Her practice explores the limits of materials and vulnerability through precarious circumstances that serve as metaphors for emotional and psychic states.

name: Jamie Boyle
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Jamie Boyle is an artist working with weaving and fiber techniques whose practice responds to living with chronic illness and the unpredictability of the body. Their work combines material engagement with conceptual exploration, informed by collaborations in dance, theater, and textile traditions.

name: Jenna Boyles
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Jenna Boyles is a Digital and Physical Computing Technician and artist who works sculpturally with electronics and reclaimed materials using craft-based techniques. Her practice includes research and workshops in electronic textiles, particularly soft circuitry and heat-sensitive fabrics, exploring the intersection of squishy bodies and hard-wired machines.

name: Andrew Thomas Dunn
title: Visiting Scholar
bio: Andrew Thomas Dunn's research explores the intersections of media, technology, and aesthetics, with a focus on how digital platforms shape contemporary culture and the public sphere. His work examines issues of personalization, gamification, and networked democracy through an ecological and critical media studies lens.

name: Jamie Gruzska
title: Special Faculty & CFA Photography Administrator
bio: Jamie Gruzska teaches photography and administers the college's interdisciplinary photo program while working in painting and printmaking, exploring archetypal image genres and traditional silver-based analog darkroom processes. He emphasizes how art making, particularly photography, serves as a diagnostic tool for students to forge identity and investigate their own motives.

name: Kellie Hames
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Kellie Hames is a printmaker and educator who serves as the Print Technician and Facilities Manager for the School of Art, maintaining traditional printmaking facilities and the Digital Print Studio. Her artistic practice explores themes of Midwest suburban landscapes and communities through various mediums, often utilizing a surrealist lens.

name: Rebecca Harrison
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Rebecca Harrison is a repair and fit expert and circular fashion advocate who founded Old Flame Mending, a Pittsburgh-based sewing service focused on extending the life of clothing and textiles. Her work explores resource exploitation, ecological and cultural landscape, and slow fashion practices.

name: Kristen Letts Kovak
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Kristen Letts Kovak is an Associate Teaching Professor and Senior Associate Dean who teaches undergraduate courses in drawing, painting, and aesthetic philosophy. Her artistic practice investigates connections between visual, perceptual, and cognitive patterning through surface articulations that explore the interplay of representation and abstraction.

name: Elizabeth Kurtzman
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Elizabeth Kurtzman is a playwright, performer, and professor whose research focuses on performances of fear, particularly in live horror events such as scare attractions, as well as the acting and embodiment of monsters and villains onstage and onscreen. Her dissertation examines how immersive experiences, audio performances, and virtual apps use sensory techniques to hook audiences and encourage engagement.

name: Alli Lemon
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Alli Lemon is an artist and curator whose multidisciplinary practice spans sculpture, ceramics, collage, drawing, and painting, with a focus on exploring the mysteries of sleep. She has taught at multiple institutions and founded the artist-run space maybeblue while actively participating in residencies and curatorial projects.

name: Paul Mullins
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Paul Mullins creates repetitive drawings and paintings that explore themes of class, place, memory, and masculinity, with recent works focusing on hand-drawn magazine fragments as reflections on social positions. He is an experienced educator who taught painting and drawing at all levels during his 15 years at San Francisco State University and continues to exhibit his work widely.

name: John Peña
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: John Peña is a multidisciplinary artist who explores the natural world and daily interactions through comics, sculptures, video works, and public art. His practice includes community-engaged projects like 'Larimer Stories' and site-specific installations such as '400 Million Years of Water' on the Fern Hollow Bridge.

name: John Guy Petruzzi
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: John Guy Petruzzi is an artist and educator working with watercolor painting to explore issues related to climate change, habitat loss, and extinction, informed by a lifelong passion for wildlife. His work utilizes a coded symbology derived from natural history and is embellished through universal form and color synergies.

name: Steven Sontag
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Steven Sontag is an Adjunct Instructor for the School of Art and Carnegie Mellon Architecture, currently pursuing a Master's degree in Design for Interactions. He brings expertise in custom furniture, objects, and sculptural design from his professional work as a fabricator and designer.

name: Sarah Tancred
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Sarah Tancred is a studio artist and educator based in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania who specializes in ceramic and sculptural practices. She holds an MFA with a Graduate Certificate in Women's, Gender, and Sexuality Studies and has exhibited nationally while conducting residencies at prominent clay centers.

name: Marvin Touré
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Marvin Touré is an Ivorian-American interdisciplinary artist who uses fictional narratives and objects of innocence to interrogate themes of love, loss, and memory. His practice spans sculpture, installation, and mixed media, informed by residencies at major institutions and numerous awards including grants from the Black Artist Fund and Peter S. Reed Foundation.

name: Mary Tremonte
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: Mary Tremonte is an artist, activist, and educator who works with printmaking in the expanded field, including printstallation and interactive silkscreen printing in public space. She is a founding member of Justseeds Artists' Cooperative and uses art as a means of social change, collaboration, and creating temporary utopias.

name: London Williams
title: Adjunct Professor of Art
bio: London Williams is a multidisciplinary artist who explores intersections of blackness through masculinity and sexuality across paintings, drawings, photographs, and mixed media. His work uses metaphors and symbols in materials and color to document the domestic interior and create intimacy, imagining a fictional home where experimentation informs explorations of blackness and queerness.

name: Bob Bingham
title: Professor Emeritus of Art
bio: Bob Bingham creates art systems utilizing people, live plants, and natural materials to address ecological issues and promote sustainability. His practice evolved from green mixed media installations to large-scale interdisciplinary projects addressing the interconnectedness between natural and built environments, including major urban ecological restoration and green infrastructure initiatives.

name: John Carson
title: Professor Emeritus of Art
bio: John Carson is an artist and curator who has explored the interface between high and low culture through various media including installations, soundworks, drawings, photographs, prints, and sculpture. His work spans public art projects, exhibitions, and performances internationally, with a focus on broadening audiences for contemporary art practice.

name: James Duesing
title: Professor Emeritus of Art
bio: James Duesing is an accomplished animator who has worked across traditional hand-drawn, early digital, 3D, and motion capture animation formats, exploring the medium individually and collaboratively in film, digital, installation, and print forms. His work is characterized by dark fantasy worlds and surreal human-animal hybrids that address sociological issues while offering entertaining and provocative commentary on human interactions and desires.

name: Andrew Ellis Johnson
title: Professor Emeritus of Art
bio: Andrew Ellis Johnson explores social and political issues across a variety of media, treating representation as an agency to awaken and combat torpor rather than as a hermetic mimetic tradition. His work, exhibited internationally, wrestles with boundaries between aesthetic, political, and moral orders through museum exhibitions, public collaborations, and socially engaged collective practices.

name: Elaine King
title: Professor Emerita
department: History of Art, Criticism & Museum Studies
bio: Elaine King is a Professor Emerita in the History of Art, Criticism & Museum Studies at Carnegie Mellon's School of Art who has established herself as a prominent freelance curator and art critic. She continues to write extensively for major art publications and remains active in the international art criticism community while engaging in cultural advocacy and arts education.

name: Carol Kumata
title: Professor Emerita of Art
bio: Carol Kumata is a sculptor and metalsmith who taught at Carnegie Mellon's School of Art from 1979 to 2021, working across traditional metalsmithing, mixed media, and found objects at scales ranging from hand-held works to room-sized installations. Her practice incorporates visual and word puns to reflect on object history and human situations, often exploring themes of fragility and ephemerality.

name: Joe Mannino
title: Professor Emeritus of Art
bio: Joe Mannino is a ceramic sculptor whose practice references elements of architecture and the colossus, exploring their relationship to order, values, politics, and ambitions of society. After 35 years at CMU, he retired in 2021 and continues to create works across ceramics, metal, wood, and photography.

name: Susanne Slavick
title: Professor Emerita of Art
bio: Susanne Slavick is an artist, curator, and writer whose work probes human conflict and environmental destruction through empathic unsettlement, exhibited internationally across multiple continents. Her curatorial projects have created forums for dialogue around war, its aftermath, and gun violence, while also exploring themes of environmental assault through works visualizing trees as historical records.

name: Robert Ramirez
title: Head of School
bio: Robert Ramirez became head of the School of Drama on August 1, 2022, bringing extensive experience from his role as professor and chair of the Department of Theatre and Dance at the University of Texas at Austin. He provides strategic leadership and vision for the School of Drama's academic and artistic programs.

name: Rachel Dzombak
title: Distinguished Service Professor
bio: Dr. Rachel Dzombak serves as Distinguished Service Professor of Design and Innovation and Sr. Innovation Advisor at Heinz College. She focuses on applying design and innovation methodologies to complex public policy and information systems challenges.

name: Pedro Ferreira
title: Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Pedro Ferreira is a Professor at Heinz College and the Department of Engineering and Public Policy at Carnegie Mellon University. His research focuses on the economics of information systems and digital platforms.

name: Akshaya Jha
title: Associate Professor
bio: Akshaya Jha is an Associate Professor of Economics and Public Policy at Carnegie Mellon's Heinz College and a Faculty Research Fellow at the National Bureau of Economic Research. His research focuses on applied economics and policy issues.

name: Daniel Nagin
title: Lester Hamburg University Professor of Public Policy and Statistics

name: Rema Padman
title: Professor
bio: Professor Padman's research addresses problems at the interface of healthcare, information technology and analytics, particularly focusing on healthcare decision support and operations. Her work applies advanced analytical methods to improve healthcare delivery and management.

name: Sean Qian
title: Full Professor
department: Civil and Environmental Engineering / Heinz College
bio: Sean Qian is a Full Professor jointly appointed at the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Heinz College, directing the Mobility Data Analytics Center at CMU. His research focuses on mobility analytics and transportation systems using data-driven approaches.

name: Anand Rao
title: Distinguished Services Professor
bio: Anand Rao is a Distinguished Services Professor of Applied Data Science and AI who teaches courses on operationalizing AI, responsible AI, LLM applications, and agent-based models with digital twins. His research focuses on the practical application and responsible deployment of artificial intelligence and data science technologies.

name: Shixiang Zhu
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Shixiang Zhu is an assistant professor of data analytics at Heinz College with a PhD in Machine Learning from Georgia Tech. His research interests focus on machine learning and data analytics applications.

name: Alessandro Acquisti
title: Trustees Professor of Information Technology and Public Policy
bio: Alessandro Acquisti is a Trustees Professor investigating the economics and behavioral economics of privacy, with research published in leading journals across diverse disciplines including PNAS, Journal of Consumer Research, and Marketing Science. His work on privacy in online social networks and predictability of personal information has been featured in major media outlets and has influenced policy decisions including changes to Social Security Administration practices.

name: Leman Akoglu
title: Dean's Associate Professor of Information Systems
bio: Dr. Akoglu's research spans data mining and machine learning with a focus on graph mining, pattern discovery, and anomaly detection in social and information networks. She directs the Data Analytics Techniques Algorithms (DATA) Lab and has received numerous publication awards and NSF CAREER recognition for her work.

name: Scott Andes
title: Professor of Practice
bio: Scott Andes is a Professor of Practice at Heinz College and Senior Strategic Advisor for Economic Development at Carnegie Mellon University, with extensive experience in federal policy and regional innovation. His research focuses on regional economic development, innovation ecosystems, and technology policy, informed by his leadership of major federal initiatives and academic work at Brookings Institution.

name: Martin Barrett
title: Associate Teaching Professor
department: Heinz College of Information Systems and Public Policy
bio: Martin Barrett is an Associate Teaching Professor with 20+ years of teaching experience focused on software engineering, operating systems, distributed systems, and software architecture. He is a certified Scrum Master who has supervised numerous student project groups and been active in the computer science education community since 1989.

name: Eli Ben-Michael
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Eli Ben-Michael is an Assistant Professor at Heinz College and the Department of Statistics & Data Science at Carnegie Mellon University. His research focuses on developing statistical and computational methods for causal inference and data-driven decision making, with applications to public policy and social science research.

name: Silvia Borzutzky
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Silvia Borzutzky is a Teaching Professor of Political Science and International Relations at Heinz College specializing in poverty reduction, social policies, and Latin American political economy. Her research focuses on social welfare systems, pension reform, human rights, and gender politics across developing countries and the United States.

name: Lee Branstetter
title: James M. Walton Professor of Economics and Public Policy
bio: Dr. Branstetter is a leading economist studying the impact of innovation and technology on economic growth, international trade, and labor markets. His research examines how technological change, offshoring, and policy decisions affect firms, workers, and societies, with recent focus on AI's productivity impact and the intersection of technology with work inequality.

name: Jonathan P. Caulkins
title: H. Guyford Stever University Professor of Operations Research and Public Policy
bio: Jonathan P. Caulkins is a University Professor specializing in systems analysis of problems related to drugs, crime, terror, and violence prevention. His recent work has focused on cannabis legalization policy, and he has authored over 140 refereed articles and 10 books while teaching quantitative decision-making to students across multiple continents.

name: George Chen
title: Assistant Professor of Information Systems
bio: George Chen is an assistant professor of information systems who works on machine learning for healthcare and information systems in developing countries, with a focus on forecasting applications like predicting patient hospital stays and optimal crop selling times. His research emphasizes developing reliable and interpretable nonparametric prediction methods alongside theoretical analysis and practical evidence to support decision-making.

name: David Choi
title: Associate Professor
bio: David Choi is an Associate Professor of Statistics and Information Systems who specializes in statistics and machine learning for network data, including community detection and exploratory data analysis. His research focuses on network models with latent variables, unsupervised learning approaches, and causal inference in the presence of social network effects.

name: Anthony P. Stanton
title: Teaching Professor of Graphic Media, Emeritus
bio: Anthony Stanton is interested in color reproduction, print attributes, and process control with a recent focus on digital media. He frequently collaborates with colleagues from the Printing Industries of America and has consulted extensively in the graphic arts industry.

name: Arthur A. Boni
title: John R. Thorne Distinguished Career Professor of Entrepreneurship, Emeritus
bio: Arthur Boni is an emeritus professor specializing in entrepreneurship, venture capital, and technology commercialization, with particular expertise in biotechnology and new venture creation. His research focuses on innovation strategies, leadership in entrepreneurial organizations, and broad-based ownership models in emerging companies.

name: Dale Hershey
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Law Emeritus
bio: Dale Hershey is an Associate Teaching Professor of Law Emeritus at the Tepper School of Business with extensive experience in legal practice and public service. His career has focused on antitrust law, class action litigation, and pro bono legal work in the Pittsburgh community.

name: David E. Tungate
title: Distinguished Service Professor of Law, Emeritus
bio: David E. Tungate is a Distinguished Service Professor of Law, Emeritus, with expertise in business contract law, corporate governance, intellectual property protection, and the implications of technology for business law. He has advised hundreds of students on legal careers and serves as a consultant and expert in corporate liabilities and licensing trends.

name: Evelyn M. Pierce
title: Teaching Professor of Business Management Communication, Emerita
bio: Evelyn M. Pierce is a Teaching Professor of Business Management Communication, Emerita at the Tepper School of Business. She is recognized for sustained excellence in teaching undergraduate and graduate business administration programs.

name: Fallaw Sowell
title: Associate Professor of Economics, Emeritus
bio: Fallaw Sowell is an econometrician specializing in time series modeling, forecasting, and structural economic models. His research applies advanced econometric techniques to problems in energy systems, macroeconomics, and policy analysis.

name: Finn E. Kydland
title: University Professor of Economics and Richard P. Simmons Distinguished Professor, Emeritus
bio: Finn Kydland is a Nobel Laureate in Economic Science (2004) and University Professor of Economics at CMU's Tepper School of Business. His research focuses on macroeconomic dynamics, economic theory, and the analysis of time inconsistency in economic policy and business cycles.

name: Geoffrey Hitch
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Acting and Business Communications, Emeritus
bio: Geoffrey Hitch is an Associate Teaching Professor of Acting and Business Communications whose research focuses on live communication and executive presentations. He has extensive experience consulting with organizations on presentation skills and has worked with numerous corporations including Cognizant Technology Solutions, Giant Eagle, and law firms.

name: Gérard P. Cornuéjols
title: IBM University Professor of Operations Research, Emeritus
bio: Gérard P. Cornuéjols is an IBM University Professor Emeritus of Operations Research at Carnegie Mellon University's Tepper School of Business, specializing in integer programming, optimization theory, and cutting plane methods. His research focuses on developing theoretical foundations and practical algorithms for solving complex discrete optimization problems, with particular emphasis on polyhedral methods and cut-generating functions.

name: Jay Apt
title: Professor Emeritus
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Jay Apt is a Professor Emeritus at Carnegie Mellon's Tepper School of Business and Department of Engineering and Public Policy who has authored over 120 peer-reviewed scientific papers on electric power grid reliability, renewable energy, and low-carbon technologies. His research focuses on mathematical, regulatory, and market characteristics of renewable power systems, low-carbon economics, and physical risk management in energy infrastructure.

name: John Hooker
title: T. Jerome Holleran Professor of Business Ethics and Social Responsibility; University Professor of Operations Research, Emeritus
bio: John Hooker is a University Professor of Operations Research and expert in the integration of optimization and constraint programming technologies, having pioneered logic-based Benders decomposition and decision diagrams as optimization methods. He is also deeply engaged in business ethics and cross-cultural management, having written multiple books on these subjects and founded the Journal of Business Ethics Education.

name: John R. O'Brien
title: Associate Professor of Accounting and Experimental Economics, Emeritus
bio: John O'Brien is an experimental economist whose research spans conventional and responsible banking, sports analytics, and experimental market design. His work applies quantitative analysis and experimental methods to understand financial decision-making, asset allocation strategies, and comparative banking practices.

name: Joseph B. Kadane
title: Leonard J. Savage University Professor of Statistics and Social Sciences, Emeritus
bio: Joseph Kadane is an emeritus professor specializing in Bayesian statistics, decision analysis, and applications to marketing and auction theory. His research combines statistical methodology with practical applications in e-commerce, consumer behavior, and legal consulting.

name: Kenneth B. Dunn
title: Professor of Financial Economics, Emeritus
bio: Kenneth Dunn is an emeritus professor specializing in financial economics with a focus on debt contract pricing, mortgage markets, and fixed-income securities. His research examines refinancing decisions, callable bonds, and the valuation of mortgage-backed and convertible securities using theoretical and empirical methods.

name: Marvin Sirbu
title: Professor
department: Engineering and Public Policy, Electrical and Computer Engineering, Tepper School of Business
bio: Marvin Sirbu is a Professor of Engineering and Public Policy with joint appointments in Electrical and Computer Engineering and the Tepper School of Business. His research focuses on the intersection of technology, policy, and business strategy.

name: Mildred S. Myers
title: Teaching Professor of Business Management Communication Emerita
bio: Mildred S. Myers is an emerita teaching professor specializing in business communication, corporate social responsibility, and organizational management. Her research and publications span decades of work on written communication, corporate ethics, and the strategic role of communication in business organizations.

name: Richard O. Young
title: Teaching Professor of Business Management Communication, Emeritus
bio: Richard Young is an expert in persuasive communication and how audiences decide, with a focus on cognitive approaches to business communication. His research and teaching emphasize the intersection of communication strategy, audience psychology, and organizational effectiveness.

name: Robert E. Kraut
title: Herbert A. Simon Professor of Human-Computer Interaction, Emeritus
bio: Robert E. Kraut is the Herbert A. Simon Professor of Human-Computer Interaction with a joint appointment at Tepper School of Business. He has conducted empirical research on online communities, the social impact of the internet, and the design of information technology for small-group intellectual work.

name: Robert M. Dammon
title: Dean Emeritus and Richard C. Green Professor of Financial Economics Emeritus
bio: Robert Dammon is a renowned financial economist who served as the ninth dean of Tepper School and is a three-time winner of the George Leland Bach Teaching Award. His research focuses on lifetime savings, investing, and asset allocation, with extensive publications in top finance journals including the Journal of Finance and the Review of Financial Studies.

name: Ronald Placone
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Business Management Communication, Emeritus
bio: Ronald Placone is an Associate Teaching Professor of Business Management Communication at the Tepper School of Business. He specializes in teaching and research related to effective communication in business and management contexts.

name: Steven Shreve
title: Orion Hoch Professor of Mathematical Sciences
bio: Steven Shreve's research spans financial mathematics and queueing systems, with expertise in continuous-time models using stochastic calculus for derivative securities, utility maximization, and optimal transaction execution. He also studies queueing systems in heavy traffic using diffusion approximations, with recent work applying these methods to limit-order books in electronic exchanges.

name: Alex Grelli
title: Adjunct Professor of Law and Entrepreneurship
bio: Alex Grelli is an Adjunct Professor of Law and Entrepreneurship at the Tepper School of Business. He teaches and conducts research at the intersection of law, entrepreneurship, and business formation.

name: Cody James Januszko
title: Adjunct Professor of Business Management Communication

name: Elias Towe
title: Professor
department: Electrical and Computer Engineering & Material Science and Engineering
bio: Professor Towe is an expert in electrical and computer engineering with a focus on materials science and semiconductor technology. His research spans photonics and advanced device applications.

name: Frank Demmler
title: Adjunct Professor of Entrepreneurship
bio: Frank Demmler is an Adjunct Professor of Entrepreneurship at the Tepper School of Business, bringing practical experience to the study of venture creation and business development. His work focuses on preparing students for entrepreneurial careers and understanding startup ecosystems.

name: JJ Xu
title: Adjunct Instructor of Entrepreneurship
bio: JJ Xu is an Adjunct Instructor of Entrepreneurship at the Tepper School of Business, teaching courses on entrepreneurship and new venture development. He brings practical industry experience to his instruction of aspiring entrepreneurs and business leaders.

name: Juan Rivera Lebron
title: Distinguished Service Professor
bio: Juan Rivera Lebron is a Distinguished Service Professor of Business Communication at the Tepper School of Business, where he teaches courses on organizational and corporate communication. His research focuses on how communication shapes organizational effectiveness, leadership, and strategic business outcomes.

name: Mary Butler-Everson
title: Adjunct Professor
bio: Mary Butler-Everson is an Adjunct Professor of Technology Strategy and Product Management at the Tepper School of Business. She brings expertise in helping organizations navigate technology decisions and product development.

name: Ming Ming Chapman
title: Distinguished Service Professor of Design
bio: Ming Ming Chapman is a Distinguished Service Professor of Design at the Tepper School of Business, focusing on design thinking and its applications in business contexts. Her research explores how design principles can drive innovation and improve organizational effectiveness.

name: Nick Psaris
title: Adjunct Professor of Finance
bio: Nick Psaris is an Adjunct Professor of Finance at the Tepper School of Business with expertise in financial markets and investment management. He brings industry experience and practical insights to the classroom and student mentorship.

name: Raji Sankar
title: Adjunct Professor of Entrepreneurship
bio: Raji Sankar is an Adjunct Professor of Entrepreneurship at the Tepper School of Business who brings practical industry experience to academic instruction. He focuses on preparing students for venture creation and entrepreneurial challenges in business environments.

name: Rebekah Apple
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Rebekah Apple is the Director of the Master of Medical Management Program at Heinz College, where she teaches and researches healthcare management and organizational dynamics. Her work focuses on improving healthcare delivery systems and preparing leaders for complex medical organizations.

name: Robert Kelley
title: Distinguished Service Professor of Management
bio: Robert Kelley is a Distinguished Service Professor of Management at the Tepper School of Business specializing in organizational behavior and leadership. His research focuses on how individuals and teams perform effectively within organizational settings.

name: Sean Ammirati
title: Distinguished Service Professor of Entrepreneurship
bio: Sean Ammirati is a Distinguished Service Professor of Entrepreneurship at the Tepper School of Business specializing in venture capital, startup development, and entrepreneurial ecosystems. His research focuses on how entrepreneurs build and scale successful ventures in competitive markets.

name: Sham Kekre
title: Distinguished Service Professor of Operations Management
bio: Sham Kekre is a Distinguished Service Professor of Operations Management with extensive expertise in supply chain optimization, logistics, and inventory management. His research and industry experience span global operations strategy, service parts logistics, and cost management across major multinational enterprises.

name: Divakaran Liginlal
title: Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Divakaran Liginlal is a Teaching Professor of Information Systems at CMU whose research focuses on information security, human-computer interaction, and decision support systems. His work examines privacy compliance, security investments, and human error in digital systems, with publications in leading journals including JMIS, Communications of the ACM, and IEEE-TKDE.

name: Randy Weinberg
title: Teaching Faculty Emeritus
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Randy Weinberg is a teaching faculty emeritus in Information Systems who specializes in innovative educational approaches including service-learning pedagogy and distributed student collaboration. His research focuses on global project management, sustainable development visualization, and strategies for recruiting and retaining underrepresented groups in information systems education.

title: Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences

title: Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences

title: Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences

title: Associate Teaching Professor, Emeritus
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences

name: Selma Limam Mansar
title: Teaching Professor, Emeritus
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences

name: Daniel C. Phelps
title: Associate Teaching Professor, Emeritus
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences

name: Randy S. Weinberg
title: Teaching Faculty, Emeritus
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences

name: Kyle Haden
title: Associate Professor of Acting
bio: Kyle Haden is an artistic leader, director, educator, and Equity actor who serves as Associate Professor of Acting and Senior Associate Head at CMU Drama, as well as Artistic Producer of ColLABo, a production development incubator. His work spans directing world premieres, leading new play festivals, and advancing BIPOC representation and equity in theater through his involvement with Black Lives, Black Words International Project and artEquity's Leadership Circle.

name: Amy Nichols
title: Associate Head, Director of Operations
bio: Amy Nichols serves as Associate Head and Director of Operations at CMU Drama, managing administrative operations including resource allocation, financial planning, and curricular planning. She brings experience from teaching Italian at the University of Pittsburgh and a background in Italian Language and Literature and Religious Studies.

name: David Holcomb
title: Associate Head, Director of Production
bio: David Holcomb oversees the production laboratory experience and teaches production management classes at CMU's School of Drama, where he has been since 2003. With over 30 years of experience as a production manager in theater, ballet, and live entertainment, he is deeply committed to management education and mentorship both within and outside CMU.

name: Dick Block
title: Teaching Professor of Design
bio: Dick Block is a Teaching Professor of Design at Carnegie Mellon with 33 years of faculty experience and extensive credits in Broadway, regional theatre, and opera design. He is co-author of 'Scene Design and Stage Lighting' and serves as Associate Head of School, supporting day-to-day operations and fostering collaboration between students, faculty, and staff.

name: Wendy Arons
title: Professor of Dramatic Literature
bio: Wendy Arons is a Professor of Dramatic Literature and Area Chair for Dramaturgy at CMU's School of Drama, where she teaches courses in dramaturgy and dramatic literature. Her research interests include performance and ecology, theatre history from the 18th and 19th centuries, feminist theater, and German theater, with published works on ecodramaturgy and historical translations of dramatic texts.

name: Kent Barrett
title: Associate Professor of Scenic Design
bio: Kent Barrett is a multidisciplinary designer and artist working across collaborative contemporary performance, experimental theater, and new media. His practice spans live performance, independent films, gallery installations, and architectural lighting, with a focus on design-driven, radical new work through his role as Co-Artistic Director of Valley Traction Arts Collective.

name: Claudia Benack
title: Teaching Professor of Voice
bio: Claudia Benack is a Teaching Professor of Voice at CMU Drama who teaches private singing lessons to Music Theater majors and co-teaches Musical Theatre Literature and Repertoire courses. Her extensive international singing career with major symphonies and opera companies informs her teaching approach, emphasizing vocal technique, stage presence, and artistic expression.

name: Stewart Blackwood
title: Assistant Professor of Composition and Sound Design
bio: Stewart Blackwood is a composer, sound designer, and creative coder who creates atmospheric soundscapes and interactive audio experiences using spatial and immersive audio technologies, live coding, and custom software development. His work blends digital synthesis, field recording, and effect processing to evoke original tonalities and emotions, often utilizing unconventional approaches with video game engines and bespoke tools created in Max/MSP, Pure Data, and the JUCE SDK.

name: David Boevers
title: Associate Professor of Production Technology and Management / Technical Director
bio: David Boevers is an Associate Professor and Technical Director who teaches production technology, scenic fabrication, stage rigging, and technical direction courses at CMU Drama. He is an ETCP-recognized rigging instructor and subject matter expert for theatre certification programs with extensive industry experience at venues including Yale Repertory Theatre, Hudson Scenic Studios, and Cirque Du Soleil.

name: C. Todd Brown
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Lighting and Production Technology
bio: C. Todd Brown is an Associate Teaching Professor who developed the Production Technology program and serves as core faculty in Lighting Design at CMU's School of Drama. With extensive career experience in live entertainment including roles at La Jolla Playhouse and Ringling Bros., he teaches courses in lighting design, technology, and production management while maintaining active design work across Pittsburgh theaters.

name: Tomé Cousin
title: Professor of Dance
bio: Tomé Cousin is a Professor of Dance at CMU's School of Drama who teaches contemporary and musical theater dance, theatrical/film intimacy education, and is a Certified Mental Health Aid. An interdisciplinary artist with an award-winning international career, he has composed over 70 original works spanning dance, theater, music, film, and installation, while serving as a leading advocate for theatrical intimacy and diversity in performance.

name: Brett Ashley Crawford
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Brett Crawford is an Associate Teaching Professor who runs the Arts Management and Technology Laboratory, investigating arts management and technology's impact on the performing arts. Her research focuses on management, audience engagement, technology, health, and sustainability across diverse arts contexts from regional theaters to Broadway.

name: Mark Domencic
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Mark Domencic is an accomplished pianist and music director with expertise in music theater and performance. He teaches in CMU's School of Drama and School of Music while maintaining a 25-year tenure as Artistic Director of ACT ONE Theatre School.

name: Gianni Downs
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Gianni Downs is an Adjunct Instructor in Scenic Design at CMU School of Drama who teaches skills-based classes including Vectorworks, Design for Screens, and Introduction to Design. His work has been nationally recognized with awards including a Kevin Kline Award and multiple nominations, and he is dedicated to innovative teaching practices in theatrical design.

name: Andrew Dunn
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Andrew Dunn's research explores the intersections of media, technology, and aesthetics, taking an ecological approach to understanding contemporary media culture. His work examines topics including personalization, gamification, and the networked public sphere.

name: Kaja Dunn
title: Associate Professor
bio: Kaja Dunn is an intimacy professional, diversity consultant, and associate faculty at Theatrical Intimacy Education with extensive performance experience across over 40 productions in 5 countries. Her work focuses on race and theatre, intimacy direction, and advancing equity and anti-racist practices in theater education and performance.

name: Rick Edinger
title: Associate Professor of Music Theater
bio: Rick Edinger is an Associate Professor of Music Theater and Area Chair of Acting & Music Theater at CMU Drama, where he serves as vocal coach for senior music theater students and directs annual showcases. He is an accomplished music director, vocal coach, and conductor with extensive credits in Broadway and off-Broadway productions, including work on new musicals and pre-Broadway workshops.

name: Mindy Eshelman
title: Associate Professor
bio: Mindy Eshelman is an Associate Professor of Costume Design with extensive experience in film, television, and commercial design, having worked with renowned directors and major brands throughout her career. She teaches courses in design for dance and film, explores costume history and its cultural significance, and is recognized as a 2019 Wimmer Faculty Fellow for her innovative pedagogical approaches.

name: Lucas Fedele
title: Assistant Teaching Professor of Dance
bio: Lucas Fedele teaches Dance at CMU's School of Drama, emphasizing classical techniques and oral traditions as pathways to embodied dramatic expression through consent-based, anti-racist, and trauma-sensitive practices. With over twenty years of experience as a performer, choreographer, and educator, he brings a commitment to theatre vitalization and has worked with notable figures including Billy Porter and Molly Smith.

name: Amanda Gladu
title: Special Visiting Faculty
bio: Amanda Gladu is a costume designer working across theater, dance, opera, and film with extensive experience at prestigious institutions including Lincoln Center Theatre, Manhattan Theatre Club, and Arena Stage. She teaches costume design courses and advises students through their design processes while maintaining an active professional practice.

name: Kristi Good, PhD
title: Assistant Professor of Dramaturgy
bio: Kristi Good is an Assistant Professor of Dramaturgy at CMU Drama specializing in theatre of trauma and uncovering suppressed narratives, particularly through new play development. Their scholarship and professional practice focus on dramaturgical work, accessibility in theatre, and supporting playwrights through script consultation and workshop dramaturgy.

name: Rob Handel
title: Professor of Dramatic Writing
bio: Rob Handel is a librettist and playwright whose works explore innovative operatic and theatrical forms, including surveillance capitalism opera and science fiction pieces. His collaborative works with composer Kamala Sankaram have been produced at major festivals and venues internationally, with recordings achieving critical acclaim.

name: Hugh Hanson
title: Associate Professor of Costume Production
bio: Hugh Hanson teaches costume production courses ranging from pattern development to specialized construction techniques, and mentors student costume designers on production assignments. With extensive experience as a draper and craftsperson across Broadway, opera, dance, and film, he brings decades of professional expertise to understanding textiles and costume creation.

name: Kevin Hines
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Production Technology & Management
bio: Kevin Hines teaches the scientific and engineering principles underlying theatrical fabrication and the utilization of traditional and emerging trade crafts. With over 25 years of experience at CMU, he maintains a freelance career as a technical designer and detailer for Broadway, touring productions, and construction projects.

name: Gwendolyn Kelso
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Gwendolyn Kelso is a professional actor, director, and educator with extensive experience in regional and off-Broadway theater productions. She is a founding member of Hedgepig Ensemble Theatre, a New York-based company known for their work on Expand the Canon, which advocates for gender equity and the production of plays by women and non-binary writers.

name: Randy Kovitz
title: Special Faculty, Acting
bio: Randy Kovitz is a veteran actor, fight director, and educator with extensive credits across theater, film, and television, including Broadway productions and numerous film and TV roles. He has developed and taught the Acting for the Camera curriculum at CMU for 15 years and is an award-winning screenwriter and film director.

name: Gary Logan
title: Professor of Speech and Dialects
bio: Gary Logan is a Professor of Speech and Dialects who has worked as a voice, text, and dialect coach on over 140 professional productions with major theatre companies including the Royal Shakespeare Company and Shakespeare Theatre Company. He is an acclaimed author, director, and visiting guest artist who specializes in Shakespearean language, phonetics, and dialects, with extensive teaching experience across leading conservatories and universities.

name: Owen Lowery
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Owen Lowery is an adjunct professor teaching Embedded Electronics, the integration of electronic and interactive components into props, sets, and costumes. As an interactive artist, they create situations that foster curiosity while prioritizing Universal Design to ensure all people can engage meaningfully regardless of background or accessibility needs.

name: Adil Mansoor
title: Special Visiting Faculty
