bio: Adil Mansoor is a Pittsburgh-based director and educator who centers queer folks and people of color in his artistic practice. He has developed new work with major theaters including The Eugene O'Neill Theater Center, Manhattan Theatre Club, and Public Theater, while also serving as a founding member of Hatch Arts Collective and former Artistic Director of Dreams of Hope.

name: Melissa Martin
title: Adjunct Faculty
department: Dramatic Writing & The John Wells Directing Program
bio: Melissa Martin is an award-winning producer, writer, and director working in independent film and theatre who teaches graduate-level screenwriting and directing for film at CMU Drama. Her work spans feature films, web series, and shorts that have screened at major international festivals including Cannes, Berlin, and Sundance.

name: Tony McKay
title: Associate Professor of Acting
bio: Tony McKay is an Associate Professor of Acting at CMU Drama with extensive experience as an actor, director, and playwright. His career spans Broadway and regional theater productions, directing at prestigious venues, and developing new theatrical works.

name: Michael E. McKelvey
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Michael E. McKelvey is an Assistant Professor of Music Theater & Voice who brings extensive experience as a stage director, music director, and arts administrator across numerous professional theatre companies. His career spans performer, educator, and producer roles, with a focus on musical theatre education and lesser-performed theatrical works.

name: Catherine Moore
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Catherine Moore is a Teaching Professor specializing in physical approaches to actor training, including The Viewpoints, Laban, Suzuki, and stage combat techniques. She has served as fight choreographer and movement director for over 85 productions and conducts international workshops on physical theatre methodology.

name: Amanda Olmstead
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Dr. Olmstead is an adjunct faculty member in dramaturgy whose research focuses on theatre history, choreography, embodied dramaturgy, and performance as a tool for communication. Her published work examines dance in musical theatre and the use of the ensemble body as a narrative device.

name: Joe Pino
title: Professor of Sound Design
bio: Joe Pino is a professor of sound design specializing in content and conceptual design with over forty years of experience creating soundscores for theatrical and film productions. His teaching focuses on process and helping students develop artistic practices through courses in sound design, synthesis, ear training, and audio technology.

name: Giovanna Reyes-Mir
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Giovanna Reyes-Mir is an accomplished professor of voice and choral conductor with over 20 years of professional experience in opera and musical theater across the Greater New York, Pittsburgh, and Washington DC areas. Her extensive knowledge of vocal technique, style, and repertoire has guided students and clients to success in prestigious programs, international opera houses, and Broadway productions.

name: Nica Ross
title: Associate Professor of Video & Media Design
bio: Nica Ross is an artist, educator, and cultural producer who creates participatory video installations and games that reveal and challenge social constructions reinforced by technology and performance. As Director of The Frank-Ratchye STUDIO for Creative Inquiry and Associate Professor of Video & Media Design, they foster transdisciplinary creative practice and critical examination of tools, technology, and assumptions in media making.

name: Brian Russman
title: Teaching Professor of Costume Production
bio: Brian teaches costume craft-focused courses including fabric dyeing, painting, millinery, mask making, footwear, armor, mold making and casting, and jewelry creation. With 15 years at CMU and extensive Broadway and regional theatre experience, he brings deep expertise in costume production and design to his instruction.

name: Emily Sapa
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Emily Sapa is an AmSAT-certified teacher of the Alexander Technique, flutist, and music educator who teaches AT at Carnegie Mellon University School of Drama and Point Park University Theatre Department. She works with actors, musicians, and dancers in her private practice, offering Alexander Technique workshops and classes throughout the Pittsburgh metro area.

name: Tina Shackleford
title: Teaching Professor of Stage Management
bio: Tina Shackleford is a Teaching Professor of Stage Management at CMU Drama with extensive professional credits across major theatres nationwide. She is committed to formalizing stage management education standards, mentoring emerging professionals, and advancing diversity and inclusion in the theatre industry.

name: Lawrence Shea
title: Associate Professor of Video & Media Design
bio: Lawrence Shea is an artist and educator working at the intersection of art and media technology, leading the Video & Media Design program at CMU's School of Drama. His practice encompasses experimental video, interactive digital media, video installations, and kinetic sculpture, with extensive experience collaborating on theatrical visuals, museum exhibitions, and large-scale public artworks.

name: Andrew William Smith
title: Associate Professor of Acting
bio: Andrew William Smith is an Associate Professor of Acting at Carnegie Mellon who teaches first and second-year acting students through heightened text and film/TV audition techniques. He is also the founding Co-Artistic Director of Project Y Theatre Company in New York City, which produces innovative new theatre with a focus on unheard and diverse voices, including the Women in Theatre Festival now in its 10th year.

name: Mary Ellen Stebbins
title: Assistant Professor of Lighting Design
bio: Mary Ellen Stebbins is a lighting designer working across theater, opera, dance, and music with collaborations at major institutions including Shakespeare Theater Company, Opera Philadelphia, and the Chicago Symphony Orchestra. She is a 2020 Bessie Award Honoree and 2019 Opera America Tobin Director-Designer Prize recipient with extensive experience in regional and international productions.

name: Ausar Stewart
title: Assistant Professor of Voice and Acting
bio: Ausar Stewart is a heart-centered acting teacher and voice coach who bridges theory and practice through innovative pedagogy, including his award-winning curriculum on holistic wellness for actors. He is a Designated Linklater Voice Teacher, experienced accent and dialect coach, and dynamic director who mentors students across all levels of the acting program.

name: Robert Thomson
title: Associate Professor of Lighting Design
bio: Robert Thomson is a widely recognized Canadian theater, opera, and dance lighting designer with over 40 years of distinguished international design experience. His work is noted for precision, visual poetry, psychological insight, and dramaturgical impact across repertory companies and major institutions.

name: Susan Tsu
title: Bessie F Anathan Professor of Design & University Professor
bio: Susan Tsu is an award-winning costume designer whose work has graced over 45 major theatres in the United States and international venues, with notable productions including Godspell, The Joy Luck Club, and recent designs for Oregon Shakespeare Festival and the Guthrie Theater. She is a passionate educator and curator who has led costume programs at major universities, co-curated international exhibitions including the Prague Quadrennial, and served as Chief Curator of major contemporary costume design exhibitions touring worldwide.

name: Rachel Tecora Tucker
title: Assistant Teaching Professor of Dance
bio: Rachel Tecora Tucker is an Assistant Teaching Professor of Dance who teaches in the Acting/Music Theater program at CMU's School of Drama. With extensive professional experience as a dancer and choreographer, including as an original cast member of Disney's Broadway production of The Lion King, she brings expertise in multiple dance disciplines and a commitment to sharing knowledge with young artists.

name: Jonathan Vandenberg
title: Assistant Professor of Directing
bio: Jonathan Vandenberg is a director and educator specializing in ancient and modern global classics as well as new theatrical work, with productions presented internationally, off-Broadway, and regionally. He leads the Text to Stage graduate directing sequence in CMU's John Wells Directing Program and serves as an advisor for departmental productions by MFA and BFA directors.

name: Lisa Velten Smith
title: Associate Professor of Voice
bio: Lisa Velten Smith is a Designated Linklater Voice teacher and professional actor with 20+ years of experience in Broadway, regional theater, television, and film. She specializes in voice coaching, dialect work, and has developed The Resilient Voice workshop that combines meditation science with vocal exercises.

name: Bria Walker-Rhoze
title: Associate Professor
bio: Bria Walker-Rhoze is a multidisciplinary artist and inaugural recipient of the Akram and Watfa Midani Career Development Professorship at CMU, serving as an accomplished actor, director, and choreographer. She teaches Acting 1 and 2, directs and engages in intimacy, movement, and fight choreography for School of Drama productions, and is the Artistic Associate of Fleetwood-Jourdain Theatre.

name: Chelsea M. Warren
title: Associate Professor of Scenic Design
bio: Chelsea M. Warren is a multimedia artist specializing in scenic and puppet design for theater, with expertise in new work, devised work, and spectacle theater. Her teaching approach emphasizes helping students find their artistic voice through a brave classroom laboratory that models practice and instigates creative revelation.

name: Miso Wei
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Miso Wei is an Assistant Teaching Professor in Production Technology and Management who brings extensive international theater experience from institutions like La Jolla Playhouse, Broadway, and Shanghai Disney Resort. Her research focuses on culturally responsive teaching pedagogy for international students and embodiment practices in leadership within theater and professional settings.

name: Kim Weild
title: Associate Professor of Directing
bio: Kim Weild is an award-winning live-performance director and Area Chair of the John Wells Directing Program who creates groundbreaking, inclusive bi-lingual performances integrating American Sign Language and voiced languages with d/Deaf and Disabled artists. Her practice investigates themes of otherness and difference across culture, language, gender, ability, and race, while exploring access aesthetics and disability performance innovation.

name: Andy Wolk
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Andy Wolk is an award-winning writer and director for film, television, and theater who has worked on acclaimed productions including The Sopranos, Damages, and Criminal Justice. He teaches in the John Wells Directing Program at Carnegie Mellon, where he earned his MFA, and brings extensive industry experience in directing and screenwriting across multiple mediums.

name: Yong Suk Yoo
title: Associate Professor of Directing
bio: Yong Suk Yoo is an international theater director and educator who merges ancient imagination with digital media to reimagine transnational identities through innovative performance forms. His work integrates new media technology into the performing arts, creating groundbreaking productions that foster critical thinking and challenge conventional theatrical boundaries.

name: TJ Young
title: Associate Professor of Dramaturgy
bio: TJ Young is an Associate Professor of Dramaturgy who specializes in New Play Development and serves as Co-Chair of the Awards Ceremony for the School of Drama. A Texas-born playwright and dramaturg, he is recognized for his original plays and service to the dramatic writing community through the Dramatists Guild and Kennedy Center American College Theatre Festival.

name: Beth Zamborsky
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Beth Zamborsky is a Master Scenic Artist with extensive professional experience in theatrical scenic painting, television and film design, and museum installations. At Carnegie Mellon since 2003, she teaches scenic painting and drawing while serving as the Paint Shop Manager, helping students develop strong design and theater technician skills.

name: Brannon Bowers
title: Co-Artistic Director
bio: Brannon Bowers is the Co-Artistic Director of the Center for New Work at CMU, which connects the School of Drama with Pittsburgh arts institutions to generate new theatrical works. Previously the Producing Director of the American Music Theatre Project at Northwestern University, Brannon has developed over 45 musical projects and worked with renowned artists and Tony Award winners.

name: Ben Brosche
title: Staff Accompanist
bio: Ben Brosche is a freelancing pianist, accompanist, and music director in Pittsburgh, PA, performing nationally and internationally across theater and jazz genres. He has extensive experience as music director and piano conductor for productions with CLO, Pittsburgh Public Theater, and other regional venues, alongside active jazz performance.

name: Catie Brown
title: Staff Accompanist
bio: Catie Brown is a Staff Accompanist and Music Director at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Drama since 2015, where she also teaches voice and serves as a vocal coach. She is an accomplished Pittsburgh-based musician and educator who regularly works with Broadway performers and regional theater companies.

name: Zachary Brown
title: Audio Manager
bio: Zachary Brown is the Audio Manager at CMU Drama, overseeing all audio equipment for productions and teaching sound stagecraft. He brings extensive professional experience in sound design and engineering from venues including the Wilma Theatre and multiple Pittsburgh-based theatre companies.

name: Ben Carter
title: Scene Shop Supervisor & Instructor
bio: Ben Carter is a Scene Shop Supervisor and Instructor in Pre-College Fabrication Technology with extensive expertise spanning carpentry, sculpture, machining, and advanced fabrication techniques. He has consulted on numerous feature films, television productions, and collaborated with multiple CMU departments and external clients including Lockheed-Martin and the US Air Force on specialized fabrication and robotics projects.

name: Thomas Feather
title: Lighting Manager
bio: Thomas Feather oversees the Lighting Department's shop, crew, and equipment while instructing Lighting Stagecraft 1 at CMU Drama. With extensive professional experience in theatrical lighting and immersive attractions, he supports student technicians and designers in achieving their creative lighting goals.

name: Anna Hanson
title: Associate Producer
bio: Anna Hanson is an Associate Producer at CMU's Center for New Work, managing operations and supporting the development of new theatrical projects in collaboration with faculty, students, and guest artists. As a multi-hyphenate theatre producer, director, and playwright, she is committed to new work development and building processes that balance artistic and human needs.

name: Todd R. Kulik
title: Props Manager
bio: Todd R. Kulik is a props manager and maker at Carnegie Mellon School of Drama, responsible for sourcing, building, and maintaining stage properties for theatrical productions. With extensive experience in theatrical craftsmanship, upholstery, antique restoration, and props artistry, he brings expertise from major theaters including Hartford Stage and Pittsburgh Public Theater.

name: Tiia Torchia Lager
title: Costume Studio Manager
bio: Tiia Torchia Lager is the Costume Studio Manager at Carnegie Mellon University, bringing 17 years of professional experience from New York City theaters including Manhattan Theatre Club, Playwrights Horizons, and the Public Theatre. She has worked with renowned costume designers and managed costume shops for major theatrical productions across Broadway, Off-Broadway, and regional theater.

name: Shannon Lown
title: Academic Program Manager & Graduate Advisor
bio: Shannon Lown is the Academic Program Manager and Graduate Advisor in the School of Drama, providing advising, administration, and academic support for MFA students and programs. She is dedicated to supporting students through acts of care, accessibility, and institutional leadership.

name: Melissa Lubina
title: Facilities Coordinator
bio: Melissa Lubina is a Facilities Coordinator at the School of Drama and recent graduate of the Stage and Production Management MFA program. She manages classroom and theatrical facilities, including room scheduling, key maintenance, and work order processing.

name: Jennifer Marks
title: Wardrobe Supervisor
bio: Jennifer Marks is a Wardrobe Supervisor at CMU Drama with extensive experience in theater costume work across Pittsburgh area theaters. She specializes in wardrobe management, costume construction, and theatrical production support.

name: Collin McCormack
title: Media Manager
bio: Collin McCormack is a Media Manager at CMU Drama with over two decades of experience in video and live event production across corporate, educational, and freelance sectors. He guides students in video production and live event technical work while mentoring them for careers in the entertainment industry.

name: Shannon Musgrave
title: Director of Communications & Special Projects
bio: Shannon Musgrave oversees internal and external communications, marketing, and special projects for CMU's School of Drama, including the Tony Awards partnership and the ColLABo production incubator. With over 15 years of experience in artistic and management roles across major theater institutions, she brings expertise in arts administration, artistic development, and theatrical production.

name: Ross Nugent
title: Facilities and Operations Manager
bio: Ross Nugent is an experienced media educator and artist with over 20 years in film education and exhibition, specializing in experimental documentary and expanded cinema works. His artistic practice and curatorial involvement spans film festivals, institutional venues, and nonprofit cinema organizations.

name: David J. Randolph
title: Senior Facilities Manager
bio: David J. Randolph is the Senior Facilities Manager at Carnegie Mellon's School of Drama, overseeing administrative and technical production operations since joining in 1998. With extensive experience in theatre production, scene design, and facilities management across multiple institutions, he has been instrumental in managing the school's technical infrastructure and supporting its productions.

name: Sayantee Sahoo
title: Production Manager
bio: Sayantee Sahoo is an experienced production manager and technical director with expertise in theatre production and technical design, having worked across major institutions including the 52nd St. Project in NYC and BRIC. She brings international experience from India and the US, specializing in new play production, technical direction, and creative direction for theatre organizations.

name: Marlene Speranza
title: Tailor/Costumer
bio: Marlene is a tailor and costumer who has worked professionally in theater and film since the 1980s, serving as a staff member of the CMU Costume Studio since 1989. Her expertise spans costume fittings, builds, alterations, and assistance to costume designers across regional theater, opera, film, and television productions.

name: Louis Stein
title: Network Administrator
bio: Louis is an Information Technologies Specialist with more than 35 years of industry experience who joined CMU Drama in 2003. His background includes computer and network support at major institutions, and he serves as the in-house photographer for the School of Drama.

name: Maria Stoy
title: Box Office Manager, Associate Director, PreCollege Program

name: Aimee Thomas
title: Audio Visual Supervisor
bio: Aimee Thomas is an AV Supervisor at CMU Drama with 12+ years of broadcast and video production experience working with major clients including NBC, CBS, and Discovery Channel. She oversees audio and video equipment operations, manages the documentation team for archival recordings, and brings expertise in camera operation, stage management, and graphic design.

name: Kristin Ward
title: Senior Arts Technician, Prop Shop
bio: Kristin Ward is a Senior Arts Technician in CMU's Prop Shop who has been collaborating with props managers and student designers since 2004, guiding students through intricate prop-making processes. Beyond her technical expertise, she is an accomplished storyteller and performer who creates original works rooted in folklore, earning recognition including a Project Stream Grant from the PA Council on the Arts and appearances on PBS Kids.

name: Sean West
title: Theater Supervisor/Instructor
bio: Sean West is an experienced theater technical professional who serves as Pittsburgh Opera's head carpenter and de facto technical director. He is also president of Pittsburgh's IATSE #3 union and teaches in CMU's School of Drama.

name: Barbara Anderson
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Barbara Anderson was a Professor Emeritus who taught costume design, construction, and history, and co-authored the textbook 'Costume Design.' Her extensive career spanned theatrical productions, feature films, television, and historical costume creation for institutions including the Heinz Regional Historic Center.

name: Natalie Baker-Shirer
title: Associate Professor Emeritus
bio: Natalie Baker-Shirer is an internationally recognized authority on American Standard Speech who developed the online AMERICAN ENGLISH SPEECH course, which has served over 20,000 students worldwide. She has taught speech, phonetics, dialects, and voice-over acting at Carnegie Mellon since 1993, while also serving as a dialect coach for numerous theatrical productions and films.

name: James Caton
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Dance, Emeritus
bio: James Caton is an accomplished choreographer and dance educator who taught at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Drama for over three decades, retiring in 2025. His career spans professional ballet performance, choreography for theatrical productions, and innovative artistic projects such as the Moon Ballet, which has been exhibited internationally.

name: Judith Conte
title: Teaching Professor of Dance, Emeritus
bio: Ms. Conte was a member of the dance faculty at the School of Drama for 47 years, providing choreography and staging for more than 50 productions and creating Dance/Light, a major cross-option Drama project. Her work spans theatrical choreography, film and television, and directing, with credits including Pittsburgh Public Theater productions and collaborations across design, production, and management disciplines.

name: Peter Cooke
title: University Professor Emeritus
bio: Peter Cooke is an internationally recognized performing arts educationalist and practitioner who has designed over 150 productions across drama, opera, dance, and large-scale events, and served as Head of Carnegie Mellon's School of Drama for 12 years. His research focuses on theatre design training pedagogy and the history of theatrical design, with particular expertise in international drama school practices and the development of design education curricula.

name: Janet Madelle Feindel
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Janet Madelle Feindel is a Professor Emeritus of Voice and Alexander Technique at CMU Drama who has taught since 1996 and worked extensively in theater, film, and television coaching actors in voice and movement. She is an internationally recognized voice and Alexander Technique expert who has published a book, articles, and presented at conferences worldwide on vocal training and performer development.

name: Jed Allen Harris
title: Associate Teaching Professor Emeritus
bio: Jed Allen Harris is an accomplished theater director with over 40 years of experience directing critically acclaimed productions in drama and musical theater across Pittsburgh, the United States, and internationally. His work spans experimental theater, classical drama, and innovative adaptations, with significant contributions to CMU's Main Stage and a long tenure at the City Theatre.

name: Gary Kline
title: Teaching Professor Emeritus
bio: Gary Kline is a nationally recognized voice teacher and musical theater educator who has taught at Carnegie Mellon's School of Drama since 1989, creating innovative courses in singing for actors and cabaret performance. He is known for developing the CMU/CLO New Works Partnership and mentoring numerous Broadway performers and Tony Award winners through his teaching and directorial work.

name: Gregory Lehane
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Gregory Lehane is a distinguished director with extensive experience in theatre, opera, and television, having directed numerous productions across major networks and regional theaters worldwide. At Carnegie Mellon University, he served as a Professor of Drama and Music for two decades, coordinating undergraduate and graduate directing programs and serving as Associate Head of the School of Drama.

name: Cindy Limauro
title: Emeritus University Professor of Lighting Design
bio: Cindy Limauro is an internationally recognized lighting designer specializing in theater, opera, dance, and architectural lighting projects, with distinguished contributions to design education and artistic creativity. Her work has been featured in major productions and architectural installations, and she is a Fellow of USITT and member of professional organizations including USA and IALD.

name: Barbara MacKenzie-Wood
title: University Professor Emeritus
bio: Barbara MacKenzie-Wood is an emeritus professor at Carnegie Mellon's School of Drama with a distinguished career spanning professional acting, directing, and theater education. She is recognized as a master teacher in the Meisner Technique and has developed innovative pedagogical approaches documented in her Game Guide and featured in Acting Teachers of America.

name: Dan Martin
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Dan Martin is an arts management scholar and former Dean of the College of Fine Arts who founded the Center for Arts Management and Technology (CAMT) to research the implementation of computer and information technology in arts administration. His work has focused on strategic planning, nonprofit governance, and the development of innovative management education programs for cultural and screen-based creative industries.

name: Anne Mundell
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Anne Mundell is a Professor Emeritus of Scene Design who taught courses in scene design, digital tools, and design thesis at CMU Drama. She is known for her work in theatrical scene design, her leadership of the Growing Theater Outreach program, and her collaborative contributions to The Roboceptionist project, an internationally recognized initiative exploring human-robot interaction through personality-driven design.

name: Ingrid Sonnichsen
title: Associate Professor Emeritus
bio: An accomplished actress with over fifty years of professional experience on Broadway, Off-Broadway, and regional stages, Ms. Sonnichsen has been teaching acting at Carnegie Mellon since 1995. Her expertise spans performance, audition technique, and theater direction, with a focus on training the next generation of theater professionals.

name: Don Wadsworth
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Don Wadsworth taught voice and speech techniques at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Drama for over twenty years, directing main stage productions and leading the Acting and Music Theater option. He has coached voice and dialect work for Broadway productions, feature films, television, and regional theater, working with Oscar-nominated actors and CMU alumni.

name: Eric Anderson
title: Professor
bio: Eric Anderson is a tenured professor with over 40 years of professional experience in industrial design and product innovation. He is a co-founder of the Integrated Innovation Institute and has dedicated more than 25 years to Carnegie Mellon, where he teaches undergraduate and graduate courses and leads the Master of Integrated Innovation for Products and Services program.

name: Mark Baskinger
title: Professor
bio: Mark Baskinger is a Professor and Chair of Product Design whose research focuses on tangible interaction design, deep time artifacts, material culture, and visual thinking methodologies. His work bridges design practice with technology and has been exhibited internationally while contributing to foundational conversations about design in tech industries.

name: Jonathan Chapman
title: Professor
bio: Jonathan Chapman is Professor and Director of Doctoral Studies at Carnegie Mellon's School of Design, leading the PhD in Transition Design program focused on designing for positive global change. His research advocates for an 'experience heavy, materials light' design sensibility that increases product longevity and quality while advancing sustainable futures.

name: Wayne Chung
title: Professor
bio: Wayne Chung is a Professor and Director of Undergraduate Studies whose research spans medical systems, robot design, and consumer products, utilizing design research methods to understand people in context. His work emphasizes how material, aesthetics, fit, and user experience can only be effectively designed once the problem is appropriately framed relative to human, artifacts, and spatial context.

name: Dina El-Zanfaly
title: Associate Professor
bio: Dina El-Zanfaly is a computational design and interaction researcher who directs the hyperSENSE: Embodied Computations Lab, investigating new roles of computational design and physicality in embodied sense-making, human perception, cognition, and experience. Her work focuses on creating interactions through computation and embodiment to enable people to shape experiences of products, social environments, and interconnected technologies while exploring how intelligent machines and humans can learn from and collaborate with each other.

name: Derek Ham
title: Director
bio: Derek Ham is the Director of Carnegie Mellon University's Entertainment Technology Center, leading innovative research at the intersection of technology, design, and storytelling. His work focuses on virtual reality, augmented reality, and immersive media experiences that advance interactive entertainment and social impact.

name: Bruce Hanington
title: Professor
bio: Bruce Hanington is a Professor of Design whose research focuses on methods and practices for human-centered design, with emphasis on design ethnography, participatory design, and the meaning of form in context. His work spans academic research, professional education, and industry collaboration with organizations like GE and Johnson & Johnson to develop innovative, user-focused design solutions.

name: Sunki Hong
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Sunki Hong is an Assistant Professor in the School of Design at Carnegie Mellon University specializing in product and furniture design. Her work explores the interplay between culture, nature, and philosophy, creating designs characterized by balanced aesthetics and refined, understated forms.

name: Kristin Hughes
title: Professor
bio: Kristin Hughes is a professor whose interdisciplinary research integrates design thinking, creativity, and communication design to foster meaningful change through community-engaged and participatory design approaches. Her work specializes in translational design for equitable healthcare delivery, merging design thinking with medicine to address systemic barriers and empower individuals.

name: Terry Irwin
title: Professor
bio: Terry Irwin is a Professor and Director of the Transition Design Institute at Carnegie Mellon University, and a co-originator of Transition Design, a transdisciplinary approach for addressing complex societal and environmental challenges. With over 50 years of design practice experience and faculty positions across multiple institutions, she has led efforts to integrate design for society and the environment throughout university curricula.

name: Haeyoung Kim
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Haeyoung Kim is an Assistant Professor exploring the connections between objects, culture, materials, and technology to design human-centric physical and digital interactions. Her research focuses on creating interactive learning installations that foster social engagement through emerging technologies, and redefining material paradigms to develop novel design frameworks for responsive wearables and products.

name: Daphne Peters
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Daphne Peters is an Associate Teaching Professor and Chair of the Environments track who focuses on digital and physical environments as well as service design. She teaches students 3D form-building and interaction design while helping them develop graphic and strategic solutions to complex design problems.

name: Ana Maria Pinto da Silva
title: Head of School
bio: Ana Maria Pinto da Silva is an accomplished designer, technologist, and educator committed to advancing the role of designers, engineers, and researchers in developing future-defining technology innovation centered on equity and inclusion. She brings strategic leadership experience in design education, curriculum development, and fostering community engagement through design practice.

name: Stacie Rohrbach
title: Professor
bio: Stacie Rohrbach is a Professor and Director of Graduate Studies who investigates the design of learning experiences as complex systems, translating abstract information into concrete, experiential forms to foster enjoyment and aid understanding. Her research applies innovative learning theories to contemporary challenges across formal and informal learning contexts, with recent work focused on eating disorders, financial wellbeing, chronic illness management, and educational assessment tools.

name: Daniel Rosenberg Muñoz
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Daniel Rosenberg Muñoz is an Assistant Professor whose work advances face-to-face interaction through design processes and technologies that enhance mindful awareness and engagement in physical spaces. He directs the Spatiality Lab, which investigates how spatial interactions can enrich human experiences of presence in co-located environments by bringing interaction back into the physical world.

name: Peter Scupelli
title: Associate Professor
bio: Peter Scupelli is an Associate Professor of Design and director of the Learning Environments Lab, focusing on learning environments and design futures with expertise in aligning short-term design action with long-term vision goals. His research integrates architecture, interaction design, and human-computer interaction to embed values into design processes and advance transition design practice.

name: Stephen Stadelmeier
title: Associate Professor
bio: Stephen Stadelmeier is an Associate Professor in the School of Design who has served the institution since 1977, teaching studio courses across undergraduate curriculum in product and environmental design tracks. His research focuses on the relationship between people's beliefs and the choices they make, with a particular interest in how individuals relate to the artifacts they create and use.

name: Andrew Twigg
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Andrew is an Associate Teaching Professor at CMU School of Design teaching interaction design, prototyping, and design systems, while pursuing a PhD in Transition Design focused on improving collaboration and care through design. His research develops frameworks for team performance and composition to enhance collaboration outcomes and mutual support among collaborators.

name: Dylan Vitone
title: Professor
bio: Dylan Vitone is a Professor in the School of Design who teaches courses in digital photographic imaging and communication design, with work exhibited in major museums worldwide. His photographic practice centers on geographical locations as a means to explore broader social and cultural phenomena.

name: Brett Yasko
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Brett Yasko is an Associate Teaching Professor of Communications who teaches studio and seminar courses while maintaining a one-person design studio working with artists, curators, and educators on publication, exhibition, and identity projects. His work has been recognized internationally and included in major museum collections, and he is committed to celebrating uncelebrated artists and helping communities through participatory design initiatives.

name: Matthew Zywica
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Matthew Zywica is an Associate Teaching Professor who teaches courses in product design, drawing, visualization, form meaning, and creative form making. His work explores form language and design pedagogy, with a focus on K-12 design education and craft development.

name: Vicki Crowley
title: Special Faculty
bio: Vicki Crowley is a designer with over 30 years of experience in print, environmental, and information design, particularly for cultural and nonprofit institutions. She teaches at both undergraduate and graduate levels while maintaining an independent consulting practice serving academic, cultural, and community organizations in Pittsburgh.

name: Gideon Kossoff
title: Special Faculty
bio: Gideon is an Associate Director of the Transition Design Institute who teaches and conducts research in transition design and ecoliteracy, focusing on the convergence between philosophical and scientific holism and ecophilosophical traditions. He integrates contemporary developments in complexity theory, whole systems economics, and social psychology to inform the transition to alternative social, political and economic systems.

name: Tricia Douglas
title: Teaching Fellow
bio: Tricia Douglas is a designer and educator who bridges design theory with educational practice, focusing on creating equitable and liberatory learning experiences. Her work examines how design can address systemic inequities in K-12 education and create pathways for historically underrepresented candidates in design and professional fields.

name: Kyla Fullenwider
title: PhD Researcher and Teaching Fellow
bio: Kyla Fullenwider is a design researcher and educator focused on applying human-centered design and innovation practices to government and public sector challenges. Her work spans policy innovation, organizational transformation, and design leadership, with extensive experience modernizing federal programs and building design capacity within institutions.

name: Luis Garcia
title: Teaching Fellow
bio: Luis Garcia is an Ecuadorian designer focused on exploring the role of design and designers in transdisciplinary processes to tackle complex, wicked problems, fostering critical consciousness in non-expert designers to strengthen collaboration across diverse contexts. His work spans sustainable urban mobility, public spaces, healthcare, and community engagement, driven by a passion for challenging dominant perspectives and amplifying people's capability to positively transform their reality.

name: William Martin
title: PhD Researcher and Teaching Fellow
bio: Will Martin is a PhD Researcher and Teaching Fellow who designs transdisciplinary strategies to empower communities to build inclusive, equitable, and resilient futures. His work spans web applications, geospatial mapping, architectural design, data visualization, and policy design across physical and digital environments.

name: Alisha Saxena
title: Teaching Fellow
bio: Alisha Saxena is a design researcher specializing in the intersection of low-income sectors and environmental justice, currently pursuing a PhD at Carnegie Mellon's School of Design. Her work integrates interdisciplinary methodologies and sustainable practices to drive impactful solutions for vulnerable communities affected by environmental injustices.

name: John Choi
title: Adjunct of Practice
bio: John Choi is a seasoned interactive digital media artist specializing in AR/VR/MR/XR, robotics, IoT, and real-time game development with over 10 years of experience transforming concepts into functional prototypes. He collaborates extensively across CMU departments and internationally, creating conversational AI virtual avatars while serving as the School of Design's resident Unity expert and maintaining the Spatial Experience Lab.

name: Ashley Deal
title: Special Faculty
bio: Ashley Deal is a designer with 20 years of experience in education, healthcare, and design research who specializes in creating compelling stories from complex information and transforming confusing processes into seamless interactions. As a researcher, she captures lived experiences of participants, gathers feedback through prototyped experiences, and distills qualitative data into actionable insights.

name: Rachel Delphia
title: Curator
bio: Rachel Delphia is the Alan G. and Jane A. Lehman Curator at Carnegie Museum of Art, specializing in modern and contemporary design and craft. Her research focuses on designers and makers of the 20th and 21st centuries, exploring the intersections of design, craft, art, and design's relationship to humanism and the Anthropocene.

name: Jay Fanelli
bio: Jay Fanelli is a multi-disciplinary designer and founder with extensive experience in design management, most notably as Duolingo's first design manager where he built and led a major design department. He has founded multiple design-focused companies including Cotton Bureau and Full Stop, and brings both startup and established company perspectives to design practice.

name: Josh Horowitz
title: Adjunct of Practice
bio: Josh Horowitz is an award-winning creative director and founder of Fake Love, a pioneering experience design agency that bridges art and technology to create immersive brand experiences. His work spans cutting-edge creative campaigns for major corporations, with recognition including Cannes Lions Grand Prix and One Show awards.

name: Arthi Krishnaswami
title: Adjunct of Practice
bio: Arthi Krishnaswami is a CMU Design graduate who teaches Design for Social Innovation and works at the intersection of public policy, design, and technology with public and private sector institutions. She founded RyeCatcher, a web-based application supporting student success, and the Community Success Institute to improve educational outcomes for underserved communities.

name: Raelynn O'Leary
title: Special Faculty
bio: Raelynn O'Leary is a practitioner and educator specializing in information design, data visualization, and interaction design who co-founded the Pittsburgh design firm Dezudio. Her work focuses on making meaning visible and accessible through designing communications, tools, and services that inform decision-making across domains including education equity and reproductive health.

name: Paul Pangaro
title: Adjunct of Practice | Visiting Scholar
department: School of Design & School of Architecture
bio: Paul Pangaro's career spans startups, consulting, research, and education, exploring the role of conversation in expanding human agency in human-human and human-machine interactions. His work integrates cybernetics, systems theory, conversation theory, design, and artificial intelligence to address complex systems-level challenges in technology, society, and planetary health.

name: Josiah Stadelmeier
title: 3D Lab Manager
bio: Josiah Stadelmeier manages the 3D Lab within the School of Design, overseeing methods, tools, materials, facilities, and training for physical prototyping and model creation. He brings extensive experience from roles as a service shop manager, repair technician, and exhibit fabricator.

name: Daragh Byrne
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Daragh Byrne is an Associate Teaching Professor at Carnegie Mellon Architecture who explores the design of experiential media systems through process-oriented methods and human-centered exploration of emerging technologies, with a focus on Internet of Things and tangible interaction design. He manages the NSF-funded XSEAD project and leads MakeSchools, an effort to catalog making in higher education.

name: Jonathan Cagan
title: George Tallman and Florence Barrett Ladd Professor in Engineering
bio: Jonathan Cagan researches engineering design automation and methods, merging AI, machine learning, and optimization with cognitive science problem solving. His work focuses on computational modeling of designer processes, biomechanical system design, and user-centered product development practices.

name: Stefan Gruber
title: Associate Professor
bio: Stefan Gruber is a registered architect and Associate Professor who investigates spatial practices and political dimensions of cities through the negotiation of top-down planning and bottom-up urban transformations. His work explores emerging spaces and communities performing beyond private/public dichotomies, with particular focus on ownership, commoning practices, and shared economies.

name: Christina Harrington
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Christina Harrington is an Assistant Professor of Human-Computer Interaction who combines industrial design, interactive systems, and human factors psychology to focus on universal, accessible, and inclusive design. Her research centers historically excluded communities such as Black and LatinX populations in maintaining health, wellness, and autonomy while defining technological futures.

name: Suguru Ishizaki
title: Professor
bio: Suguru Ishizaki is a scholar and designer specializing in communication and user experience design with over 30 years of experience in academia and industry. His current research focuses on technology-enhanced learning for writing and computer-assisted rhetorical analysis, drawing from expertise in visual communication, information visualization, and enterprise application design.

name: David Kaufer
title: Mellon Distinguished Professor Emeritus
bio: David Kaufer is a Mellon Distinguished Professor Emeritus who develops technological solutions for textual analysis and writing instruction. He works with the writing and communication team to advance research in rhetoric and computational communication.

name: Cameron Tonkinwise
title: Professor
bio: Cameron is a Professor of Design Studies focused on sustainable design, service design, and transition design for structural change toward sustainable futures. His research integrates continental philosophy, material cultural studies, and sociologies of technology to advance social sustainability through design practice.

name: Joe Ballay
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Joe Ballay is an Emeritus Professor of Design who has been instrumental in shaping CMU's design curriculum and founding MAYA Design, Inc., an interdisciplinary design consultancy applying design theory to complex contemporary problems. His work emphasizes the art-science axis, integrating creative problem-solving from arts, science, technology, and humanities to explore how people perceive, represent, and interact with form in everyday products and experiences.

name: Dan Boyarski
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Dan Boyarski is Professor Emeritus in the School of Design at Carnegie Mellon University who taught courses in typography, information and interaction design, and time-based communication for 35 years. His research focused on kinetic typography and how type, image, sound, and movement combine for effective communication, with applications in interface design and dynamic information systems.

name: Charlee Brodsky
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Charlee Brodsky is a fine art documentary photographer whose work explores social issues, mental health, labor history, and community transformation through photographic narratives and collaborative book projects. Her practice combines portraiture and documentary photography with interdisciplinary collaboration, addressing themes of economic change, health, and social justice in communities like Homestead, Pennsylvania.

name: Mark Mentzer
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Mark Mentzer is a Professor Emeritus in the School of Design who specializes in foundation design courses including design drawing and color communication. He is an active artist with extensive exhibition history and has created over 6,000 catalogued drawings throughout his career.

name: Thomas Merriman
title: Teaching Professor Emeritus
bio: Tom Merriman teaches furniture design and advanced digital/production prototyping, focusing on the process of defining and refining concepts while maintaining design intent through material and manufacturing constraints. His research includes long-term collaborations with New York University on motor skill development in infants and numerous furniture commissions.

name: Robert Swinehart
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Professor Emeritus Bob Swinehart taught at Carnegie Mellon's School of Design from 1974 to 2010, specializing in graphic and communication design. He was a prominent figure in design education and professional organizations, serving as chair of multiple design programs and holding leadership positions in the International Institute of Information Design and the Graphic Design Education Association.

name: Jared Abraham
title: Special Faculty in Architectural Design
bio: Jared Abraham is an educator and architectural designer who coordinates first-year studios and teaches in Carnegie Mellon's Master of Urban Design program, with research focused on design pedagogy and housing typologies. His work engages material practice and adaptive reuse, with current projects exploring hardwood CLT frameworks for workforce housing in Pennsylvania.

name: Karen Abrams
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Karen Abrams is an urban planner and strategist who works at the intersection of racial, spatial, and climate justice, collaborating with residents and municipalities to dismantle interconnected policies that perpetuate structural oppression. She is passionate about co-designing tools that engage communities and promote self-determination while addressing environmental hazards and economic inequality in cities.

name: Vicky Achnani
title: Special Faculty
bio: Vicky Achnani is an educator and architectural designer specializing in design-build projects with unconventional and sustainable materials, bringing expertise from licensed practice in India and a Master's from Yale. His work emphasizes material detailing, on-site production involvement, and serving underserved communities through thoughtfully designed architecture tailored to site conditions.

name: Burcu Akinci
title: Professor & Dean of the College of Engineering
bio: Burcu Akinci is Professor and Dean of the College of Engineering at Carnegie Mellon University whose research focuses on modeling information-rich histories of buildings and infrastructure systems to streamline construction and operations. Her work investigates the integration of building information models with data capture technologies like 3D imaging and embedded sensors to create digital twins and support predictive operations and management.

name: Niloufar Alenjery
title: Special Faculty
bio: Niloufar Alenjery is a registered architect and educator whose work explores architecture as a language that inscribes memory, time, and cultural narratives, grounded in phenomenology and poetic design approaches. Her practice extends across design, installation art, and critical pedagogy, examining how the built environment holds human experience while resisting technological mediation and cultural homogenization.

name: Sarosh B. Anklesaria
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Sarosh Anklesaria is an architect and educator interested in synthesizing architecture's formal and tectonic capacities with questions of socio-ecological pertinence, particularly examining agency across scales and geographies. His research investigates entanglements between justice, ecology, worldmaking, and architecture, with recent focus on the afterlives of modernism in South Asia and architectures of just transitions in post-industrial contexts.

name: Nina Marie Barbuto
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Nina Barbuto is an artist and architect whose practice spans multiple media including architecture, film, sound, and installation, often exploring the transformation of noise into systems and elevating vernacular elements. She founded Assemble, a community space for arts and technology, and teaches spatial concepts to students across disciplines.

name: Joshua D. Bard
title: Associate Professor
bio: Joshua Bard is an architectural educator conducting applied research at the intersection of construction culture and robotic technology, interrogating traditional binaries in design culture through augmented construction and design systems. He collaborates with roboticists, computer scientists, historians, and tradespeople to explore human-machine collaboration in the high-skill domain of building trades.

name: William J. Bates
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: William Bates is an adjunct faculty member with extensive experience in international development, corporate real estate, and construction management, having held senior positions at major corporations including PNC Bank and Marconi Communications. He is a former AIA president and founding president of the Pittsburgh Chapter of the National Organization of Minority Architects, bringing decades of professional practice and community leadership to his teaching.

name: Fred Betz
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Fred Betz is a founding principal of NeuMod Labs, a technology startup developing building design technology focused on data warehousing and automated insights generation. His research and consulting expertise spans building performance diagnostics, energy efficiency, decarbonization, and code compliance in the AEC industry.

name: Heather Bizon
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Heather Bizon is an artist and architect whose work explores individual perception of space and public/private domains through new media, technology, sculpture, architecture, and film. She teaches design studios and seminars with a focus on thesis development and serves as Director of Pre-College Architecture, an intensive summer program for high school students.

name: Liza C. Boffi
title: Special Faculty
bio: Liza Boffi teaches undergraduate and graduate design studios with a pedagogical approach emphasizing observation, narrative, and conceptual clarity through experiential storytelling. She leads L. Boffi Studios, a multidisciplinary concept design practice spanning architecture, UX/UI design, installations, and visual research, which informs her teaching and studio critique.

name: Daniel Cardoso Llach
title: Associate Professor
bio: Daniel Cardoso Llach is an Associate Professor at Carnegie Mellon Architecture who chairs the graduate Computational Design program and directs CodeLab. His research critically explores the nexus of design and computation through historical scholarship, applied research projects, and pedagogical frameworks that cultivate critical technical practitioners.

name: Gloria Chang
title: Special Faculty
bio: Gloria Chang is a cross-disciplinary architect and designer working at the intersection of design, fabrication, and speculative spatiality, with a focus on humanitarian response and material innovation. Her research examines spatial patterns through built and unbuilt forms, systems recharacterization, and imagined agricultural futures through applied technology.

name: Xin Chen
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Xin Chen is an adjunct faculty member at Carnegie Mellon Architecture who teaches the history of Asian architecture from prehistoric times through the 20th century. Their work emphasizes interdisciplinary and cross-cultural perspectives in architectural design across China, Japan, Korea, India, and Southeast Asia.

name: Jimmy Wei-Chun Cheng
title: Special Faculty
bio: Jimmy Wei-Chun Cheng is a designer, researcher and educator who investigates how technique, medium and technology transform the architectural design process, with particular emphasis on digital representation in the context of artificial intelligence. His work analyzes how AI affects the meaning of language, imagery and form through frameworks of semiotics, media theory and simulation.

name: Lori Claus
title: Special Faculty
bio: Lori Claus is the Director for Career Development Opportunity and Special Faculty at Carnegie Mellon Architecture, with extensive experience in construction project management and professional development education. She is committed to preparing students and alumni for internships and employment through instruction in resume writing, networking, interviewing techniques, and broader professional practice skills.

name: Erica Cochran Hameen
title: Associate Professor
bio: Erica Cochran Hameen is an architectural designer and Associate Professor whose research investigates the impact of building and environmental characteristics on occupant health and performance, with a focus on energy efficiency and sustainable design. She serves as Co-Director of the Center for Building Performance & Diagnostics and leads research on environmentally responsible architectural solutions and indoor environmental quality.

name: Donald Coffelt
title: Adjunct Professor
bio: Donald Coffelt is the Associate Vice President for Facilities Management and Campus Services at Carnegie Mellon University, leading a 400-member team managing infrastructure and operations for the 150-acre Pittsburgh campus. His expertise spans facility services, infrastructure management, utility operations, and university-wide sustainability coordination.

name: Doug Cooper
title: Andrew Mellon Professor of Architecture
bio: Doug Cooper is an artist and muralist whose work combines story, history, and memory into panoramic images of cities, typically developed through collaboration with local residents. He is the Andrew Mellon Professor of Architecture at Carnegie Mellon, where he teaches drawing and architectural design using classical techniques informed by drawing pedagogy.

name: Dana Čupková
title: Professor
bio: Dana Čupková is a Professor and Track Chair for the Master in Regenerative and Sustainable Design program who directs EPIPHYTE Lab, a design research collaborative. Her work investigates the relationship between ecology and technology through evidence-based design solutions emphasizing circularity, thermodynamics, and bio-based processes for remediation and decarbonization.

name: Gerard Damiani
title: Associate Professor
bio: Gerard Damiani is an Associate Professor at Carnegie Mellon Architecture and founder of studio d'ARC architects in Pittsburgh. His work integrates architectural practice with education, focusing on design studios, materials technology, and architectural detailing, with recognition from national and international architectural publications and awards.

name: Stefani Danes
title: Professor of Practice
bio: Stefani Danes is a Professor of Practice at Carnegie Mellon Architecture with over 40 years of combined experience in architecture, urban design, and teaching. Her work focuses on sustainable housing, urban revitalization, and specialized residential environments, particularly for aging populations and underserved communities.

name: Catherine Armbruster
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Catherine Armbruster investigates how bacteria evolve in biofilms within the built environment and transition to establish chronic infections in hosts, with a focus on opportunistic pathogens like Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Her lab uses bacterial genetics, host-pathogen model systems, and evolutionary genomics to identify public health and therapeutic strategies to prevent and treat infections from environmental sources.

name: Alison L. Barth
title: Maxwell H. and Gloria C. Connan Professor in the Life Sciences
bio: Alison Barth investigates how experience assembles and alters neural circuits in the cerebral cortex, with a focus on somatosensory processing in mice and the mechanisms of synaptic plasticity underlying perception and learning. Her lab employs electrophysiology, electron microscopy, and computational modeling to understand how cortical networks are constructed, optimized, and modified during development, learning, and disease.

name: Drew Bridges
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Drew Bridges studies how bacteria make developmental decisions based on extracellular sensory information, focusing on the formation and disassembly of multicellular bacterial communities called biofilms. His lab combines imaging approaches with genetics, biochemistry, and biophysics to discover the molecular mechanisms controlling biofilm dispersal and bacterial lifestyle changes across multiple scales.

name: En Cai
title: Assistant Professor
bio: En Cai develops advanced super-resolution imaging techniques to visualize biological structures and processes at the nanometer scale, particularly focusing on synaptic structures and immune cell function. Her work employs quantum dots and fluorescence microscopy to track receptor dynamics and visualize the transfer of tumor antigens among dendritic cells.

name: Dannie Durand
title: Associate Professor
bio: Dannie Durand uses computational approaches to study the role of gene duplication in the acquisition of new gene function and the evolution of vertebrate genomes. Her research focuses on understanding how large-scale gene duplications shaped early vertebrate evolution and contributed to innovations such as skeletal structure, limbs, and a complex central nervous system.

name: Charles A. Ettensohn
title: Professor
bio: Charles Ettensohn studies how genomic DNA sequences are translated into three-dimensional multicellular form during embryogenesis using the sea urchin as a model organism. His research focuses on early patterning, morphogenesis, and the gene regulatory networks that control development, employing molecular biological, genomics-based, and embryological approaches.

name: Aryn H. Gittis
title: Distinguished Professor of Life Sciences
bio: Aryn Gittis studies how neural circuits in the basal ganglia transform thoughts into actions, with a focus on movement regulation, learning, and motivation. Her laboratory investigates how these circuits are altered by experience and breakdown in movement disorders like Parkinson's disease using electrophysiology, optogenetics, and behavioral studies in mice.

name: Jonathan Henninger
title: Assistant Professor
bio: The Henninger lab explores mechanisms by which RNA regulates cell fate in health and disease, using molecular biology, soft-matter physics, and super-resolution imaging. The research aims to uncover fundamental mechanisms in gene control, develop RNA-based therapies, and understand how RNA defects contribute to human disease.

name: N. Luisa Hiller
title: Associate Professor of Biological Sciences
bio: Her laboratory investigates the genomic diversity and plasticity of commensal pathogen Streptococcus pneumoniae using comparative genomics, bioinformatics, and phylogenetics on clinical bacterial isolates. The research integrates genomic analysis with molecular and cellular biology to understand bacterial strain variation, genetic modification, and mechanisms of host-pathogen communication.

name: Kate Hong
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Kate Hong studies how the brain senses and responds to environmental stimuli by investigating cortical and subcortical circuit interactions using the mouse whisker system. Her research combines behavioral, imaging, and electrophysiological methods to understand sensory-motor transformations and neural recovery mechanisms following cortical injury.

name: Irene Kaplow
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Irene Kaplow studies the transcriptional regulatory mechanisms underlying phenotypic evolution across mammals, focusing on how differences in enhancer activity between species explain evolutionary changes in traits like brain size and longevity. She develops computational frameworks and experimental approaches to associate species differences in tissue-specific enhancer activity with phenotypic evolution, collaborating through the Zoonomia Consortium.

name: Zheng Kuang
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Dr. Kuang studies the molecular basis of interactions between gut microbiota and circadian rhythms in mammalian metabolism and immunity. His research employs genomics, gnotobiotics, and laser capture microscopy to understand transcriptional and epigenetic mechanisms regulating host-microbial interactions, with implications for metabolic and immunological diseases.

name: Tina H. Lee
title: Associate Professor
bio: Dr. Lee's research focuses on understanding the structure and function relationship of the endoplasmic reticulum and how it responds to changes in cell physiology through the study of structuring proteins like atlastin and Yip1A. Her work combines cell-based RNA interference approaches with biochemical assays and fluorescence microscopy to elucidate the mechanisms by which these proteins regulate ER organization and membrane fusion.

name: Adam D. Linstedt
title: Professor
bio: Adam Linstedt is a Professor of Biological Sciences who studies intracellular protein trafficking, Golgi apparatus organization, and membrane dynamics. His research has focused on understanding how cells regulate organelle structure and function, particularly through mechanisms of membrane tethering and protein transport.

name: Brooke M. McCartney
title: Associate Professor
bio: The McCartney lab investigates fundamental cellular mechanisms that drive developmental processes including oogenesis and systemic growth in Drosophila, with a focus on how different cytoskeletal networks interact and coregulate. Research includes studying actin-microtubule crosstalk, dynamic maintenance of actin structures, and the collaboration between Arc1 and gut microbiota in promoting growth.

name: C. Joel McManus
title: Associate Professor
bio: C. Joel McManus investigates how variation in gene expression contributes to phenotypic diversity and disease, with a focus on mechanisms regulating mRNA translation in fungal species. His lab develops high-throughput assay systems to identify RNA regulatory elements and their impact on protein production, particularly studying upstream open reading frames (uORFs) and translational control during fungal pathogen infection.

name: Jonathan S. Minden
title: Professor
bio: Jonathan Minden's lab studies how cells and organisms adapt to environmental changes and mutations, with a focus on pattern formation and repair in Drosophila embryos. His research employs advanced proteomics methods and imaging techniques to understand cellular adaptation mechanisms across diverse organisms.

name: Elizabeth Ransey
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Elizabeth Ransey studies gap junctions, which are essential intercellular pores formed by connexin proteins that enable direct communication and metabolic exchange between cells. Her research combines cellular assays, biochemistry, imaging, and electrophysiology to understand how specific connexin isoforms interact to form functional channels and regulate intercellular signaling in health and disease.

name: Gordon S. Rule
title: Professor
bio: Gordon Rule's research focuses on understanding inter-molecular interactions in biological systems using molecular biology, computational biology, NMR spectroscopy, and x-ray crystallography. His work spans enzyme-substrate interactions, protein-DNA interactions, and the development of inhibitors for thymidylate kinases with potential antimicrobial and antimalarial applications.

name: Russell S. Schwartz
title: Professor and Head, Computational Biology Department
bio: Russell Schwartz develops algorithms, machine learning, and numerical methods for understanding complex biological systems, with particular focus on somatic evolution, cancer genomics, and tumor phylogenetics. He is also active in computational biology education, working to establish standards and competencies for computational training in the life sciences.

name: Russell Schwartz
title: Professor and Head
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Dr. Schwartz works broadly on models and simulations of biological systems, with primary focus on computational cancer biology and algorithmic development related to clonal evolution in cancers and tumor heterogeneity. His lab also pursues work in stochastic simulation with emphasis on models and inference methods for complex self-assembly dynamics.

name: John L. Woolford, Jr.
title: Professor
bio: John Woolford studies the pathway of assembly of ribosomes in eukaryotes using yeast as a model organism, employing genomics, proteomics, molecular biology, and classical genetic approaches. His research focuses on identifying non-ribosomal proteins necessary for ribosome biogenesis and understanding how ribosome assembly is coordinated with cell growth and proliferation.

name: Eric Yttri
title: Associate Professor
bio: Eric Yttri studies how neural circuits in the brain lead to action selection decisions, particularly in motor, reward, and cognitive systems. His lab uses advanced electrophysiological recordings, behavioral paradigms, and computational methods to understand the neural mechanisms underlying movement and behavior, with applications to treating disorders like Parkinson's disease.

name: Huaiying Zhang
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Huaiying Zhang studies how liquid-liquid phase separation organizes proteins and RNAs into liquid compartments in cells, investigating how this process promotes cellular functions in healthy cells and how its misregulation leads to cancer. Her research combines optogenetic tools, biophysical characterization, and synthetic biology approaches to engineer organelles and develop therapeutic strategies targeting phase transition in cancer cells.

name: Yongxin (Leon) Zhao
title: Associate Professor
bio: Yongxin Zhao leads an interdisciplinary research group developing novel bioengineering and chemical approaches to interrogate biological and pathological processes in cells and tissues. His lab creates innovative imaging and sensing technologies, including expansion microscopy and genetically encoded indicators, to map nanoscale molecular properties and probe dynamics of complex biological systems.

name: DJ Brasier
title: Teaching Professor and Assistant Department Head for Graduate Affairs
bio: DJ Brasier is a neuroscientist studying how astrocytes communicate with neurons and regulate neural activity. He is dedicated to science education and mentoring students in understanding scientific inquiry, critical thinking, and peer-reviewed publication.

name: Maggie Braun
title: Teaching Professor and MCS Senior Associate Dean for Student Success and Strategic Initiatives

name: Amy Burkert
title: Teaching Professor and Senior Vice Provost for Academic Initiatives
bio: Amy Burkert is a biologist whose research focused on the molecular etiology of disease, with particular expertise in occupational asthma developed through multinational collaborative projects. She is now Vice Provost for Education overseeing university-wide education initiatives and has been a pioneer in developing innovative undergraduate learning experiences, research opportunities, and interdisciplinary programs in biological sciences.

name: Jason D'Antonio
title: Associate Teaching Professor and Director, Health Professions Program
bio: Jason D'Antonio is an Associate Teaching Professor who specializes in teaching biological sciences courses ranging from introductory to advanced levels, with particular expertise in human physiology, immunology, and cancer biology. As Director of the Health Professions Program, he advises students pursuing careers in medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, and other health professions while maintaining an active research background in prostate cancer and androgen receptor signaling.

name: Carrie Doonan
title: Teaching Professor and Director, Undergraduate Laboratories
bio: Carrie Doonan is a Teaching Professor and Director of Undergraduate Laboratories in the Department of Biological Sciences at Carnegie Mellon University. She specializes in molecular biology education and has coordinated numerous outreach programs introducing high school and undergraduate students to genetic engineering and bacterial transformation techniques.

name: Emily Drill
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Emily Drill is an Associate Teaching Professor who specializes in teaching upper-level undergraduate laboratory courses covering molecular biology, cell biology, developmental biology, and neuroscience. She is actively engaged in outreach and mentorship, leading research projects for high school students through summer programs at Pennsylvania Governor's School for the Sciences and Summer Academy for Math and Science.

name: Kenneth Hovis
title: Teaching Professor and MCS Associate Dean for Educational Initiatives
bio: Kenneth Hovis oversees the MCS Core Education program, an innovative initiative launched in 2015 that prepares undergraduates to be 21st century scientists. His work focuses on fostering student growth as scholars, professionals, citizens, and people through comprehensive educational initiatives.

name: Annette Vincent
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Annette Vincent is a Teaching Professor in Biological Sciences who focuses on laboratory instruction and research in molecular biology, biochemistry, and phage genomics. Her research includes bacteriophages as biomonitoring tools, metagenomics analysis, and investigating the effects of artificial sweeteners on human kidney health.

name: Stephanie Wong-Noonan
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Stephanie Wong-Noonan is an Associate Teaching Professor in Biological Sciences at Carnegie Mellon University with expertise in biomedical engineering and molecular biology. She teaches courses in modern biology, applied cell and molecular biology, and computational biology.

name: Jeremy Avigad
title: Professor
department: Mathematical Sciences and Department of Philosophy
school: Mellon College of Science and Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Jeremy Avigad is a Professor of Philosophy and Mathematical Sciences and Director of the Charles C. Hoskinson Center for Formal Mathematics. His research focuses on formal verification, mathematical logic, and the intersection of mathematics and computation.

name: Steve Awodey
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Steve Awodey is a Professor of Philosophy specializing in homotopy type theory, univalent foundations, and algebraic set theory. His research bridges mathematical logic, category theory, and topology, with a focus on foundational questions in mathematics and the formal structure of mathematical reasoning.

name: Tom Bohman
title: Professor
bio: Tom Bohman's research focuses on extremal and probabilistic combinatorics, with applications to information theory, statistical physics, and theoretical computer science. His recent work includes Shannon capacities of odd cycles, randomized network algorithms, list-coloring problems, and hypergraph discrepancy.

name: Boris Bukh
title: Professor
bio: Boris Bukh searches for the largest and best combinatorial objects, often encountering problems of algebraic and geometric flavor. His research spans Turán problems, geometric selection theorems, combinatorial number theory, and coding theory.

name: Clinton Conley
title: Associate Professor
bio: Clinton Conley works in descriptive set theory, studying how the ability to define sets confers useful mathematical properties, with a focus on equivalence relations arising from measurable group actions and graphs. His research sits at the intersection of set theory, dynamics, and combinatorics, translating ergodic-theoretic and combinatorial properties into set-theoretic results.

name: James Cummings
title: Professor
bio: James Cummings works in combinatorial set theory, studying combinatorial objects in infinite settings with a particular focus on singular cardinal combinatorics. His research spans forcing, large cardinals, Ramsey theory, PCF theory, and has recently included structure theory of linear orderings, rainbow Ramsey theory, and consistency results in singular cardinal combinatorics.

name: Christopher Eur
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Christopher Eur is an Assistant Professor of Mathematical Sciences specializing in algebraic geometry and combinatorics. His research interests include matroid theory, tropical geometry, and their applications to mathematical structures.

name: Irene Fonseca
title: Kavčić-Moura University Professor of Mathematics
bio: Irene Fonseca's research focuses on applied mathematics at the interface of mathematics, physical sciences, and engineering, with particular emphasis on the mathematical study of shape memory alloys, ferroelectric materials, composites, and phase transitions. Her work also includes mathematical analysis of image processing problems such as segmentation, denoising, and recolorization in computer vision.

name: Florian Frick
title: Associate Professor
bio: Florian Frick uses topological and geometric methods to solve problems that require understanding global information and detecting global obstructions. His research spans geometric topology, combinatorics, fair division problems, and packing inequalities, with applications to convex hulls, hypergraph colorings, and game theory.

name: Alan Frieze
title: University Professor of Mathematical Sciences
bio: Alan Frieze's research focuses on probabilistic combinatorics and its applications to theoretical computer science and operations research, with particular emphasis on the properties of random graphs and thresholds for graph properties. His work includes the development of randomized approximation schemes for computational problems, analysis of algorithm performance on random data, and extensions of random graph theory to hypergraphs and matroids.

name: Rami Grossberg
title: Professor
bio: Rami Grossberg's primary research focuses on model theory, a major field of mathematical logic, with secondary interests in related combinatorial and set-theoretic problems with applications to algebra. He works on developing classification theory for abstract elementary classes and has made significant contributions to Shelah's categoricity conjecture and main gap theorem.

name: Gautam Iyer
title: Professor
bio: Gautam Iyer studies partial differential equations arising in applied mathematics, with early work providing probabilistic representations of Navier-Stokes equations for incompressible fluids. His current interests include global existence questions in fluid dynamics, mixing of passive scalars, anomalous diffusion effects, and the Q-Tensor equations for liquid crystals.

name: David Kinderlehrer
title: Alumni Professor of Mathematical Sciences, Professor of Materials Science and Engineering
bio: David Kinderlehrer is an applied mathematician specializing in partial differential equations and optimal transport theory with applications to materials science and cell biology. His research focuses on understanding the evolution of material microstructure through grain boundaries and developing continuum-scale mathematical models for stochastic biological systems including protein motors and ion transport.

name: Dmitry Kramkov
