She teaches Arabic language and culture courses while serving as coordinator of the Arabic Studies program and exploring themes of social justice, indigeneity, and intercultural dialogue.

name: Khaled Al Masaeed
title: Associate Professor of Applied Linguistics, Second Language Acquisition and Arabic Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Khaled Al Masaeed is an applied linguist who works with graduate and undergraduate students in applied linguistics, second language acquisition and Arabic studies. His research explores how individuals and societies navigate multilingual and multidialectal contexts, with particular attention to the tension between language ideologies and everyday sociolinguistic practices.

name: Uju Anya
title: Associate Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Uju Anya is a scholar of language learning and Black experiences in multilingualism, focusing on how race, gender, sexual and social class identities intersect with language learning. Her research explores the multilingual journeys of African American students and proposes translanguaging and plurilingual practices as tools for effective language pedagogy and social justice.

name: Mathieu Berbiguier
title: Visiting Assistant Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Mathieu Berbiguier is a scholar of Korean studies who explores the dynamics of K-pop fandoms and their role in shaping perceptions of authenticity in Korean popular culture. His research examines how expressions of authenticity, national identity, and language are negotiated within global K-pop fan communities.

name: Stephen Brockmann
title: Professor of German Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Stephen Brockmann specializes in the interconnection between culture and national identity in German studies, with particular expertise in film, theater, and literature from the nineteenth to twenty-first centuries. He is the author of A Critical History of German Film and has served as president of both the German Studies Association and the International Brecht Society.

name: Katharine E. Burns
title: Associate Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Katharine Burns conducts interdisciplinary qualitative research on applied sociolinguistics, examining how power dynamics and language ideologies influence second language learning and learner identity formation. She specializes in bilingualism, multilingualism, heritage languages, and endangered minority languages within the context of L2 acquisition.

name: Stephan Caspar
title: Associate Teaching Professor in Media Creation and Multicultural Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Stephan Caspar is an Associate Teaching Professor with a background in creative media, film production, and digital learning who directs The Kenner Room at Carnegie Mellon University. His research focuses on immersive technologies, digital storytelling, and language learning, while he remains active as an artist specializing in XR creation and film production.

name: Isabelle Chen
title: Assistant Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Isabelle Chen is an Assistant Professor of Global French Studies who teaches French language and Francophone culture courses at all levels. Her research examines French-language novels through translation, analyzing how diasporic authors use translation as a stylistic and thematic device to illustrate migration and interculturality, with current focus on representations of Chinese identities in French literature.

name: Sébastien Dubreil
title: Teaching Professor of Global French Studies, Second Language Acquisition and Technology-Enhanced Learning
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Sébastien Dubreil is a researcher in computer-assisted language learning who designs technology-rich learning environments that engage learners in culturally relevant ways beyond the classroom. His work examines how technological tools like games, multimedia, and generative AI can foster language and culture learning through social pedagogies, linguistic landscapes, and ludic approaches.

name: Kenya C. Dworkin y Méndez
title: Professor of Hispanic Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Kenya C. Dworkin y Méndez is a scholar of Cuban, U.S. Latino, and Latin American Jewish and Sephardic literary and cultural studies. She is Director of the M.A. in Global Communication & Applied Translation Program and is actively engaged in community-based teaching and research.

name: José Estrada
title: Associate Professor of Hispanic Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: José Estrada's research focuses on early-modern Spanish literature, particularly theater, and the transatlantic society resulting from the encounter between Europe and the Americas, with significant emphasis on Juan Ruiz de Alarcón. He studies cultural and linguistic exchange in Colonial Mexico, including Nahuatl translations of Spanish Baroque theater, analyzing how space, place, and performance build identities in emerging global societies.

name: Kiyono Fujinaga-Gordon
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Dr. Fujinaga-Gordon is a researcher and educator specializing in Japanese linguistics, language documentation, and heritage language acquisition, with particular expertise in Ryukyuan languages and linguistic relativity. She is committed to advocating for marginalized communities and fostering inclusion for international students and heritage language learners at Carnegie Mellon University.

name: Giuseppina Gemboni
title: Assistant Teaching Professor of Italian Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Dr. Gemboni is passionate about bringing her knowledge of Italian language, culture, cinema and history into the classroom, helping students move beyond textbooks and stereotypes to discover the multifaceted nature of Italian studies. She contributes to interdisciplinary research and teaching at CMU while serving as coordinator and advisor of the Italian Studies program, aiming to create a dynamic and inclusive learning environment that inspires curiosity and critical thinking.

name: Tatyana Gershkovich
title: Associate Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Tatyana Gershkovich is an associate professor of Russian Studies whose scholarly work investigates 19th and 20th-century Russian prose, particularly the works of Tolstoy and Nabokov. Her research combines literary analysis with digital humanities methods to explore questions of aesthetics, intellectual history, and the cultural legacy of Russian émigré communities.

name: Felipe Gómez
title: Teaching Professor of Hispanic Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Felipe Gómez's research focuses on 20th- and 21st-century Latin American cultural studies, emphasizing countercultural artistic expressions and representations of political violence across literature, film, music, and comics. His current work examines Hispanic/Latino comics and graphic novels within academic and cultural contexts, exploring cultural, racial, and gender issues while contributing significantly to digital humanities through the award-winning Latin American Comics Archive.

name: Sanghee Kang
title: Assistant Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Sanghee Kang is an Assistant Professor of Second Language Acquisition, ESL, and Korean Studies whose research explores the role of generative AI in second language learning, with a focus on AI-generated feedback and collaborative writing. She specializes in task-based language teaching, digital multimodal composition, and has made significant contributions to Korean language curriculum development and teacher training.

name: Gang Liu
title: Teaching Professor of Chinese Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Gang Liu is a Teaching Professor of Chinese Studies who has developed and taught Chinese language courses at all levels, along with content courses on topics including business language, poetry, literature, and modern Chinese society. His research interests focus on classical Chinese poetry, literati miscellanies, Chinese as a Foreign Language pedagogy, and technology-enhanced learning, with publications and editorial work on teaching Chinese in global contexts.

name: Christopher Lowy
title: William S. Dietrich III Assistant Professor of Japanese Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Christopher Lowy is an Assistant Professor of Japanese Studies whose research focuses on the role of written language in Japanese literature and depictions of HIV/AIDS in Japanese literature from the 1980s and 1990s. He teaches courses on contemporary Japanese literature and culture, queer literature, and literary representations of illness and disease.

name: Lama Nassif
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Arabic Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
researchAreas: attention and noticing in language learning
bio: Dr. Nassif's research focuses on noticing and attention in second language development, sociolinguistic development in L2 Arabic acquisition, and the interface between L2 research and pedagogy. Her work appears in peer-reviewed, international journals and she has taught Arabic and linguistics at multiple institutions including Williams College, Middlebury, and UT Austin.

name: Mame-Fatou Niang
title: Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Mame-Fatou Niang is a professor of Global French Studies and founder of the Center for Black European Studies & the Atlantic at Carnegie Mellon University, whose research examines Black geographies, Blackness in France, and the institutionalization of Black Studies. She is an author, filmmaker, and curator who has published widely on contemporary French racism, Afro-French identity, and the politics of commemoration.

name: David Parker
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: David Parker's research explores literary depictions of the individual in late Soviet prose, with a particular focus on its relationship to modernist and socialist realist literature that shaped early 20th-century Russian writing. He advocates for cross-cultural and interdisciplinary exchange as both a subject of study and classroom practice, while examining Russian, Soviet, and Yugoslav cultural history.

name: Candace Skibba
title: Teaching Professor of Hispanic Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Candace Skibba specializes in contemporary Spanish literature and film, focusing on the intersection of literary and film studies with the study of the body, particularly the abnormal body. Her work encompasses disability studies, health humanities, and explores agency and empathy in artistic expression and classroom practices.

name: Therese Tardio
title: Teaching Professor of Hispanic Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Therese Tardio's research incorporates different geographic areas in the Spanish-speaking world, with a focus on contemporary cultural production from El Salvador, Nicaragua, and the U.S.-Mexico Borderlands, shaped by feminist and gender studies, decolonial studies, and visual studies. Her primary commitment as an educator is to challenge students to think critically and analytically about subject matter, themselves, and their relationships to the world.

name: Rémi A. van Compernolle
title: Professor of Applied Linguistics, Second Language Acquisition and Global French Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Rémi A. van Compernolle studies the intersections between cognition, affect and identity in second language development using Vygotskian cultural-historical psychology. His research examines how opportunities for language development are co-constructed between people and how cultural artifacts are internalized to mediate cognition, affect and identity performance, primarily in formal educational contexts.

name: Seth Wiener
title: Associate Professor of Second Language Acquisition and Chinese Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Seth Wiener is an Associate Professor who directs the Language Acquisition, Processing and Pedagogy (LAPP) Lab at Carnegie Mellon University, where graduate and undergraduate students conduct experimental linguistics research. His work focuses on second language acquisition, psycholinguistics, and developing pedagogical innovations for language instructors and learners.

name: Sue-mei Wu
title: Teaching Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Sue-mei Wu is dedicated to creating effective, learner-centered language learning environments that incorporate innovative strategies and technological tools. Her research interests include linguistically informed materials design, technology-enhanced language and culture learning, and the study of Chinese folk performance traditions and popular culture.

name: Yoshihiro Yasuhara
title: Associate Teaching Professor
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Yoshihiro Yasuhara's primary research explores the relationship between aesthetic expression and social realities, grounded in comparative literature with a focus on modern Japanese culture in a transnational context. His work examines Japanese literature and film, the intersection of advertising and poetry in 1980s Japan, and how marginalized voices in Japanese society challenge prevailing artistic norms and expose overlooked social issues.

name: Bonnie L. Youngs
title: Teaching Professor of Global French Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Bonnie Youngs is a teaching professor specializing in applied linguistics, language teaching, and the integration of technology and AI tools in second language instruction. She directs the M.A. in Applied Linguistics & Second Language Acquisition Programs and focuses on course development, curriculum design, and creating optimal learning environments.

name: Martina Wells
title: Associate Professor of the Practice of German Studies
department: Department of Languages, Cultures & Applied Linguistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Martina Wells directs the summer study abroad program in Freiburg, Germany, offering immersive German language instruction through partnership with the Goethe Institute. She supports students in developing language skills while experiencing German culture and exploring Europe.

name: Aaditya Ramdas
title: Associate Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: <span>Aaditya's research spans theory, algorithms, and applications in machine learning and statistical inference. One line of recent work focuses on the theme of reproducibility in science and technology (multiple hypothesis testing, selective inference) by designing new algorithms for controlling false discoveries in static and dynamic settings. Another line of work involves active sequential ex

name: Ann Lee
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: <span>Ann&#8217;s research interests are in developing machine learning methodology for complex data and problems in the physical sciences. She is particularly interested in trustworthy scientific inference and uncertainty quantification with generative models, and in bridging classical statistics and machine learning for simulation-based inference and experimental design. Her recent work incl

name: Alan Montgomery
title: Professor
bio: Dr. Montgomery works on the application of data mining and statistical analysis to solve marketing problems. His research has focused on developing price and promotional strategies from purchase transaction data and the analysis of clickstream data to predict consumer behavior in online environments.

name: Roni Rosenfeld
title: Professor
bio:
Dr. Rosenfeld’s ML-related work focuses on modeling the evolution of viral epidemics. He uses machine learning, large scale simulations, network analysis and stochastic process theory to try to answer research questions such as:
  1. How, and to what extent, can the evolution of infectious diseases like Influenza be predicted?
&am

name: Ziv Bar-Joseph
title: Co-Founder and Chief Science Officer
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Co-founder and Chief Science Officer focused on advancing computational approaches in biology. Leads research at the intersection of computer science and biological sciences.

name: Ben Busby
title: Senior Alliances Manager
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Senior Alliances Manager for Genomics at NVIDIA working on partnerships in computational biology. Bridges industry and academic research in genomic data analysis.

name: A. Ercument Cicek
title: Assistant Professor
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Assistant Professor developing computational methods to analyze genomic data and understand mechanisms of genomic perturbations in disease. His research spans metabolomics, transcriptomics, and genome analysis using information theory and network approaches.

name: Hosein Mohimani
title: Associate Professor
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Associate Professor at the David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA working on computational methods in biology. Conducts research at the intersection of computer science and medicine.

name: Christopher Langmead
title: Director of Data Science for Biologics Discovery
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Director of Data Science for Biologics Discovery at Amgen developing machine learning algorithms to address challenges in medicine and bioengineering. His research includes patient triage, disease progression modeling, cell therapy optimization, and therapeutic protein design.

name: Armaghan "Rumi" Naik
title: Entrepreneur in Residence
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Armaghan Naik is an entrepreneur and researcher who has pioneered AI applications in vaccine design, achieving efficacy improvements over standard treatments, and now leads the Future of Engineering and Science of Automation Initiative at CMU. His work spans machine learning, biological automation, symbolic reasoning, and engineering intelligence with experience at major companies including Intel, Sanofi, and Bloomberg.

name: Ashley Brown
title: Lab Technician Manager
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Ashley Brown serves as Lab Technician Manager for the Pfenning Lab in the Computational Biology Department. She oversees laboratory operations and technical support for research initiatives.

name: Xiaoyu Cao
title: Research Assistant
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Xiaoyu Cao is a Research Assistant in the Xu Lab within the Computational Biology Department. She supports research efforts in computational genomics and molecular biology.

name: Vijay Cherupally
title: Research Associate
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Vijay Cherupally is a Research Associate in the Pfenning Lab at the Computational Biology Department. He contributes to research in computational genomics and biological data analysis.

name: Penny Cuda
title: Software Developer
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Penny Cuda is a Software Developer for the Ruffalo Lab in the Computational Biology Department. She develops computational tools and software for biological research applications.

name: Sean Donahue
title: Research Programmer
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Sean Donahue is a Research Programmer for the Bar-Joseph Lab in the Computational Biology Department. He develops computational methods and programming solutions for biological data analysis.

name: Lahari Gadey
title: Lab Technician
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Lahari Gadey is a Lab Technician for the Pfenning Lab in the Computational Biology Department. She provides technical and operational support for laboratory research activities.

name: Sushmita Halder
title: Research Associate
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Sushmita Halder is a Research Associate in the Cuba Samaniego Lab at the Computational Biology Department. She contributes to research in computational methods for biological analysis.

name: Runmin Jiang
title: Research Associate
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Runmin Jiang is a Research Associate in the Xu Lab within the Computational Biology Department. He supports research initiatives in computational genomics and biological data analysis.

name: Mercury Liu
title: Research Associate
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Mercury Liu is a Research Associate in the Kangas Lab at the Computational Biology Department. She contributes to research in computational methods and biological analysis.

name: Thanh-Huy Nguyen
title: Research Associate
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Thanh-Huy Nguyen is a Research Associate in the Xu Lab within the Computational Biology Department. He supports research efforts in computational genomics and molecular analysis.

name: Grace Oualline
title: Research Associate
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Grace Oualline is a Research Associate in the Yu Lab at the Computational Biology Department. She contributes to research in computational methods and biological data analysis.

name: Heather Harper Sestili
title: Research Programmer
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Heather Harper Sestili is a Research Programmer for the Pfenning Lab in the Computational Biology Department. She develops computational tools and programming solutions for biological research.

name: Yuzhou Wang
title: Research Assistant
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Yuzhou Wang is a Research Assistant in the Xu Lab within the Computational Biology Department. She supports research efforts in computational genomics and molecular biology.

name: Ted Zhang
title: Research Programmer
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Ted Zhang is a Research Programmer for the Murphy Lab in the Computational Biology Department. He develops computational methods and programming solutions for biological research.

name: Yang Zhang
title: Project Scientist
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Yang Zhang is a Project Scientist in the Ma Lab at the Computational Biology Department. He leads research projects in computational methods and biological data analysis.

name: Umut Acar
title: Professor
bio: Umut Acar's research focuses on programming languages, parallel computing, and algorithms, with the goal of raising the level of abstraction for reasoning about computational problems. He develops abstractions, language constructs, algorithms, and software systems to bridge the gap between human reasoning and machine execution while maintaining efficiency.

name: Anil Ada
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Anil Ada is an Associate Teaching Professor in the Computer Science Department at Carnegie Mellon University. His work focuses on algorithms, computational complexity, and computer science education.

name: Jonathan Aldrich
title: Professor
bio: Jonathan Aldrich's research aims to improve software quality and security by providing novel ways to express and enforce structural and behavioral aspects of software design through language design and type systems. He evaluates the practical benefits of his work via case studies, human subjects experiments, and corpus studies with open source codebases.

name: George Amvrosiadis
title: Associate Research Professor
bio: George Amvrosiadis is an Associate Research Professor in Electrical & Computer Engineering affiliated with the Computer Science Department and a core member of the Parallel Data Lab. His research focuses on distributed and cloud storage systems, new storage technologies, high performance computing, and storage optimization for machine learning, with work that has been integrated into the Linux kernel and recognized with multiple R&D100 and best paper awards.

name: David Andersen
title: Professor
bio: David Andersen is a professor focusing on networks and distributed systems, with research aimed at creating systems that meet goals such as robustness, high availability, and energy efficiency. His work encompasses projects like FAWN (Fast Arrays of Wimpy Nodes) for energy-efficient computational clusters and XIA (Expressive Internet Architecture) for developing a secure, evolvable internet architecture.

name: Daniel Anderson
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Daniel Anderson is an Assistant Teaching Professor in the Computer Science Department at Carnegie Mellon University. He focuses on computer science education and course development, teaching foundational programming courses.

name: Maria Balcan
title: Cadence Design Systems Professor
department: Machine Learning Department, Computer Science Department
bio: Maria Balcan's research tackles fundamental questions in machine learning, algorithmic game theory, and algorithms, developing deep connections between these areas to solve central and emerging challenges. Her work focuses on foundations for modern learning paradigms including interactive learning, distributed learning, and multi-task learning, as well as algorithmic game theory and analysis of algorithms beyond worst-case scenarios.

name: Stephanie Balzer
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Stephanie Balzer's research aims to enable the construction of failure-free software through rigorous reasoning methods such as type systems and verification logics. She focuses on compositional verification approaches that ensure individually verified components compose to form verifiable wholes, with applications to real-world security and privacy problems.

name: Nathan Beckmann
title: Associate Professor
bio: Nathan Beckmann is an Associate Professor researching energy-efficient, general-purpose computing through spatial dataflow architectures and near-data computation. His work spans hardware and software, with a focus on building complete systems including compilers, computer architecture, and silicon testchips to improve energy efficiency by orders of magnitude.

name: Guy Blelloch
title: Professor
bio: Guy Blelloch's research focuses on the interaction between algorithms and programming languages, particularly in the context of parallel computing through both theoretical and experimental work. His work centers on designing high-level cost models for parallel constructs, building systems that make these costs meaningful, and developing efficient parallel algorithms that leverage modern language features.

name: Christopher Bogart
title: Senior Systems Scientist
bio: Christopher Bogart is a Senior Systems Scientist focused on educational technology and intelligent tutoring systems, particularly for computer science and cybersecurity workforce training. His research explores AI literacy, automated assessment tools, and experiential learning approaches to develop critical thinking skills.

name: Stephen Brookes
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Stephen Brookes is internationally recognized for seminal contributions to semantics for concurrent programs and logics for reasoning about program behavior. His work on Concurrent Separation Logic with Peter O'Hearn, which won the 2016 Gödel Prize, enables correctness proofs for software involving pointer manipulation and shared-memory concurrency.

name: David Brumley
title: Professor
bio: David Brumley's primary research focuses on software security techniques that provide users with guarantees, leveraging model checking, formal methods, compilers, and logic applied to security problems. His work spans symbolic execution, bit-level arithmetic reasoning, sound decompilation, and applied cryptography, with particular emphasis on binary-level code analysis and the automatic generation of exploits.

name: Randal Bryant
title: Faculty Emeritus
bio: Randal Bryant is a pioneering computer scientist known for his foundational work in formal verification and model checking of hardware and software systems. His research has significantly advanced techniques for verifying the correctness of complex digital circuits and concurrent systems.

name: Iliano Cervesato
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Iliano Cervesato is a teaching professor who researches the principles of programming languages and security with expertise in computational logic and cryptographic protocol analysis. He has published over 100 articles and served as program or general chair of thirteen international scientific conferences.

name: Beidi Chen
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Beidi Chen is an Assistant Professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department at Carnegie Mellon University with affiliated faculty status in the Computer Science Department. Her research focuses on building efficient and scalable systems for machine learning and distributed computing.

name: Tianqi Chen
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Tianqi Chen is an Assistant Professor interested in the intersection of machine learning and systems, focusing on how advanced learning techniques and systems can be combined effectively. He is a strong advocate for open source and open science, having created widely adopted ML systems including Apache TVM, XGBoost, and Apache MXNet.

name: Vincent Conitzer
title: Professor
bio: Vincent Conitzer is a Professor of Computer Science whose research focuses on artificial intelligence, game theory, and mechanism design. His work spans computational social choice, multiagent systems, and the intersection of economics and computer science.

name: Thomas Cortina
title: Teaching Professor, Associate Dean for Undergraduate Programs
bio: Thomas Cortina is a Teaching Professor and Associate Dean for Undergraduate Programs who focuses on computer science education and making computing accessible to broader populations through innovative teaching approaches. His research includes work on responsible computing education, unplugged activities for learning, and advancing computing interest through teacher education programs.

name: Keenan Crane
title: Associate Professor
bio: Keenan Crane develops fundamental algorithms for working with real-world geometric data by drawing on insights from differential geometry and computer science. His research spans computer graphics, geometric computing, and the numerical solution of partial differential equations on complex domains.

name: Philip R. LeDuc
title: Professor
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: LeDuc studies the fundamental links between mechanics and biochemistry at cellular and molecular levels, with applications spanning orthopedics, cardiovascular medicine, and cell division. His work integrates computational biology, genetics, and biotechnology to develop novel approaches to tissue engineering, genomics, and medical diagnostics.

name: Jeffrey Schneider
title: Research Professor
bio: Dr. Schneider is interested in active learning algorithms for efficiently controlling the experimental process during the creation and fitting of biological models. His research focuses on selection algorithms to improve the model discovery process, with previous efforts in in vivo CNS drug discovery.

name: Eric Xing
title: Professor
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Dr. Xing develops statistical models and machine learning algorithms for biological network inference, gene expression analysis, and genomic applications including cancer development and morphagenesis. He also develops statistical machine learning methodologies including graphical models and Bayesian approaches with applications to high-dimensional data analysis, vision, and natural language processing.

name: Robert F. Murphy
department: Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department
bio: Robert F. Murphy leads the Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department at Carnegie Mellon, where he pioneers computational approaches to framing and solving biological problems. His research emphasizes rigorous computational solutions grounded in both natural and computational sciences, with a focus on using computational models to drive experimental biology.

name: Karl Crary
title: Associate Professor
bio: Karl Crary researches the design and implementation of advanced programming languages with a focus on applying types to improve software development, maintenance, and performance. His current work emphasizes mechanized metatheory and certified code techniques to replace dynamic protection checks with static ones for improved reliability and security.

name: Roger Dannenberg
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Roger Dannenberg is a Professor Emeritus in the Computer Science Department specializing in artificial intelligence and music technology. His research focuses on audio processing, real-time systems, and the intersection of computer science and music composition.

name: Tim Dettmers
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Tim Dettmers' work focuses on making foundation models like ChatGPT accessible by reducing their resource requirements and developing open-source agent systems that run on consumer hardware. His research spans efficient LLM architectures, on-device deployment, and AI-driven scientific automation, with contributions recognized through awards at ICLR, NeurIPS, and the creation of the widely-used bitsandbytes library.

name: Gianni Di Caro
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Gianni Di Caro is a Teaching Professor in the Computer Science Department at Carnegie Mellon Qatar. His research focuses on swarm robotics, distributed multi-agent systems, and machine learning approaches to complex optimization problems.

name: Chris Donahue
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Chris develops and deploys generative AI for music and creativity, focusing on improving machine learning methods for controllable generative modeling of music and audio. His work extends language model methods to address domain-specific challenges in audio and music generation while creating real-world interactive systems that enable users to harness generative music AI.

name: Dave Eckhardt
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Dave Eckhardt is a Teaching Professor focused on computer systems and networking, with research interests in wireless networks and error handling. He has extensive experience teaching systems courses and mentoring students in operating systems and network design.

name: Christos Faloutsos
title: Fredkin University Professor of Computer Science
bio: Christos Faloutsos is a Fredkin University Professor specializing in graph mining and stream mining, focusing on discovering patterns in large-scale graphs and time-series data. His research emphasizes scalability and leverages power-laws and self-similarity to detect anomalies and regularities in real-world networks and sensor data.

name: Giulia Fanti
title: Associate Professor
department: Electrical and Computer Engineering / Computer Science
school: School of Computer Science / College of Engineering
bio: Giulia Fanti is an Associate Professor whose research focuses on networked systems with emphasis on security, privacy, and robustness. Her work spans blockchain systems, fraud detection, differential privacy, and the security implications of distributed systems and networks.

name: Matt Fredrikson
title: Associate Professor
bio: Matt Fredrikson's research focuses on understanding fundamental security and privacy issues in real systems through formal analysis techniques and countermeasures. His current work emphasizes privacy in machine learning applications, privacy-aware programming support, and analysis of probabilistic programs to provide rigorous guarantees against adversarial inference.

name: Gregory Ganger
title: Professor
bio: Gregory Ganger conducts broad research in computer systems including cloud computing, storage/file systems, operating systems, and distributed systems. His work focuses on systems for large-scale machine learning, cloud/cluster resource scheduling, and exploitation of new storage technologies.

name: David Garlan
title: Professor
bio: David Garlan's research focuses on software engineering, specifically software architecture, self-adaptive systems, and cyber-physical systems, with an emphasis on controlling complexity through scientific design and analysis. His work includes developing languages and tools for architectural design, investigating self-securing systems with runtime adaptation, and exploring multi-view design methods for systems combining physical and computational elements.

name: Charlie Garrod
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Charlie Garrod is an Associate Teaching Professor and Director of the CSD Undergraduate Program, focusing on computer science education and student mentorship. His research interests include crowdsourcing systems, human-computer interaction, and teaching methodologies for computer science.

name: Phillip Gibbons
title: Professor
bio: Phillip Gibbons is a Professor of Computer Science whose research focuses on systems and parallel computing, with particular emphasis on processing-in-memory architectures and their applications to database systems and data structures. His work bridges the gap between algorithmic design and hardware implementation, exploring how novel computational models can overcome traditional memory bottlenecks.

name: Garth Gibson
title: Consulting Professor
bio: Garth Gibson pursues research in large-scale parallelism in computer systems, focusing on clustering technologies, parallel and distributed file systems, and storage optimization. His work, including pioneering RAID research and the Parallel Data Laboratory, translates technological advances from academic research into commercial reality and widespread industrial adoption.

name: Virgil Gligor
title: Professor, Affiliated Faculty
bio: Virgil Gligor is a Professor focused on security, cryptography, and formal verification of computer systems. His research addresses provable security, kernel implementation verification, and establishing trust in commodity computer systems.

name: Seth Goldstein
title: Associate Professor
bio: Seth Goldstein's research centers on ensembles—systems with massive numbers of interacting components—with a goal to understand, design, and build robust systems that scale to millions or billions of elements. His work spans reconfigurable computing, molecular computing through the Claytronics project, compiler optimization, and the BoLT platform exploring new monetary systems and distributed ledgers.

name: Vipul Goyal
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Vipul Goyal is an adjunct faculty member specializing in cryptography and security theory at Carnegie Mellon University. His research focuses on cryptographic protocols, private computation, and secure distributed systems including blockchain applications.

name: Meredith Grelli
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Entrepreneurship
bio: Meredith Grelli is an Associate Teaching Professor of Entrepreneurship and Assistant Dean for Entrepreneurship Initiatives. She focuses on fostering entrepreneurial initiatives and supporting technology innovation and commercialization.

name: Anupam Gupta
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Anupam Gupta's research focuses on network design and metric embeddings, with applications to approximation algorithms and graph problems. His work addresses algorithmic challenges in network optimization, uncertainty handling in data, and the design of provably good algorithms for complex network and metric-based problems.

name: Venkatesan Guruswami
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Venkatesan Guruswami is an expert in coding theory, list decoding, and the algorithmic and combinatorial aspects of error-correcting codes. His research focuses on refutation algorithms for constraint satisfaction problems, recovery of information from corrupted data, and the properties of random linear codes.

name: Mohammad Hammoud
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Mohammad Hammoud is an Associate Teaching Professor in the Computer Science Department at CMU Qatar. He is dedicated to teaching foundational computer science concepts and mentoring students in systems and algorithms.

name: Mor Harchol-Balter
title: Dr. Bruce J. Nelson Professor of Computer Science
bio: Mor Harchol-Balter is a leading researcher in performance analysis and design of computer systems, particularly distributed systems. She develops analytical models to understand system characteristics and redesign systems for improved performance, challenging conventional wisdom in areas like load balancing, job migration, and scheduling policies.

name: Robert Harper
title: Professor
bio: Robert Harper's research develops a comprehensive theory of programming that integrates with software development practice, centered on type theory as the language of computation. His work applies the propositions-as-types principle to connect programming with mathematical reasoning and proof verification.

name: Khaled Harras
title: Teaching Professor, Computer Science (Qatar)
bio: Khaled Harras is the Senior Associate Dean of Faculty and Director of the HBJ Center for CS Education at Carnegie Mellon University Qatar, where he leads initiatives in computer science education and faculty development. He serves as a Teaching Professor and is dedicated to advancing CS education and academic excellence.

name: Marijn Heule
title: Associate Professor
bio: Marijn Heule's research focuses on solving hard problems automatically with high confidence in results using formal methods and automated reasoning. His work spans trusted computing, proof systems, and developing tools for formal verification with applications to safety-critical systems in healthcare, finance, and aviation.

name: Jessica Hodgins
title: Allen Newell University Professor of Computer Science And Robotics
bio: Dr. Hodgins's research focuses on the coordination and control of dynamic physical systems, both natural and human-made, exploring techniques that allow robots and animated creatures to plan and control their actions in complex environments. Her work combines motion capture data with physically realistic simulation to generate natural motion for computer animation and robotic control.

name: Jan Hoffmann
title: Associate Professor
bio: Jan Hoffmann's research focuses on discovering mathematical techniques for quantitative analysis and verification of programs, enabling developers to reason about properties like energy usage, execution time, and security. His work combines formal methods, programming languages, and constraint solving to create compositional and practical tools for understanding resource usage and probabilistic properties in software.

name: Farnam Jahanian
title: President, Professor
bio: Farnam Jahanian is the President of Carnegie Mellon University and a Professor in the Computer Science Department. His research interests span quantum computing, cyber-physical systems, and networked computing applications.

name: Aayush Jain
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Aayush Jain is interested in theoretical and applied cryptography and its connections with related areas of theoretical computer science. He was a Postdoctoral Fellow at NTT Research and completed his PhD at UCLA under Prof. Amit Sahai.

name: Limin Jia
title: Research Professor
bio: Limin Jia is a Research Professor working on systems security and program analysis. Her research focuses on vulnerability detection, exploit generation, and formal methods for secure software systems.

name: Zhihao Jia
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Zhihao Jia is an assistant professor working on computer systems and machine learning, focusing on accelerating deep learning computations on modern hardware and optimizing quantum computations on intermediate-scale quantum devices. His research bridges systems design with emerging application domains including machine learning, quantum computing, and large-scale data analytics.

name: Takeo Kanade
title: SCS Founders University Professor
bio: Takeo Kanade is a pioneering researcher in computer vision and robotics who develops sound mathematical and computational models based on physical and geometric principles to create intelligent machines. His research spans motion recovery, stereo vision, object recognition, virtualized reality, medical robotics, and autonomous systems, with notable contributions including the factorization method and multi-baseline stereo techniques.

name: Zeliha Dilsun Kaynar
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Dilsun Kaynar is an Associate Teaching Professor at Carnegie Mellon's Computer Science Department with research expertise in formal verification, distributed systems, and security policy enforcement. Her work focuses on the theoretical foundations of programming languages, formal modeling, and verification of secure systems.

name: Fatma Kilinc-Karzan
title: Professor
bio: Fatma Kilinc-Karzan is a Professor of Operations Research at the Tepper School of Business with affiliated faculty status in the Computer Science Department. Her research focuses on convex relaxations, optimization algorithms, and the mathematical foundations of large-scale optimization problems.

name: Zico Kolter
title: Professor
bio: Zico Kolter's research focuses on making deep learning algorithms more robust, safer, and understanding how data impacts model function. His group works on assessing robustness, embedding complex modules like optimization solvers within deep architectures, and developing new types of neural network architectures.

name: David Kosbie
title: Teaching Professor
bio: David Kosbie is a Teaching Professor focused on computer science education and supporting diverse learners in programming. His research interests include constraint-based programming systems, graphical user interface design, and equitable approaches to K-12 computer science education.

name: Pravesh Kothari
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Pravesh Kothari designs efficient algorithms and studies algorithmic thresholds for average-case computational problems in theoretical computer science and related areas. His research includes the development of sum-of-squares methods that apply proof complexity ideas to semidefinite programming relaxations for hard optimization problems.

name: Aviral Kumar
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Aviral Kumar is an Assistant Professor in the School of Computer Science with joint appointments in the Computer Science Department and Machine Learning Department. His research focuses on reinforcement learning and decision-making, particularly offline reinforcement learning, scaling up reinforcement learning, and the intersection of reinforcement learning with foundation models.

name: Swarun Kumar
title: Professor
bio: Swarun Kumar is a Professor who designs and builds systems addressing fundamental technical challenges in wireless networks, with a special focus on interference management and novel services for IoT. His research explores new connections between wireless networking and disciplines such as robotics, radar, computer vision, security, and signal processing.

name: William Kuszmaul
title: Assistant Professor
bio: William's research focuses on designing and analyzing randomized algorithms and data structures. His work addresses fundamental problems in algorithms and computational complexity.

name: Tai-Sing Lee
title: Professor
bio: Tai-Sing Lee's research seeks to elucidate the computational principles and neural mechanisms underlying visual perception using computational, mathematical, and neurophysiological techniques. His work aims to discover the secrets of neural computation, advance computer vision technology, and restore vision to the visually impaired.

name: Jason Li
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Jason Li is an Assistant Professor in the Computer Science Department specializing in algorithms and complexity theory. His research focuses on understanding the fundamental limits and possibilities of efficient computation.

name: Minchen Li
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Minchen is an assistant professor at CMU whose research focuses on advancing physical simulation in visual computing, manufacturing, and robotics through methods combining numerical analysis, high-performance computing, and machine learning. His work emphasizes achieving robustness, efficiency, accuracy, and versatility, including the development of the groundbreaking Incremental Potential Contact (IPC) method for frictional contact simulation.

name: Yang Liu
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Yang Liu is an Assistant Professor at Carnegie Mellon University whose research spans mathematics and theoretical computer science, with particular interests in graph algorithms, optimization, high-dimensional geometry, and additive combinatorics. His work has been recognized with the A.W. Tucker Prize, the Google Ph.D. Research Fellowship, and Best Paper awards at top-tier conferences including FOCS, STOC, and ITCS.

name: Brandon Lucia
title: Professor
bio: Brandon Lucia is a professor researching the intersection of computer architecture, systems, and programming languages to create more capable, reliable, and efficient computing systems. His research focuses on intermittent computing devices that harvest energy from their environment and parallel computing systems for the post-Moore's Law era.

name: Ruben Martins
title: Assistant Research Professor
bio: Ruben Martins conducts research at the intersection of constraint programming with program synthesis, analysis, and verification, with recent focus on using programming synthesis to improve programmer productivity and automate data science tasks. He has published extensively in top-tier venues including POPL, PLDI, and FSE, and developed award-winning constraint solvers including Open-WBO, a MaxSAT solver used for solving real-world discrete optimization problems.

name: Gary Miller
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Gary Miller's research focuses on sequential and parallel algorithm design with applications to scientific computation and image processing, emphasizing both theoretical guarantees and practical efficiency. His work spans mesh generation, spectral graph theory, and fast algorithms for image processing, particularly in medical imaging and convex optimization.

name: Stefan Mitsch
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Stefan Mitsch conducts research on formal verification and dynamic logic for hybrid systems, with applications to safety-critical domains like collision avoidance and autonomous vehicles. His work focuses on formally verified models and refinement techniques for complex systems.

name: Todd Mowry
title: Professor
bio: Todd Mowry's research focuses on dramatically boosting the performance of future microprocessor-based systems by exploiting various forms of parallelism through novel architectural, compiler, and operating systems support. His work addresses key challenges in single-chip multiprocessing and tolerating off-chip latency through techniques like thread-level data speculation and intelligent prefetching.

name: Ryan O'Donnell
title: Professor
bio: Ryan O'Donnell is a theoretical computer scientist whose research focuses on Fourier analysis of Boolean functions, constraint satisfaction problems, and quantum computation. His work explores fundamental questions in complexity theory, property testing, and the theoretical foundations of computation.

name: David O'Hallaron
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: David O'Hallaron works in computer systems with broad interests including cloud computing, online education, and scientific computing. His primary focus is on autograding systems and developing cloud-based services for programming education.

name: Matthew O'Toole
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Matthew O'Toole is an Assistant Professor researching computational imaging, a field combining optics, electronics, and computational processing to capture and display visual information. His work focuses on using programmable lights and cameras to analyze the world, with applications spanning computer vision and graphics.

name: Bryan Parno
title: Professor
bio: Bryan Parno is a Professor of Computer Science at Carnegie Mellon University whose research focuses on fundamental improvements in designing and building secure systems, combining theory and practice to provide formal security guarantees. His work spans verifiable computation, bootstrapping trust in computers, and formally verified secure systems, with applications to cloud computing, Intel CPUs, Windows, and iOS.

name: Jignesh Patel
title: Professor
bio: Jignesh Patel is a professor whose research focuses on data management, emphasizing both system efficiency through scalable data platforms and human efficiency via LLM-based query interfaces. His work explores high-performance database systems that work synergistically with hardware and democratizing data analytics through no-code approaches.

name: Andrew Pavlo
title: Associate Professor
bio: Andrew Pavlo is an Associate Professor of Databaseology at Carnegie Mellon University whose research focuses on database systems, distributed systems, and data-intensive computing. He is the CEO and co-founder of OtterTune, a database tuning start-up, and has received numerous distinctions including the NSF CAREER Award, Sloan Fellowship, and ACM SIGMOD Jim Gray Dissertation Award.

name: Richard Peng
title: Associate Professor
bio: Richard Peng designs and analyzes fast algorithms for fundamental computational problems including graph algorithms, dynamic algorithms, and linear algebraic algorithms. He is part of the theory group at CMU and brings expertise from postdoctoral work in Applied Math at MIT.

name: Frank Pfenning
title: Professor
bio: Frank Pfenning's research focuses on understanding the principles of programming languages through the development of meta-languages, expressive type systems, and applications of programming language techniques to domains like robotics and grid computing. His work aims to improve program development, software maintenance, and correctness through well-designed languages and formal reasoning about language properties.

name: Nancy Pollard
title: Professor
bio: Nancy Pollard studies physical interaction with the environment, focusing on how humans and robots select and apply forces to manipulate objects and navigate complex terrain. Her work bridges robotics and computer graphics by using measured human examples to create more dexterous robots and natural-looking animated character motion, with particular emphasis on modeling and controlling hand behavior.

name: Aditi Raghunathan
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Aditi Raghunathan is an Assistant Professor in the Computer Science Department specializing in machine learning and artificial intelligence. Her research focuses on developing robust and reliable machine learning systems.

name: Brian Railing
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Brian Railing is an Associate Teaching Professor focused on computer science education and systems teaching. His research investigates pedagogical approaches to teaching parallelism, computer architecture, and assessment methods in computer science courses.

name: R. Ravi
title: Vasantrao Dempo Professor of Operations Research and Computer Science
department: Tepper School of Business, Computer Science Department (Affiliated)
bio: R. Ravi is the Vasantrao Dempo Professor of Operations Research and Computer Science at CMU, with expertise in approximation algorithms and combinatorial optimization. His research focuses on theoretical computer science problems in network design, routing, and algorithmic mechanism design with applications to real-world optimization challenges.

name: Raj Reddy
title: Moza Bint Nasser University Professor
bio: Dr. Reddy's research interests include the study of human-computer interaction and artificial intelligence. His current research projects span spoken language systems, gigabit networks, universal digital libraries, and distance learning on demand.

name: Kelly Rivers
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Kelly Rivers is an Associate Teaching Professor who specializes in introductory programming education with a focus on active learning and live coding. Her prior research has explored intelligent tutoring systems and data-driven hint generation for programming, though she currently focuses on teaching and student advising.

name: Feras Saad
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Feras Saad develops techniques for building scalable computing systems that enable flexible probabilistic modeling and sound probabilistic inference by integrating ideas from programming languages and probabilistic AI. His research spans the computing stack from foundational questions about probability representation to programming systems and statistical algorithms for data science applications.

name: Majd Sakr
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Majd Sakr is a Teaching Professor focused on computing education and the development of AI literacy in academic and occupational settings. His research examines the effectiveness of AI-generated learning tools, automated assessment systems, and training methods for emerging AI-related careers.

name: Tuomas Sandholm
title: Angel Jordan University Professor of Computer Science
bio: Tuomas Sandholm's research focuses on artificial intelligence, game theory, and mechanism design with applications to electronic marketplaces and automated systems. His work bridges theoretical game-theoretic incentive issues with computational algorithms, developing both market mechanisms and software agents that operate optimally in real-world settings such as kidney exchanges, advertising markets, and poker.

name: Mahadev Satyanarayanan
title: Jaime Carbonell University Professor of Computer Science
bio: Satya's multi-decade research career has focused on the challenges of performance, scalability, availability and trust in information systems that reach from the cloud to the mobile edge of the Internet, pioneering advances in distributed systems, mobile computing, and edge computing. His 2009 publication on VM-based cloudlets has led to the emergence of Edge Computing and he is exploring applications including wearable cognitive assistance and human-in-the-loop machine learning systems.

name: Dana Scott
title: University Professor Emeritus
bio: Dana Scott is a pioneering logician and computer scientist whose foundational work in mathematical logic and semantics has shaped the theoretical underpinnings of programming languages and computation. His research spans denotational semantics, lambda calculus, type theory, and automata theory, with lasting influence on both mathematics and computer science.

name: Vyas Sekar
title: Tan Family Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Vyas Sekar's research focuses on providing practical and rigorous solutions for network infrastructures to meet evolving performance and security requirements of modern applications. His work spans programmable software-defined network functions and network support for large-scale content distribution, with emphasis on balancing architectural rigor with real-world deployability.

name: Srinivasan Seshan
title: Joseph Traub Professor of Computer Science
bio: Srinivasan Seshan is a professor whose primary interests lie in network protocols and distributed network applications, including transport/routing protocol interactions, wireless networks, and RAID system design. His current research explores next-generation Internet architectures, video content delivery mechanisms, and systems challenges in mobile and wireless networks for data collection and privacy management.

name: Nihar Shah
title: Associate Professor
bio: Nihar Shah's research focuses on statistical learning, game theory, and information theory, with emphasis on crowdsourcing and learning from people. His work addresses problems in peer review, reviewer assignment, and understanding how multiple sources of information can be aggregated and analyzed effectively.

name: Mary Shaw
title: A. J. Perlis University Professor
bio: Mary Shaw's research focuses on establishing a genuine engineering discipline for software systems design and development, with particular emphasis on software architecture and design methods for building complex systems. Her current interests include sufficient correctness, design spaces, self-adaptive systems informed by control theory, and translating research results into actionable guidance for practitioners.

name: Justine Sherry
title: Associate Professor
bio: Justine's research focuses on software and hardware networked systems, including middleboxes, FPGA packet processing, measurement, cloud computing, and congestion control. Her work brings the benefits of cloud computing to networking by proposing that middlebox functionality be implemented as a software service running in the cloud.

name: Reid Simmons
title: Research Professor
bio: Reid Simmons' research focuses on reliable autonomous systems, particularly mobile robots operating in uncertain, unstructured environments. His work addresses robot planning, reasoning about uncertainty, error diagnosis and recovery, human-robot interaction, and multi-robot coordination using probabilistic reasoning techniques.

name: Dimitrios Skarlatos
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Dimitrios Skarlatos is an assistant professor who leads the Computer Architecture and Operating Systems (CAOS) group, focusing on performance, security, and efficiency for AI datacenters. His cross-cutting research bridges computer architecture and operating systems, with significant contributions recognized through numerous awards including the NSF CAREER award and multiple best paper awards.

name: Danny Sleator
title: Professor
bio: Danny Sleator works on algorithms and data structures, with expertise in amortized analysis, competitive algorithms, and self-adjusting data structures. His research spans diverse areas including natural language parsing, game playing, and the theoretical foundations of algorithm design.

name: Akshitha Sriraman
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Akshitha Sriraman is an Assistant Professor whose research focuses on systems design, particularly in cloud computing and sustainable server architecture. Her work addresses the democratization of computing resources and the environmental impact of large-scale computing systems.

name: Peter Steenkiste
title: Professor of Computer Science and Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Peter's research focuses on computer networking and distributed systems, with an emphasis on future Internet architecture and wireless networking. He builds prototype systems that incorporate research ideas and evaluates them using real applications in realistic settings.

name: Mark Stehlik
title: University Professor, Assistant Dean for Outreach
bio: Mark Stehlik is a University Professor and Assistant Dean for Outreach who focuses on computer science education at the K-12 level, with particular emphasis on making programming accessible to diverse learners. His research explores effective pedagogical approaches to teaching foundational concepts like loops and code comprehension, while supporting multilingual students in computing classrooms.

name: Klaus Sutner
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Klaus Sutner is a Teaching Professor who specializes in automata theory, dynamical systems, and computational models, with a focus on abelian automaton groups and reversible computation. His research explores the intersection of theoretical computer science and mathematical structures, and he has contributed to understanding physics-like models of computation.

name: Michael Taylor
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Michael Taylor is an assistant teaching professor who teaches Fundamentals of Programming and Computer Science, having completed his Ph.D. at the Robotics Institute's CREATE Lab where he focused on low-cost air quality sensing and automated sensor calibration. His research has contributed to real-world applications including Airviz Inc. for affordable air quality monitoring and Sylvia's Sisters, a non-profit addressing education disparities.

name: David Touretzky
title: Research Professor
bio: David Touretzky's research focuses on robotics and computing education, with a particular emphasis on making advanced robot programming accessible to beginners through frameworks like Tekkotsu. His work spans from developing affordable mobile manipulators for educational use to creating programming curricula that teach computational thinking concepts like finite state machines to K-12 students.

name: Rashmi Korlakai Vinayak
title: Associate Professor
bio: Rashmi's research focuses on designing theoretically optimal solutions for distributed data systems with practical implementations. Her work on fault tolerance and resource efficiency has been integrated into Apache Hadoop 3.0 and is used by major companies in their storage and analytics products.

name: Patrick Virtue
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Pat Virtue is an Assistant Teaching Professor focusing on teaching techniques for artificial intelligence and machine learning, with emphasis on active learning methods and inclusive learning environments. His research interests span AI/ML curriculum development, effective instruction for large classes, and applications of neural networks to medical imaging.

name: Riad Wahby
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Riad Wahby is an Assistant Professor in Electrical and Computer Engineering with affiliated faculty status in the Computer Science Department. His research focuses on theoretical computer science with applications to cryptography and verification.

name: Weina Wang
title: Associate Professor
bio: Weina Wang's research focuses on applied probability and stochastic systems with applications in data centers, cloud computing, and privacy-preserving data analytics. Her work addresses fundamental limits of large-scale computing systems and designs algorithms with optimal performance guarantees for throughput and latency.

name: Jeannette Wing
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Jeannette Wing's research focuses on providing rigorous foundations for software design and implementation, with particular emphasis on trustworthy computing including reliability, security, privacy, and usability. She is interested in formal specification and analysis techniques for complex software systems, seeking compositional approaches and formal logics for security, privacy, and information flow analysis.

name: David Woodruff
title: Professor
bio: David Woodruff is a Professor whose research focuses on communication complexity, data stream algorithms, and lower bounds with applications to machine learning and numerical linear algebra. His work spans algorithm design, sketching techniques, and sparse recovery with theoretical foundations in computational complexity.

name: Franceska Xhakaj
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Franceska Xhakaj is an Assistant Teaching Professor focused on computer science education and pedagogy. She teaches foundational computer science courses and develops curriculum to improve student learning outcomes.

name: Wenting Zheng
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Wenting Zheng is an Assistant Professor interested in system security and applied cryptography, focusing on building practical systems for secure outsourced computation in the cloud and secure collaborative computation among mutually distrusting parties. She believes that combining systems and advanced cryptography can enhance application security and enable new capabilities.

name: Jun-Yan Zhu
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Jun-Yan Zhu is an Assistant Professor at CMU who conducts research in artificial intelligence with a focus on neural rendering and generative modeling. His work addresses fundamental problems in computer vision and graphics through deep learning approaches.

name: Henny Admoni
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Henny Admoni leads the Human And Robot Partners (HARP) Lab and develops intelligent autonomous robots that assist humans on complex tasks through algorithms grounded in human behavior research. Her work applies robotics, computer science, and cognitive psychology to enable robots that detect, interpret, and respond to human verbal and nonverbal behavior, with applications in home assistants, tutoring robots, and robotic therapy.

name: Dimitrios (Dimi) Apostolopoulos
title: Principal Systems Scientist
bio: Dimitrios Apostolopoulos is a Principal Systems Scientist at the Robotics Institute and NREC who specializes in using robotics for discovery, exploration, and hazardous duty applications. His research focuses on autonomous robots for mining, all-terrain mobility mechanisms, and the design and prototyping of unmanned ground vehicles for demanding industrial and scientific environments.

name: Christopher G. Atkeson
title: Professor
bio: Christopher Atkeson is a Professor in the Robotics Institute specializing in enabling robots to learn strategies and tasks through direct instruction, observation, and reinforcement learning. His research combines machine learning algorithms with implementations on actual robots to develop humanoid systems and human-aware environments capable of perceiving and interacting with humans.

name: J. Andrew (Drew) Bagnell
title: Consulting Professor
bio: Drew Bagnell focuses on 'closing the loop' on complex systems by designing algorithms that allow systems to observe their operation and improve performance. His research bridges planning, control, and computational learning with applications in mobile robotics and structured probabilistic models for decision making.

name: Andrea Bajcsy
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Andrea Bajcsy is an Assistant Professor leading the Interactive and Trustworthy Robotics Lab, where she develops algorithms and theory for enabling embodied AI agents to reliably interact with the open world. Her research focuses on quantifying robot confidence, computing safe interaction policies, and aligning AI models with human values, drawing upon optimal control, reinforcement learning, dynamic games, and deep learning.

name: David Bourne
title: Principal Systems Scientist
bio: David Bourne's research focuses on building intelligent systems for automated manufacturing, from design capture through automated production. His work spans design representation, tolerance semantics, setup and process planning, sensor fusion, and real-time distributed coordination to enable production of high-quality one-off parts.

name: Howie Choset
title: Kavcic-Moura Professor of Computer Science
bio: Howie Choset brings precision from computer science and applied mathematics to mechanical systems design, with a focus on highly articulated snake robots that can navigate constrained spaces. His research spans mechanism design, motion planning, and path planning with applications in minimally invasive surgery, search and rescue, and manufacturing.

name: Fernando De la Torre Frade
title: Research Professor
bio: Dr. De la Torre's research focuses on machine learning, signal processing, and computer vision, with emphasis on understanding human behavior from multimodal sensors such as video and body sensors. His work spans component analysis methods, human sensing and modeling, and face analysis algorithms for tracking, recognition, and emotion detection.

name: John M. Dolan
title: Principal Systems Scientist
bio: John Dolan creates systems and methodologies for collaboration between groups of robots and humans, combining autonomous perception and control with effective human-machine interfaces. His research spans autonomous driving, telesupervision of multiple robots, and mission planning for reliable robotic teams.

name: Artur W. Dubrawski
title: Alumni Research Professor of Computer Science
bio: Artur W. Dubrawski is an Alumni Research Professor interested in intelligent systems that are practical and economically viable, driven by real-world applications in public health, food safety, and equipment health monitoring. His research focuses on machine learning algorithms, data structures, and probabilistic modeling to enable predictive analysis and understanding of complex data.

name: Michael Erdmann
title: Professor
bio: Michael Erdmann is interested in making robots act purposefully and successfully despite sensor noise, imprecise actions, and environmental uncertainty. His research applies topological methods to robot planning and control problems, including work on strategy complexes, controllability theorems, and topological perspectives on privacy.

name: Zackory Erickson
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Zackory Erickson's research explores healthcare robotics with an emphasis on empowering older adults and people with disabilities through physically assistive systems. His work combines physics-based simulation, perception, and learning to enable robots to intelligently assist people with activities of daily living.

name: John Galeotti
title: Senior Systems Scientist
bio: John Galeotti is a Systems Scientist directing the Biomedical Image Guidance Laboratory, focusing on applying computer-controlled optics, image analysis, and visualization to image-guided interventions and biomedical research. His research aims to develop novel optical imaging modalities and improve patient outcomes through interdisciplinary work in medical robotics, computer vision, and biomedical engineering.

name: Hartmut Geyer
title: Professor
bio: Hartmut Geyer develops unified theories of legged dynamic systems with a focus on speed, robustness, and agility, implementing this research on bipedal robots of human scale. His work also includes developing physiologically plausible models of human neuromuscular control and designing powered prosthetic legs that achieve human-like gait stability through bio-inspired control strategies.

name: Ioannis Gkioulekas
title: Associate Professor
bio: Ioannis Gkioulekas focuses on computational imaging through the joint design of optics, electronics, and computation to create imaging systems with unprecedented capabilities. His research includes imaging systems that see around corners or through skin, passive 3D sensing, ultrafast programmable lenses, and adaptive imaging systems.

name: Abhinav Gupta
title: Professor
bio: Abhinav Gupta develops representation and reasoning approaches for deeper understanding of visual scenes by grounding them in 3D structure and physical, functional, and causal relationships. His research explores the intersection of language and vision, human-object interactions, and how visual perception depends on affordances and human abilities.

name: Zakia Hammal
title: Assistant Research Professor
bio: Zakia Hammal is an Assistant Research Professor specializing in multimodal human behavior modeling in social interaction, health informatics, and affective computing. Her research focuses on computational models for assessing psychiatric disorders, pain intensity, autism spectrum markers, and facial expressiveness using computer vision and machine learning techniques.

name: David Held
title: Associate Professor
bio: David Held develops machine learning and computer vision methods for robotic perception and control that enable robots to operate in cluttered, dynamic environments. His research applies deep learning to understand environmental changes and has applications in object manipulation and autonomous driving.

name: Jessica K. Hodgins
title: Allen Newell University Professor of Computer Science and Robotics
department: Robotics Institute / School of Computer Science
bio: Jessica Hodgins' research focuses on the coordination and control of dynamic physical systems, exploring techniques that enable robots and animated creatures to plan and control actions in complex environments while interacting with humans. Her work spans computer graphics for human motion animation, human-robot interaction design, and control techniques for humanoid robots to produce natural-looking motion.

name: Ralph Hollis
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Ralph Hollis conducts research on innovative robotic hardware and systems, with expertise in distributed agent-based cooperative manipulation, haptic human-computer interaction, and dynamically stable mobile robots. His work emphasizes synthesizing novel robotic technology from physical principles and applying it to real-world problems in assembly, medical applications, and human-centered robotics.

name: Jeffrey Ichnowski
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Jeffrey Ichnowski is an Assistant Professor at The Robotics Institute focused on advancing robotic grasping, manipulation, and motion planning through deep learning approaches.
