His research addresses foundational challenges in robotics, combining theoretical insights with practical applications in robotic control and perception.

name: Laszlo A. Jeni
title: Assistant Research Professor
bio: Laszlo Jeni directs the Computational Behavior (CUBE) Lab, focusing on computer vision and machine learning for behavior science and affective computing. His research models, analyzes, and synthesizes human behavior using diverse sensors including video and biosensors, with applications in human health, computer graphics, and assistive vision.

name: Michael Kaess
title: Associate Professor
bio: Michael Kaess is an Associate Professor at the Robotics Institute whose research focuses on 3D mapping and localization using diverse sensors including vision, laser, inertial, GPS and sonar for mobile robots. His work explores novel algorithms for efficient and robust inference at the intersection of linear algebra and probabilistic graphical models to enable online robot operation.

name: George A. Kantor
title: Research Professor / Associate Director of Education
bio: George Kantor's research addresses the control of robotic mechanisms with non-trivial dynamics and state estimation through indirect measurements using both analytical and experimental approaches. His work spans applications including agricultural robotics, autonomous vehicles, underwater robotics, and mining robotics, translating theoretical concepts into real-world implementations.

name: Alonzo Kelly
title: Faculty Emeritus
bio: Alonzo Kelly develops practical mobile robot systems focused on solving fundamental challenges in real-world applications, from structured indoor environments to unstructured off-road terrains. His research spans modeling and simulation, position estimation, motion control, path planning, and human-robot interfaces for autonomous systems.

name: Kris M. Kitani
title: Associate Research Professor
bio: Kris M. Kitani is an Associate Research Professor at the Robotics Institute whose research spans computer vision, human activity forecasting, and first-person vision with applications to assistive technologies for people with visual impairment. He directs the Cognitive Assistance Laboratory and conducts research in deep learning, inverse reinforcement learning, and human-robot collaboration.

name: Adam Bjorndahl
title: Associate Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Adam Bjorndahl is an Associate Professor of Philosophy specializing in modal logic, game theory, decision theory, and formal epistemology. He serves as the Director of Graduate Studies in the Department of Philosophy.

name: Atoosa Kasirzadeh
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Atoosa Kasirzadeh is an Assistant Professor of Philosophy at Carnegie Mellon University who specializes in the ethical implications of artificial intelligence and emerging technologies. Her research focuses on bridging philosophy and AI policy to address questions of fairness, accountability, and responsible AI development.

name: Kevin Kelly
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Kevin Kelly investigates the philosophical foundations of scientific reasoning, particularly how Ockham's razor and principles of simplicity enable scientists to converge on truth through successive theory revisions. His work bridges formal epistemology, mathematical logic, and artificial intelligence to justify classical norms of scientific methodology and belief change.

name: Alex John London
title: K&L Gates Professor of Ethics and Computational Technologies
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Alex John London is the K&L Gates Professor of Ethics and Computational Technologies whose work focuses on ethical and policy issues surrounding the development and deployment of novel technologies in medicine, biotechnology, and artificial intelligence. His research addresses methodological issues in theoretical and practical ethics, cross-national justice and fairness, and structural obstacles to safe and effective technologies.

name: Krzysztof Mierzewski
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences

name: Aydin Mohseni
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Aydin Mohseni is a philosopher of science and formal and social epistemologist whose work is informed by game and decision theory and Bayesian statistics. His research interests include existential risk and AI safety.

name: Joseph Ramsey
title: Special Faculty and Director of Research Computing
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Joseph Ramsey is a computational philosopher specializing in causal discovery algorithms and their applications across diverse domains including climate science, neuroscience, and medical imaging. He directs major research projects like Tetrad (causal search algorithms) and AProS (automated proof generation), while also developing novel algorithms for detecting causal structures in high-dimensional data.

name: Richard Scheines
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Richard Scheines researches causal discovery, focusing on learning about causation from statistical evidence through the TETRAD project, a 25-year collaboration spanning philosophy and computer science. He also develops educational software including intelligent tutors and virtual labs to teach causal and statistical reasoning.

name: Teddy Seidenfeld
title: Herbert A. Simon University Professor of Philosophy and Statistics
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Teddy Seidenfeld works on foundations at the interface between philosophy and statistics, with particular focus on problems involving multiple decision makers and collective agency. His research extends Bayesian theory to permit unified standards for individual and cooperative group decision-making, and examines phenomena like dilation in probabilistic reasoning and the value of information.

name: Wilfried Sieg
title: Patrick Suppes Professor of Philosophy
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Wilfried Sieg's research in mathematical logic focuses on proof theoretic investigations and the foundations of mathematics, complemented by work in the history of modern logic and philosophy of mathematics. His research connects foundational mathematical positions with philosophical views, particularly through the study of relative consistency proofs, Herbrand analyses, and the mathematical explication of computability.

name: Mandy Simons
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Mandy Simons is a professor of philosophy specializing in linguistic pragmatics and the philosophy of language, with research focusing on presupposition, implicature, and information structure in natural language. She also serves as Director of the Program in Linguistics at CMU and holds an adjunct appointment in the Department of Linguistics at the University of Pittsburgh.

name: Joel Smith
title: Distinguished Career Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Joel Smith's research focuses on philosophy of science, including historical analyses of inconsistencies in scientific theories and the nature of scientific representations in knowledge pursuit. He has also devoted significant effort to investigating how information technology can improve instruction in liberal arts, sciences, engineering, and mathematics.

name: Peter Spirtes
title: Marianna Brown Dietrich Professor and Head of Philosophy
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Peter Spirtes develops methods for inferring causal relationships from statistical data in observational studies across epidemiology, econometrics, and social sciences. His TETRAD project combines philosophy, statistics, graph theory, and computer science to create provably correct algorithms for causal structure learning and to understand the theoretical limits of reliable inference from uncontrolled data.

name: Danielle Wenner
title: Associate Professor of Philosophy
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Danielle Wenner is an Associate Professor of Philosophy at Carnegie Mellon University whose research addresses lacuna in applied ethics and applied political philosophy, including research ethics, global justice and exploitation, and democracy and group decision-making. She is also the Associate Director of CMU's Center for Ethics & Policy and completed a clinical ethics fellowship at the Cleveland Clinic.

name: Tom Werner
title: Associate Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Tom Werner is an Associate Teaching Professor of Linguistics in the Department of Philosophy at CMU, focusing on the logical and theoretical underpinnings of linguistic fieldwork, particularly monolingual elicitation. His research spans phonology, syntax, semantics, and writing systems across diverse languages including Sakha, Frisian, Japanese, and Lincos.

name: Francesca Zaffora Blando
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Francesca Zaffora Blando is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Philosophy at Carnegie Mellon University. Her research focuses on epistemology, philosophy of language, philosophy of mind, and logic.

name: Kun Zhang
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Kun Zhang is a professor in the CMU philosophy department and an affiliate faculty member in the machine learning department whose research focuses on causal discovery and causality-based learning. His work develops automated methods for causal discovery, investigates learning problems from a causal perspective, and studies philosophical foundations of causation with applications to neuroscience, computational finance, and climate analysis.

name: Kevin Zollman
title: Herbert A. Simon Professor of Philosophy and Social and Decision Sciences
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Kevin Zollman is the Herbert A. Simon Professor of Philosophy and Social and Decision Sciences at Carnegie Mellon University and Director of the Institute for Complex Social Dynamics. His research focuses on game theory, agent-based modeling, and the philosophy of science.

name: Erin Carbone
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Erin Carbone studies the psychological motivations behind counterintuitive behaviors such as self-handicapping, information avoidance, and strategic disclosure, with particular focus on privacy preferences and gender differences in sharing information. Her research draws on behavioral economics and decision science to understand why people engage in costly or disadvantageous actions.

name: Gretchen Chapman
title: Department Head and Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Gretchen Chapman is a Professor in Social & Decision Sciences whose research illuminates psychological processes underlying decision making and designs theoretically-motivated, policy-relevant interventions to facilitate healthy and prosocial behaviors such as vaccination and blood donation. She combines judgment and decision making with health psychology, using laboratory and field experiments to test behavioral interventions while exploring theoretical mechanisms and yielding policy insights.

name: John Conlon
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: John Conlon is a behavioral economist who uses experiments to explore how people form and act on their expectations, with applications to labor markets and education choices. Much of his work focuses on the role of attention and memory in belief formation and decision-making.

name: Thomas Costello
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Thomas Costello is an Assistant Professor in Social and Decision Sciences who integrates psychology, political science, and human-computer interaction to examine how viewpoints form, differ across individuals, and change over time. His research also investigates the impacts of artificial intelligence on belief systems and societal processes, including developing tools like DebunkBot.com to combat misinformation.

name: Simon DeDeo
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Simon DeDeo is a professor who leads the Laboratory for Social Minds, conducting empirical investigations and building mathematical theories of historical and contemporary social phenomena across timescales from cultural evolution to real-time animal behavior. His research spans topics including Wikipedia editing dynamics, the French Revolution, and major transitions in political order, with the goal of understanding and predicting humanity's future.

name: Julie Downs
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Julie Downs studies how to improve communication between experts and the public by bridging gaps in perspective-taking and making technical information more usable for sound decision-making. Her research spans domains including public health, medicine, and computer science, focusing on translating subject-matter expertise into accessible guidance through behavioral interventions and improved user interfaces.

name: Christina Fong
title: Senior Research Scientist
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Christina Fong is a Senior Research Scientist whose research focuses on the role of fairness in redistributive attitudes and behavior, using experimental and behavioral economics methods. Her work examines how fairness, reciprocity, and altruism influence public giving, wealth redistribution, and charitable behavior.

name: Russell Golman
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Russell Golman is a professor who combines economics, psychology, and mathematics to develop theories about decision-making. His research focuses on belief-based utility, behavioral game theory, and how social actors gather information to innovate and generate novelty outside of equilibrium.

name: Cleotilde Gonzalez
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Cleotilde Gonzalez is a Professor and founding director of the Dynamic Decision Making Laboratory who studies how decision makers adapt and learn in dynamic environments. Her research combines experimental studies and computational cognitive modeling, with a focus on Instance-Based Learning Theory and applications across domains including cybersecurity, network science, and human-machine teaming.

name: Kevin Jarbo
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Kevin Jarbo is an assistant professor studying how stereotype threat influences students' attempts to avoid negative interpersonal experiences in academic settings. His research integrates social psychology with cognitive neuroscience to understand decision-making behavior, identity, and educational outcomes.

name: George Loewenstein
title: Herbert A. Simon University Professor of Economics and Psychology
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: George Loewenstein is a leading researcher who helped found behavioral economics and neuroeconomics, focusing on applications of psychology to economics and vice versa. His work spans decision making, attention, emotions, intertemporal choice, and has informed public policy including libertarian paternalism approaches.

name: John Miller
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: John Miller's research focuses on understanding complex adaptive behavior that emerges in social systems through computational models of interacting artificial agents. He applies methodologies from economics, physics, and mathematics to analyze fundamental social phenomena including political platforms, cooperation, auction markets, and price formation.

name: Daniel Oppenheimer
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Professor Oppenheimer investigates how people determine what information to use when making decisions, what is worth learning, and how to search for needed information in a world of conflicting data. His research examines these fundamental questions and their real-world impacts on policy, business, and education.

name: Mark Patterson
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Mark Patterson conducts research in behavioral and applied economics with a special emphasis on data analysis. He is the Director of the Quantitative Social Science Scholars Program.

name: Silvia Saccardo
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Silvia studies topics at the intersection of economics and psychology using experimental methods, particularly focusing on how motivated cognition shapes ethical decision-making in areas like corruption, dishonesty, and discrimination. She also investigates how to structure incentive programs using behavioral economics insights to promote desirable behavior change.

name: Peter Schwardmann
title: Associate Professor and Director of Undergraduate Studies
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Peter is a behavioral economist whose research focuses on why people hold biased beliefs about themselves and the world, and how these biases shape market outcomes and communication. He employs both laboratory and field experiments to investigate these phenomena.

name: Manasvini Singh
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Manasvini Singh's research lies at the intersection of decision theory and health policy, with a focus on how physicians make decisions. She examines the effects of organizational and policy environments on medical decision-making and its implications for patient welfare.

name: Siva Balakrishnan
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Siva Balakrishnan is a Professor of Statistics & Data Science broadly interested in statistical machine learning, with recent focus on understanding algorithms for non-convex estimation problems. His research areas span causal inference, optimal transport, optimization, and the theoretical foundations of statistical inference.

name: Zach Branson
title: Associate Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Zach Branson is an Associate Teaching Professor of Statistics and Data Science at Carnegie Mellon University whose research focuses on experimental design and causal inference, particularly on theoretical and methodological questions about whether treatments cause changes in outcomes. His teaching interests include communicating causality and design topics to undergraduates and non-statistics audiences, as well as statistical communications through writing, presentations, and visualizations.

name: Bernie Devlin
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Bernie Devlin is a professor of statistics with over 23 years of experience in human genetics and 15 years in psychiatric genetics. His research focuses on developing statistical methods for analyzing genetic data and applying these methods to discover genetic bases of diseases including autism, schizophrenia, and neurodegenerative disorders.

name: Peter Freeman
title: Associate Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Peter Freeman is an astrostatistician who develops and applies advanced data analysis methods to complex, high-dimensional astronomical datasets. His work spans source detection algorithms, cosmic microwave background mapping, photometric redshift estimation, and galaxy morphology quantification, with extensive experience from the Chandra X-ray Telescope mission.

name: Shannon Gallagher
title: Lecturer - Special Faculty
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences

name: Chris Genovese
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Professor Genovese is a statistician specializing in high-dimensional and nonparametric inference with applications to complex scientific questions in cosmology, neuroscience, and evolutionary biology. His research addresses confidence sets for nonparametric inference, adaptive function estimation, and multiple testing, with applied projects in learning analytics and maternal health prediction.

name: Joel Greenhouse
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Joel Greenhouse is a Professor of Statistics at Carnegie Mellon University with expertise in developing statistical methods for observational studies, clinical trials, and meta-analysis. His recent work focuses on combining information from different study designs, and he has extensive experience serving on data and safety monitoring boards and scientific advisory committees for NIH and VA studies.

name: Amelia Haviland
title: E.J. Barone Professor of Health Systems Management
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Dr. Haviland's research focuses on causal analysis with observational data and analysis of longitudinal and complex survey data applied to policy issues in health and criminology. Her work includes assessing effects of health insurance plans on health care costs and disparities, mechanisms for health disparities in Medicare recipients, and extending statistical methods like group-based trajectory modeling to address causal questions in criminology.

name: Jiashun Jin
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Jiashun Jin specializes in statistical inference for Big Data, particularly in the challenging regime where signals are rare and weak, requiring novel methods beyond conventional approaches. His research spans large-scale testing, classification, clustering, variable selection, network analysis, and applications in genomics, social networks, and neuroscience.

name: Edward Kennedy
title: Associate Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Edward Kennedy's research focuses on causal inference, missing data, functional estimation, and machine learning, particularly in high-dimensional and complex data settings. He is especially interested in applications to criminal justice, health services, medicine, and public policy.

name: Spencer Koerner
title: Lecturer - Special Faculty
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Spencer Koerner is a Lecturer in Statistics & Data Science whose research focuses on diverse neuroscientific applications, particularly estimating low-dimensional latent dynamical systems and their manifestation in neural correlation structures. His work emphasizes neural synchrony estimation in large sparse networks and point process models of neural spiking.

name: Arun Kumar Kuchibhotla
title: Associate Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Arun Kumar Kuchibhotla is an Associate Professor whose research spans causal inference, computational finance, and the theoretical foundations of statistical inference. His work focuses on developing methods for high-dimensional statistics, nonparametric approaches, and optimization algorithms with applications to machine learning and time series forecasting.

name: Mikael Kuusela
title: Associate Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Mikael Kuusela is an Associate Professor of Statistics and Data Science whose research focuses on statistical machine learning, time series forecasting, and optimization algorithms. His work includes the STAMPS project and addresses fundamental challenges in applied statistical methodology.

name: Jing Lei
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Jing Lei is a Professor of Statistics whose research focuses on statistical theory and methodology, including analyzing properties of machine learning algorithms and developing new methods for high-dimensional data. His work spans dimension reduction, regression, clustering, and applied problems in astronomy and genetics using techniques like nonparametric smoothing and network estimation.

name: Jamie McGovern
title: Special Faculty, Director of the Master of Science in Applied Data Science Program
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Jamie McGovern is the Director of the Master of Science in Applied Data Science Program at Carnegie Mellon University. They lead efforts in advancing applied data science education and research within the Statistics & Data Science department.

name: Gonzalo Mena
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Gonzalo Mena develops statistical and machine learning methods for life sciences problems, with experience in neuroscience and epidemiology. He works on extracting signals from cutting-edge datasets and drawing valid inferences from large-scale, biased data, while also studying optimal transport theory.

name: Nynke Niezink
title: Associate Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Nynke Niezink is an Associate Professor specializing in statistical inference for social network data, including longitudinal modeling, multiplex networks, and relational event models. Her research applies these methodologies to social sciences and humanities data with applications in education, health sciences, organizational behavior, sociology, and criminology.

name: Rebecca Nugent
title: Fienberg University Professor of Statistics & Data Science
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Rebecca Nugent is the Stephen E. and Joyce Fienberg University Professor and Department Head of Statistics & Data Science at Carnegie Mellon. Her research focuses on clustering and classification methodology for high-dimensional data, record linkage applications, and the development of data analysis platforms for adaptive instruction and data science education.

name: Ankit Pensia
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Ankit Pensia is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Statistics and Data Science at Carnegie Mellon University. His research interests include robust and heavy-tailed statistics, inference under constraints, and machine learning with high-dimensional data.

name: Alex Reinhart
title: Associate Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Alex Reinhart is an associate teaching professor in Statistics & Data Science at Carnegie Mellon University who specializes in statistical pedagogy and educational research. His work focuses on spatio-temporal data analysis, anomaly detection, and improving student understanding of statistical concepts through innovative teaching methods.

name: Kathryn Roeder
title: UPMC University Professor of Statistics and Life Sciences
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Kathryn Roeder develops statistical methods for analyzing genetic and genomic data to identify associations between genetic variation patterns and complex diseases like autism and schizophrenia. Her group utilizes high-dimensional statistics, machine learning, and network methods, and has identified over one hundred autism risk genes while developing tools for single-cell multi-omic and proteomic data analysis.

name: Chad Schafer
title: Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Chad Schafer is a Professor of Statistics at Carnegie Mellon University who develops statistical methods for complex models and high-dimensional data in cosmology and astronomy. His research focuses on rigorous inference for large astronomical surveys like SDSS and LSST, where he tailors methods to handle theoretical models and noisy observational data.

name: Yandi Shen
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Yandi Shen is an Assistant Professor in Statistics & Data Science at Carnegie Mellon University with research interests in nonparametric and semiparametric statistics, high-dimensional inference, and applied probability. Her work spans causal inference, statistical machine learning, and foundational topics in high-dimensional statistical theory.

name: Weijing Tang
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Weijing Tang develops statistical methodology and theory for network analysis, machine learning, and survival analysis with applications to health and social sciences. Her research is motivated by challenges in analyzing massive and complex datasets for interdisciplinary research.

name: Will Townes
title: Assistant Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Will Townes is an applied statistician working primarily in biomedical and public health domains, focusing on wastewater-based epidemiology, wearable devices, and infectious disease tracking and forecasting as part of the Delphi research group. He has developed normalization and feature selection methods for single cell RNA-Seq and spatial transcriptomics, with interests spanning 'omics' collaboration, metagenomics, and computational scalability through approximate inference techniques.

name: Isabella Verdinelli
title: Professor in Residence
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Isabella Verdinelli is a Professor in Residence at Carnegie Mellon's Statistics & Data Science department who specializes in identifying and estimating hidden low-dimensional structures in high-dimensional datasets, including clusters, filaments, and hyperplanes. Her research interests span Bayesian experimental design, nonparametric inference, hypothesis testing, manifold theory, and convergence rate analysis for complex data structures.

name: Larry Wasserman
title: UPMC University Professor
department: Department of Statistics and Machine Learning Department
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Larry Wasserman is a professor of Statistics and Machine Learning at Carnegie Mellon University whose research focuses on nonparametric inference, asymptotic theory, causality, and applications to astrophysics, bioinformatics, and genetics. He is an elected fellow of the American Statistical Association, Institute of Mathematical Statistics, and the American Association for the Advancement of Science, and has authored two advanced statistics textbooks.

name: Gordon Weinberg
title: Lecturer - Special Faculty
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Gordon Weinberg is an instructor in the Department of Statistics at Carnegie Mellon University with a lifelong passion for teaching. His research focuses on statistical pedagogy and educational research, with interests in how students learn statistics and mathematics most effectively.

name: Ron Yurko
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Ron Yurko is an Assistant Teaching Professor who develops methods at the interface of inference and machine learning, with applications to sports analytics and natural language processing. He directs the Carnegie Mellon Sports Analytics Center and the department's Summer Undergraduate Research Experience program.

name: Freida Abtan
title: Associate Professor
bio: Freida Abtan is a Canadian audiovisual composer and multi-disciplinary artist specializing in immersive media who works with fixed and real-time computational technologies in sound and video for concert, installation, and large-scale multimedia performance. Her compositions range from acousmatic work to industrial and pop-influenced experimental performance, and have been featured at major festivals including ICMC, Mutek, and Elektra Festival.

name: Christopher Allen
title: Artist Lecturer in Percussion
bio: Christopher Allen is an Artist Lecturer in Percussion at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Music, where he teaches and mentors percussion students. His students have gone on to secure positions with major orchestras including the Oregon Symphony, National Symphony Orchestra, and Baltimore Symphony Orchestra.

name: Alberto Almarza
title: Associate Professor of Flute
bio: Alberto Almarza is a virtuoso flutist known for his versatile approach to both contemporary and classical repertoire, including period instruments. As a leading proponent of new music for flute, he has commissioned and premiered numerous works and mentors students who have achieved positions in major orchestras worldwide.

name: Donna Amato
title: Artist Lecturer in Piano, Staff Pianist
bio: Donna Amato is an accomplished concert pianist and staff pianist at Carnegie Mellon University with extensive international performance experience in classical music across Europe, North America, and Russia. She specializes in contemporary and romantic-era piano works, has made numerous recordings, and is particularly known for her extensive work promoting and performing the compositions of Kaikhosru Sorabji.

name: Jennifer Aylmer
title: Associate Professor of Voice
bio: Jennifer Aylmer is an accomplished American soprano known for her beautiful voice, compelling stage portrayals, and impeccable musicianship across opera, oratorio, and recital repertoire. She is particularly noted for her work in newly composed American music and serves as Chair of the Voice Division at Carnegie Mellon University.

name: Leonardo Balada
title: University Professor Emeritus of Composition
bio: Leonardo Balada is a Spanish composer whose works have been performed by leading orchestras worldwide and commissioned by major festivals and orchestras. His compositions span orchestral works, operas, and chamber music, with numerous recordings released by Naxos Records.

name: Scott Bell
title: Artist Lecturer in Oboe
bio: Scott Bell is an Artist Lecturer in Oboe at Carnegie Mellon with extensive professional orchestral experience, including a 28-year career with the Pittsburgh Symphony and performances with major American orchestras. He is dedicated to music education through teaching, master classes, and mentoring the next generation of oboists.

name: Neal Berntsen
title: Artist Lecturer in Trumpet
bio: Neal Berntsen is Chair of the Brass Division at Carnegie Mellon's School of Music and a principal trumpet with the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra since 1997. He is an accomplished soloist, chamber musician, and educator who has performed with major orchestras worldwide and published extensively on trumpet pedagogy and performance techniques.

name: Taylor Boyle
title: Ensemble Coordinator & Librarian
bio: Taylor Boyle is an Ensemble Coordinator and Librarian at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Music, bringing expertise in arts administration developed since 2013. He holds degrees in both architecture and oboe performance from Kent State University and has collaborated with major institutions including The Cleveland Orchestra and Cleveland Institute of Music.

name: Jeremy Branson
title: Artist Lecturer in Percussion
bio: Jeremy Branson is the Associate Principal Percussionist of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra and Chair of the Percussion Department at Carnegie Mellon University. He has performed with leading orchestras and conductors worldwide, and is dedicated to training the next generation of professional percussionists.

name: Navilla Burns
title: Recruitment & Academic Coordinator
bio: Navilla Burns is a musician and arts administration professional with over a decade of experience supporting prospective and current students within the School of Music. She is deeply committed to providing tangible resources to the next generation of music professionals using an intersectional approach, and serves as Co-founder and Director of Operations of the Chromatic Brass Collective.

name: William Caballero
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Horn
bio: William Caballero is the Principal Horn of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra and an Associate Teaching Professor of Horn at Carnegie Mellon University, with over 30 years of experience in leading orchestral horn sections. He is an accomplished soloist and chamber musician who has performed with major orchestras and collaborated with renowned musicians worldwide, while also developing innovative online horn teaching curricula.

name: Andrew Carlisle
title: Artist Lecturer in Bagpipe
bio: Andrew Carlisle is a world-leading solo bagpiper and Director of Piping at Carnegie Mellon University, having won numerous prestigious international piping competitions across four continents. Since joining CMU in 2010, he has transformed the university's Pipes and Drums into one of the leading pipe bands in the United States while serving as Artistic Director for the Virginia International Tattoo.

name: Mark Carver
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Collaborative Piano
bio: Mark Carver is an accomplished pianist specializing in collaborative piano and chamber music accompaniment at Carnegie Mellon University. He has extensive performance experience as a guest artist and recitalist, with particular recognition for his work accompanying ensembles like the Pittsburgh Camerata.

name: Rebecca Cherian
title: Artist Lecturer in Trombone
bio: Rebecca Cherian is Co-Principal Trombone of the Pittsburgh Symphony and a founding board member of the International Women's Brass Conference. She is an accomplished soloist and educator with extensive experience as orchestral principal and university faculty instructor.

name: Sara Chiesa
title: Special Faculty in Vocal Coaching & Collaborative Piano
bio: Sara Chiesa is a pianist, vocal coach, and music director passionate about redefining leadership in classical music through education and mentorship. She maintains a coaching studio, teaches graduate diction and opera workshop courses at CMU, and regularly serves as head coach for School of Music productions.

name: Frederic Chiu
title: Associate Professor of Piano
bio: Frederic Chiu is an accomplished concert pianist who performs internationally and collaborates across classical and non-classical genres, including work with orchestras and diverse artistic partners. He is also an educator who has developed and taught Deeper Piano Studies, a philosophic approach to piano pedagogy, at major conservatories and universities worldwide.

name: Denis Colwell
title: Associate Professor of Music
bio: Denis Colwell is an Associate Professor of Music at Carnegie Mellon who served as Head of the School of Music from 2012 to 2022 and was Music Director of the Carnegie Mellon Wind Ensemble from 1993 to 2011. He is an accomplished conductor and cornetist with extensive experience in brass ensemble direction, having led the River City Brass Band and founded youth brass programs.

name: Marianne Cornetti
title: Artist Lecturer in Voice
bio: Marianne Cornetti is an internationally recognized mezzo-soprano specializing in Verdi opera roles, having performed leading parts such as Azucena, Amneris, and Princess Eboli at prestigious opera houses worldwide. As an Artist Lecturer in Voice at Carnegie Mellon's School of Music, she combines her acclaimed performance career with teaching and mentoring the next generation of singers.

name: Daniel Nesta Curtis
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Daniel Nesta Curtis is a conductor and educator noted for his adventurous programming, interdisciplinary work, and advocacy for underrepresented composers. He has premiered over 200 compositions by CMU students and faculty while serving as Resident Conductor and leading various ensembles at Carnegie Mellon.

name: Andrés Cárdenes
title: University Professor Emeritus of Violin
bio: Grammy-nominated Andrés Cárdenes is a world-renowned violinist, conductor, and chamber musician recognized for his versatile career spanning solo performances, conducting major orchestras, and championing contemporary composers. With a 45-year teaching career, he has mentored generations of musicians and serves as a jury president for prestigious international violin competitions.

name: Roger B. Dannenberg
title: Professor Emeritus of Computer Science, Art & Music
bio: Roger Dannenberg is a Professor Emeritus internationally known for his research in computer music and for creating Audacity, a widely used audio editor. His current work focuses on live music performance with artificial computer musicians, interactive media, high-level languages for sound synthesis, and automatic composition.

name: Adrianne Davis Kelly
title: Artist Lecturer in Music Education
bio: Adrianne Davis Kelly is an Artist Lecturer in Music Education with over 35 years of teaching experience, including 33 years in Pittsburgh Public Schools where she taught general music, chorus, and instrumental music. She is an advocate for urban education, has presented workshops on urban music leadership, and currently supervises student teachers at Duquesne University.

name: Michele de la Reza
title: Teaching Professor of Dance
bio: Michele de la Reza is a Teaching Professor of Dance at CMU and Co-Founder/Co-Artistic Director of Attack Theatre, which fuses modern dance, original live music, and interdisciplinary art forms. Her work has toured internationally and she has provided extensive choreography and performance for Pittsburgh Opera, orchestras, theater companies, and museums over two decades.

name: Cynthia Koledo DeAlmeida
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Oboe
bio: Cynthia Koledo DeAlmeida is the principal oboe of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra since 1991 and an Associate Teaching Professor at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Music. She is an accomplished soloist and chamber musician who has recorded multiple solo CDs and commissioned works by contemporary composers.

name: Jeff Dee
title: Artist Lecturer in Bass Trombone
bio: Jeff Dee is an Artist Lecturer in Bass Trombone at Carnegie Mellon University and principal bass trombone with the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra. He is an accomplished orchestral and chamber musician with extensive performance and teaching experience across major orchestras and educational institutions.

name: Hiram Diaz
title: Artist Lecturer in Euphonium
bio: Hiram Diaz is an accomplished euphoniumist, trombonist, vocalist, and music educator who has won major international euphonium competitions and performed as a soloist with prestigious orchestras and ensembles across the United States and abroad. He currently serves as a euphoniumist with The President's Own United States Marine Band since 2012 and directs the Tartan Tuba Band at Carnegie Mellon.

name: Thomas W. Douglas
title: Professor of Music
bio: Thomas W. Douglas is a compelling and passionate conductor who has directed more than 200 orchestral, musical theater, opera and oratorio productions. He serves as Music Director of the Newton Symphony in Kansas and Artistic Director of the Bach Choir of Pittsburgh, known for innovative and dramatically provocative productions.

name: Jocelyn Dueck
title: Associate Professor of Collaborative Piano
bio: Jocelyn Dueck is an Associate Professor of Collaborative Piano known for her interpretations of new music and extensive experience premiering and commissioning works by contemporary composers. She is a subject matter expert in prosody and auditory coding with over thirty years of experience coaching singers and pianists in multiple languages and dialects.

name: Paul Evans
title: Artist Lecturer in Percussion
bio: Paul Evans is an Artist Lecturer in Percussion at Carnegie Mellon University with over twenty years of teaching experience, during which his students have secured positions in major orchestras, Broadway productions, and touring ensembles. He is also a founding member of River City Brass, America's only full-time professional brass band, and performs regularly on PBS productions.

name: Dan Fernandez
title: Director of Marketing & Communications
bio: Dan Fernandez is the Director of Marketing & Communications for the School of Music at Carnegie Mellon University. With a background in psychology and 10 years of arts administration experience, he previously worked in marketing and sales for the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra and is a founder and managing director of Three Rivers Ringers, Pittsburgh's premier handbell ensemble.

name: Donna S. Fox
title: Artist Lecturer in Music Education
bio: Donna Stark Fox is an Artist Lecturer in Music Education with extensive experience as an Orchestra Director and music educator. She has served leadership roles in professional music education organizations and currently supervises student teachers in music education.

name: Nancy Galbraith
title: Professor
bio: Nancy Galbraith is Professor and Head of Composition at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Music, holding the Vira I. Heinz Professorship. Her compositions for instrumental and choral ensembles are performed worldwide and are praised for their rich harmonic texture, rhythmic vitality, and emotional depth.

name: Kate Ganczarz
title: Senior Academic Advisor
bio: Kate Ganczarz is the Senior Academic Advisor for the School of Music at Carnegie Mellon University, with expertise in higher education administration and music education. She holds degrees in music education and higher education administration, and has studied at the Kodály Institute in Hungary.

name: Ross Garin
title: Associate Head
bio: Ross Garin is the Associate Head of the Carnegie Mellon University School of Music, where he has been on staff since 2001. He is an accomplished trombonist who has performed with numerous orchestras, opera companies, and renowned artists.

name: Paul Gerlach
title: Artist Lecturer in Music Education
bio: Paul Gerlach is an Artist Lecturer in Music Education at Carnegie Mellon University who has led the Kiltie Band for nearly four decades and brings extensive experience in music instruction across multiple instruments and ensemble types. With advanced degrees in applied music, music education, and musicology, combined with 32 years of public school teaching experience, he specializes in brass, woodwind, and percussion techniques as well as conducting and music adjudication.

name: James Gorton
title: Artist Lecturer in Chamber Music
bio: James Gorton is an Artist Lecturer in Chamber Music and former Co-Principal Oboist of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra, where he performed from 1971 to 2012. He is an accomplished soloist and chamber musician who has premiered numerous works and taught students who now hold positions in orchestras and universities throughout the U.S. and abroad.

name: Renée Govanucci
title: Senior Administrative Assistant
bio: Renée Govanucci is the Sr. Administrative Assistant to the Head of the School of Music with over 30 years of experience in non-profit performing arts and venue management. She is dedicated to music education and mentoring aspiring arts administrators.

name: Sari Gruber
title: Assistant Teaching Professor of Voice
bio: Sari Gruber is an accomplished soprano who has performed leading operatic and concert roles at major international opera houses and recital venues including Lincoln Center and Carnegie Hall. She teaches voice and performance at Carnegie Mellon University and Point Park University, with a focus on classical singing and musical theater vocal technique.

name: Peter Guild
title: Artist Lecturer in Double Bass
bio: Peter Guild is an Artist Lecturer in Double Bass at Carnegie Mellon University and has been a member of the Pittsburgh Symphony's double bass section since 1999. He teaches double bass orchestral repertoire and enjoys composing and arranging music across multiple genres.

name: David Harding
title: Professor of Viola and Chamber Music
bio: David Harding is a renowned viola performer and chamber musician with an extensive international career, having performed at prestigious venues and festivals worldwide and collaborated with leading ensembles. As a devoted educator and Professor of Viola and Chamber Music at Carnegie Mellon University, he is deeply committed to helping the next generation of musicians through teaching and mentorship.

name: Julia Harguindey
title: Artist Lecturer in Bassoon
bio: Argentinian-born bassoonist Julia Harguindey is Principal Bassoon with the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra and a faculty member at Carnegie Mellon University beginning in 2025–26. She has performed with major orchestras and chamber ensembles including the Saint Paul Chamber Orchestra and Santa Fe Chamber Music Festival.

name: Kristin Heath
title: Music & Catalog Librarian
bio: Kristin Heath is the Music & Catalog Librarian at Carnegie Mellon University, responsible for music reference services, cataloging scores, and building collections for the School of Music. She teaches Music Research Methods and provides instruction in music information literacy and research skills.

name: Melanie Holcomb
title: Opera Production Manager
bio: Melanie Holcomb is the Production Manager for Opera Studies at CMU School of Music since 2018, bringing extensive experience in stage and road management for professional theatre, opera, and ballet companies. She holds a degree in Stage Management from the California Institute of the Arts and has worked across multiple university arts programs and professional venues.

name: James Houlik
title: Artist Lecturer in Saxophone
bio: James Houlik is an internationally acclaimed saxophonist and teacher who has championed the development of the saxophone through performances and the commissioning of over 100 new works by prominent composers. His pedagogical approach has produced students who achieve professional success across all facets of music performance and teaching.

name: Micah Howard
title: Assistant Teaching Professor of Double Bass
bio: Micah Howard is an accomplished double bass performer and educator who has been a member of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra since 1996, performing as a soloist and chamber musician across five continents. He is dedicated to music education, serving as Artist Lecturer in Double Bass at Carnegie Mellon while also teaching at multiple universities and coaching youth orchestras.

name: Jack Howell
title: Artist Lecturer in Clarinet
bio: Jack Howell is Principal Bass Clarinet of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra with extensive experience as a soloist and chamber musician across major orchestras and festivals. He is an accomplished performer and educator specializing in clarinet performance and interpretation.

name: Annie Hui-Hsin Hsieh
title: Associate Professor of Music Theory
bio: Annie Hui-Hsin Hsieh is a Taiwanese-Australian composer working in acoustic and electroacoustic mediums who focuses on creating visceral physical experiences and articulating sonic expressions in terms of spatial gestures and relational sociality. Her work has been presented internationally at major festivals and commissioned by leading orchestras and ensembles worldwide.

name: Roseanna Irwin
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Coaching and Accompanying
bio: Roseanna Irwin is an Associate Teaching Professor specializing in vocal coaching and accompanying at Carnegie Mellon's School of Music, with extensive experience in music education and performance. She teaches vocal coaching, voice, piano, and music theater literature while maintaining a private studio and mentoring the next generation of performers.

name: John Paul Ito
title: Associate Professor of Music Theory
bio: John Paul Ito is an Associate Professor of Music Theory whose main research focuses on meter, hypermeter, and their connections with cognition, performance, and the history of musical style. He published the book Focal Impulse Theory: Musical Expression, Meter, and the Body in 2021 and has contributed to major music theory and musicology journals.

name: Paul Johnston
title: Artist Lecturer in Musicology
bio: Paul Johnston is an award-winning classical music broadcaster with 25 years of experience in public radio, having produced content for NPR, American Public Media, and other major networks. He teaches musicology at Carnegie Mellon while serving as an ordained Episcopal priest and exploring the interface between creativity and spirituality.

name: Kenneth Keeling
title: Professor Emeritus of Music
bio: Dr. Keeling is a distinguished music educator and performer with a long career in higher education, clarinet studio teaching, musicology, and conducting of instrumental ensembles. He has headed music schools at several universities and maintains an active teaching practice in music history and clarinet studio instruction.

name: Sung-Im Kim
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Sung-Im Kim is an Associate Teaching Professor of Collaborative Piano at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Music, where she teaches collaborative piano and chamber music literature while mentoring students across the department. An experienced chamber musician and collaborative artist, she has performed at prestigious venues internationally and contributed to recording projects including the world premiere of Nancy Galbraith's Piano Concerto No. 3.

name: Craig Knox
title: Artist Lecturer in Tuba
bio: Craig Knox is the Principal Tuba of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra since 2005 and an Artist Lecturer in Tuba at Carnegie Mellon University. He is an accomplished soloist, chamber musician, and educator who has performed internationally and premiered works by major composers including Jennifer Higdon and Andre Previn.

name: Peter Kope
title: Teaching Professor of Dance
bio: Peter Kope is the founder and artistic director of Attack Theatre, a company in residence at Carnegie Mellon's School of Music that integrates dance, live music, and video. His work spans international festivals and cross-disciplinary collaborations, combining dance with visual art, installation, and music composition.

name: Stephen Kostyniak
title: Artist Lecturer in French Horn
bio: Stephen Kostyniak is an Artist Lecturer in French Horn at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Music. He specializes in French horn performance and instruction.

name: Jason Kush
title: Artist Lecturer in Saxophone
bio: Dr. Jason Kush is an accomplished saxophonist, educator, and scholar with extensive performance experience across jazz, classical, and commercial genres, including collaborations with the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra and numerous renowned artists. His scholarly work focuses on saxophone history and pedagogy, and he founded the Three New Music Consortium to promote newly composed music.

name: Carla LaRocca
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Carla LaRocca is an Associate Teaching Professor of Keyboard Studies at Carnegie Mellon University who has taught nearly every music major since joining the faculty in 1989. She is an accomplished pianist and educator who also serves as Director of Beginning Piano in the Carnegie Mellon Preparatory School and was awarded the prestigious Henry Hornbostel Teaching Award in 2020.

name: Milton Rubén Laufer
title: Professor
bio: Dr. Milton Rubén Laufer is the Jack G. Buncher Chair and Head of the School of Music at Carnegie Mellon University, serving as an internationally recognized interpreter and scholar of Spanish piano music. He is a committed educator who emphasizes developing musicians as skilled performers, expressive artists, and adaptable entrepreneurial thinkers prepared to thrive in evolving artistic landscapes.

name: Victoria Luperi
title: Artist Lecturer in Clarinet
bio: Victoria Luperi is an Argentine-American clarinetist serving as Associate Principal Clarinet of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra and Artist Lecturer in Clarinet at Carnegie Mellon University. She is an accomplished soloist and chamber musician who has performed at renowned festivals and premiered multiple compositions written for her.

name: Luz Manríquez
title: Teaching Professor of Collaborative Piano and Piano Pedagogy
bio: Luz Manríquez is a distinguished pianist and pedagogue who serves as Director of Piano Division at Carnegie Mellon's Preparatory School and teaches collaborative piano. Her career spans performance, teaching, and festival direction, with a focus on chamber music and piano pedagogy across the United States, Latin America, and Europe.

name: John Marcinizyn
title: Artist Lecturer in Guitar
bio: Dr. John Marcinizyn is a composer and guitarist whose works span guitar concertos, string quartets, orchestral pieces, and chamber music that have received numerous performances and critical acclaim. He is an Artist Lecturer in Guitar at Carnegie Mellon and performs frequently as a soloist and ensemble member while teaching composition and guitar at multiple institutions.

name: Alex Marthaler
title: Artist Lecturer in Music Theory
bio: Alex Marthaler is a composer, pianist, and educator whose compositions are inspired by physical spaces and combine harmonic and rhythmic elements from music around the world. He teaches music theory and composition at Carnegie Mellon University and directs the Music Pre-College Program.

name: Anne Martindale Williams
title: Artist Lecturer in Cello
bio: Anne Martindale Williams is the principal cellist of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra since 1979 and an Artist Lecturer in Cello at Carnegie Mellon University. She is an accomplished soloist and chamber musician who has performed with renowned guest artists and at prestigious music festivals throughout America and Europe.

name: David McCarroll
title: Artist Lecturer in Violin
bio: David McCarroll is an accomplished violinist and concertmaster of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra, known for his mature musicality and deep understanding of classical repertoire. He is an active performer, chamber musician, and teacher at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Music.

name: Tatjana Mead Chamis
title: Artist Lecturer in Viola
bio: Violist Tatjana Mead Chamis is a principal violist, chamber musician, soloist, and Latin Grammy-nominated recording artist who advocates for underheard or suppressed music and experiments with new music. She founded the Clarion Quartet dedicated to performing works by forgotten composers and has conducted research on Brazilian viola compositions.

name: Monique Mead
title: Teaching Professor
department: Music Education, Entrepreneurship, and Musicianship Division
bio: As Director of Music Entrepreneurship at Carnegie Mellon School of Music, Monique Mead helps students prepare for professional life and create innovative career paths in music through practical curriculum. Her work spans classical music performance, education, community engagement, and sound healing practices designed to create transformative musical experiences.

name: Billie Miller
title: Artist Lecturer in Vocal Coaching and Collaborative Piano
bio: Billie Miller is an accomplished collaborative pianist and vocal coach specializing in opera and chamber music performance. She brings dynamic musicality to her work as a performer and instructor, with extensive experience in vocal coaching, opera accompaniment, and collaborative ensemble work.

name: Jesse Montgomery
title: Director of Operations
bio: Jesse Montgomery is the Director of Operations at the Carnegie Mellon University School of Music since September 2023. He brings over a decade of experience in artistic planning from the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra.

name: Anne Jackovic Moskal
title: Assistant Teaching Professor of Solfege
bio: Anne Jackovic Moskal is a professional violinist and orchestral musician who has performed with multiple regional orchestras including the Pittsburgh Opera Orchestra and Erie Philharmonic. She teaches solfege and violin at Carnegie Mellon University and throughout the Pittsburgh community, with expertise in music theory instruction and string performance.

name: Stephen Neely
title: Associate Professor of Music
bio: Stephen Neely is an Associate Professor of Music and Director of Graduate Studies at Carnegie Mellon, specializing in Dalcroze Eurhythmics and music pedagogy. His research focuses on the physical nature of experience and how musical gestures reflect in everyday interactions, exploring the relationship between the body, time, and experience in both musical and design contexts.

name: Rodrigo Ojeda
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Collaborative Piano
bio: Rodrigo Ojeda is an Associate Teaching Professor of Collaborative Piano at Carnegie Mellon University with extensive performance experience in solo recitals, concerto performances, and chamber music across multiple countries. He is a versatile pianist specializing in classical and contemporary repertoire, having performed works by composers including Tchaikovsky, Brahms, Gershwin, and Prokofiev.

name: Jeremy Olisar
title: Artist Lecturer in Music Education
bio: Jeremy Olisar is an Artist Lecturer in Music Education at Carnegie Mellon University since 2021, teaching music education principles, guided teaching, and directing the Kiltie Band. He has extensive experience teaching music across K-12 and college levels in concert band, jazz, marching band, and chamber ensembles, and holds a Master of Arts in band conducting from the American Band College.

name: Natalie Ozeas
title: Professor Emerita of Music Education
bio: Dr. Natalie Ozeas is a Professor Emerita of Music Education at Carnegie Mellon with over 20 years of teaching experience in preschool through high school settings. Her research and leadership focus on music education, choral conducting, Dalcroze Eurhythmics, and urban music education initiatives.

name: Dimitri Papadimitriou
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Chamber Music / Piano
bio: Dimitri Papadimitriou is an accomplished pianist and chamber music advocate who has performed with leading orchestral musicians and soloists. As Associate Teaching Professor at Carnegie Mellon, he founded and directs the Carnegie Mellon Chamber Series and holds a doctorate in music performance with research focused on Beethoven's harmonic choices.

name: Angela Park
title: Artist Lecturer in Cello
bio: Angela Park is an accomplished cellist equally at home as a soloist and chamber musician, having performed throughout the Americas, Europe, and East Asia with prestigious orchestras and at major festivals. She is increasingly in demand as a baroque cellist and maintains a deep interest in contemporary music, having participated in dozens of world premieres.

name: Mary Ellen Poole
title: Professor of Music
bio: Mary Ellen Poole is a musicologist whose research focuses on fin-de-siècle Paris, including cabaret chansons and music as political propaganda during the Radical Republic. As the Stanley and Marcia Gumberg Dean of the College of Fine Arts at Carnegie Mellon, she leads efforts to promote diversity, equity, and inclusion while expanding performance opportunities for student ensembles.

name: Ryan M. Prendergast
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Dr. Prendergast is an Assistant Teaching Professor of Musicology who teaches courses in music history, opera history, dramaturgy, and musical theater history. His research focuses on opera production and its history, musical theater, German theater, and opera recording, with particular emphasis on the theatrical collaborators of Richard Strauss.

name: Katherine Pukinskis
title: Assistant Professor of Music
bio: Katherine Pukinskis is a composer-scholar whose work explores storytelling and voice through unlikely textual and conceptual juxtapositions in the concert hall. Her scholarly research focuses on cultural identity, diaspora, traditional folk and choral music, and activism, with particular emphasis on Latvian choral music and 20th-century American Art Song.

name: Susan Raponi
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Music
bio: Dr. Susan Raponi is an award-winning music educator and curriculum design specialist who developed Learning Differentiation Music Education Theory (LDMET) to address individual learning needs in inclusive classroom settings. As Division Chair of Music Education at Carnegie Mellon, she teaches graduate courses in pedagogy, research methods, and diversity and inclusion, while supervising pre-service teachers and conducting research on transformative music education in under-served school populations.

name: Brian Riordan
title: Artist Lecturer in Electronic Music
bio: Brian Riordan is a composer, performer, improviser, producer, and sound artist whose research focuses on temporal discontinuity, delay-based performance, real-time digital signal processing, and laptop performance aesthetics. He teaches programming environments in music and collaborates extensively across multiple musical genres and continents.

name: Vahan Sargsyan
title: Artist Lecturer
bio: Armenian pianist Vahan Sargsyan is an alumnus of the Komitas State Conservatory in Yerevan and a laureate of the International Piano Competition in Rio-de-Janeiro. He works as an Artist Lecturer in collaborative piano and chamber music at Carnegie Mellon University, with extensive performance experience as a soloist and chamber musician across multiple continents.

name: Rizky Sasono
title: Artist Lecturer in Ethnomusicology
bio: Rizky Sasono is an ethnomusicologist and popular music scholar whose research interests include popular music, politics, arts, and sound studies, with particular focus on Southeast Asian contexts. He is also a renowned indie-rock and experimental musician and member of the award-winning Indonesian avant-garde theater group Teater Garasi.

name: Sergey Schepkin
title: Professor of Piano
bio: Sergey Schepkin is a renowned concert pianist and Bach specialist who has performed worldwide and recorded extensively, particularly focusing on Bach's keyboard works on the modern piano informed by historical performance practice. He is a passionate educator, sought-after master class presenter, and advocate for new music who has collaborated with numerous contemporary composers.

name: Stephen Schultz
title: Teaching Professor Emeritus of Music History
bio: Stephen Schultz is an acclaimed Baroque flute virtuoso and music history educator who has performed as principal and solo flute with leading early music ensembles including Philharmonia Baroque Orchestra and Musica Angelica. He founded American Baroque in 1986, an ensemble dedicated to combining 18th-century music with new commissioned works, and has been instrumental in commissioning contemporary compositions for Baroque flute.

name: Riccardo Schulz
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Riccardo Schulz is a Teaching Professor in the School of Music at Carnegie Mellon, where he teaches Sound Recording and runs the recording operations with a special interest in recording, editing, and mastering classical music. He has recorded and produced over a hundred compact discs across multiple genres and record labels, and received a Latin GRAMMY Award in 2009 for his recording of Inca Dances.

name: Franco Sciannameo
title: CFA Distinguished Scholar & Teaching Professor of Music
bio: Franco Sciannameo is a violinist, musicologist, and cultural historian who writes and lectures extensively on contemporary music and its relation to politics, cinema, and the arts. He has collaborated with celebrated composers and authored numerous books on topics ranging from Nino Rota's film scores to Italian opera history and the works of Giacinto Scelsi.

name: Callum Smart
title: Assistant Professor of Violin
bio: Critically acclaimed violinist and pedagogue Callum Smart is an Assistant Professor of Violin at Carnegie Mellon University with an international reputation as a performer and innovator. He is recognized for his award-winning performances, educational initiatives including an AI practice companion called Caidence, and mission to make quality violin education accessible through digital platforms.

name: Rachael Smith
title: Director of Special Music Programs
bio: Rachael Smith is an arts administrator with extensive experience as a collaborator across disciplines, having worked in leadership roles at major performing arts institutions including San Francisco Opera and Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra. She has led community engagement and professional development initiatives in higher education, managing student career development programs and community performance initiatives that reached thousands of students annually.

name: Mark Snyder
title: Director of Jazz Orchestra
bio: Mark Snyder is the Director of the Jazz Ensemble at CMU and brings 20 years of experience as a music educator in Pittsburgh Public Schools. With 35 years of active performance as a freelance musician, he has performed with numerous orchestras and renowned artists while teaching music theory and directing ensembles.

name: Maria Spacagna
title: Professor of Voice
bio: Maria Spacagna is a Providence-born soprano who has performed as a guest artist at prestigious opera houses worldwide including the Metropolitan Opera, La Scala, and many others, becoming the first American-born artist to perform Madama Butterfly at La Scala. She has recorded critically acclaimed performances of major soprano roles and has performed over 40 roles in her career, with particular distinction in works by Puccini and Verdi.

name: Sarah Steranka
title: Artist Lecturer in Chamber Music
bio: Sarah Steranka is a flutist and chamber musician dedicated to exploring innovative work by living composers and advancing experimental music. She is a core member of the new music collective Kamraton and maintains a faculty position at Carnegie Mellon University while leading the Steranka Flute Studio.

name: Richard M. Stern
title: Professor
bio: Richard Stern is a Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering with a joint appointment in the School of Music, whose research spans sound, speech, hearing, and music with particular focus on music understanding, automated accompaniment, and music information retrieval. His work also encompasses spoken language systems, psychoacoustics, and binaural perception, with efforts directed toward making automatic speech processing more robust to environmental and acoustical changes.

name: Stephen Story
title: Associate Director of Wind Ensemble
bio: Stephen Story is the Associate Director of the Carnegie Mellon Wind Ensemble in his thirteenth year, conducting contemporary and classical works. He is a passionate music educator who teaches conducting, arranging, and solfege at Carnegie Mellon while advocating for the value of music education in both academic and public school settings.

name: Peter Stumpf
title: Piano Technician
bio: Peter Stumpf is a Registered Piano Technician with over 30 years of experience in piano technology and restoration. He oversees the maintenance of over 80 instruments at the CMU School of Music and runs Pianoburgh, a piano restoration facility in Pittsburgh.

name: Peter Sullivan
title: Artist Lecturer in Trombone
bio: Peter Sullivan is an Artist Lecturer in Trombone at Carnegie Mellon and Principal Trombone of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra, with extensive performance experience as a soloist and chamber musician across North America, Europe, and Asia. He teaches at Carnegie Mellon and Duquesne University and has been involved in instrument development with the Yamaha Corporation.

name: Thomas Sullivan
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Dr. Sullivan is a Teaching Professor in Electrical and Computer Engineering with a courtesy appointment in the School of Music, specializing in signal processing for audio and music applications. His research interests span audio signal processing, music synthesis, instrument tracking, and the application of digital signal processing techniques to music technology and recording systems.

name: Marilyn Taft Thomas
title: Professor Emerita of Music
bio: Marilyn Taft Thomas is Professor Emerita of Theory and Composition at Carnegie Mellon School of Music, an active composer with works across multiple ensembles who has won numerous prizes and awards. Her research focuses on computer music, music theory software development, and innovative teaching methods in music education.

name: Kristian Tchetchko
title: Lead Engineer & Coordinator of Recording Services
bio: Kristian is a recording engineer, sound designer, and audio consultant who leads recording services at CMU's School of Music, capturing and sharing music created by students, faculty, and guest artists. He partners with Pittsburgh performing arts organizations on audio recordings and live mixing, while also teaching and conducting synthesizer workshops.

name: Daniel Teadt
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Daniel Teadt is an internationally acclaimed baritone and voice teacher with over two decades of performance experience across opera, concert, and recital stages worldwide. He serves as Assistant Teaching Professor of Voice at Carnegie Mellon University, where he teaches vocal pedagogy and leads masterclasses while maintaining an active performance career.

name: Kelly Trumbull
title: Artist Lecturer in Acting
bio: Kelly Trumbull is a multi-disciplinary theater artist and educator committed to creating empowering, inclusive spaces for performing artists. She teaches at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Music and maintains an active career as an actor, director, and playwright with extensive credits in Pittsburgh and New York.

name: Reza Vali
title: Professor Emeritus of Composition
bio: Reza Vali is a composer and music theorist born in Iran who has been a faculty member at Carnegie Mellon University since 1988. His orchestral and chamber works have been performed internationally by prestigious ensembles including the Pittsburgh Symphony, Kronos Quartet, and Boston Modern Orchestra Project, and his music is recorded on multiple classical labels.

name: William van der Sloot
title: Associate Teaching Professor of Violin
bio: William van der Sloot is a heralded violinist and teacher whose students have earned acclaim throughout the world, with many holding prominent positions in major orchestras and music schools. A frequent judge at international competitions and regular guest artist for masterclasses, he was honored in 2015 as an honorary fellow of the Royal Conservatory of Music for his contributions to music education in Canada and abroad.

name: Gretchen Van Hoesen
title: Artist Lecturer in Harp
bio: Gretchen Van Hoesen is the Principal Harpist of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra since 1977 and an accomplished soloist who has performed with major conductors and orchestras internationally. She is a faculty member at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Music and has premiered numerous compositions while maintaining an active recital and chamber music career.

name: George Vosburgh
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: George Vosburgh is an accomplished conductor, soloist, and educator with a Grammy Award and extensive international performance experience, including 25 years as Principal Trumpet of the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra. He has taught at Carnegie Mellon University since 1992 and directs the Wind Ensemble, offering masterclasses and instruction in repertoire, pedagogy, and ensemble conducting.

name: Jane Wall
title: Artist Lecturer in Music Education
bio: Jane Wall is a music educator and violinist with extensive experience teaching strings, band, and general music in public schools. She specializes in elementary music pedagogy, curriculum writing, and urban music education through classroom teaching and collaborative projects.

name: R. James Whipple
title: Artist Lecturer in Music Theory
bio: R. James Whipple is a composer, bassoonist, and chamber musician who coordinates the chamber music program and teaches composition studio at Carnegie Mellon. He is Artistic Director of the Renaissance City Winds and has extensive experience in arts administration, composition across multiple genres, and interdisciplinary collaborations including modern dance and poetry.

name: Andrew Wickesberg
title: Artist Lecturer in Chamber Music
bio: Andrew Wickesberg is a violist and Artist Lecturer in Chamber Music at Carnegie Mellon, holding the Mr. & Mrs. Martin G. McGuinn Chair with the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra since 2007. He specializes in chamber music instruction and performance, bringing experience from studying with renowned pedagogues and performing with distinguished conductors.

name: Alexa Woloshyn
title: Associate Professor of Musicology
bio: Dr. Woloshyn studies how electronic, physiological, and socio-cultural technologies mediate the creation and consumption of musical practices, with particular focus on electronically-mediated music, contemporary Indigenous musicians, and music history pedagogy. Her research examines performance practice in live electronic music and Indigenous musicians' use of mediating technologies to construct and interrogate notions of modern Indigeneity.

name: Hanna Wu Li
title: Professor Emerita of Piano and Piano Pedagogy
bio: Hanna Wu Li is Professor Emerita of Piano and Piano Pedagogy at Carnegie Mellon University, recipient of the Presidential Scholar's Distinguished Teacher Award. She developed a distinctive Piano Program for Children whose students have won numerous national and international awards and performed with major orchestras and concert series.

name: Christopher Wu
title: Artist Lecturer in Violin
bio: Christopher Wu is a virtuoso violinist who has performed as concertmaster with the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra for over three decades and is an active chamber musician who has collaborated with renowned artists. He is currently an Artist Lecturer in Violin at Carnegie Mellon University and has taught masterclasses at numerous universities, with his students winning prestigious positions in major orchestras.

name: Lenny Young
title: Artist Lecturer in Solfège
bio: Lenny Young is a teacher, oboist, improviser, arranger and conductor with extensive performance experience across multiple symphonies and musical organizations in the Pittsburgh region. He teaches music theory, history, solfège, and eurhythmics while directing new music ensembles and helping run the Marta Sanchez Dalcroze Training Center at Carnegie Mellon.

name: Monica Yunus
title: Artist Lecturer in Music Entrepreneurship
bio: Monica Yunus is a soprano and Co-Founder of Sing for Hope, a non-profit bringing arts outreach programs to underserved communities. She is a recognized voice in arts activism, having performed at major venues including The Metropolitan Opera and collaborated with renowned artists while advocating for arts accessibility.

name: Rosalyn Abbott
title: Associate Professor
bio: Rosalyn Abbott develops and tests human adipose tissue microenvironments to understand metabolic disease and insulin resistance, integrating systems-based modeling with tissue engineering and mechanical studies. Her lab uses natural biomaterials like silk to culture adipose tissues in vitro with applications toward preventative and therapeutic measures for metabolic syndrome and sustainable cellular agriculture.

name: Chala Merga Abdissa
title: Assistant Professor
department: School of Electrical and Computer Engineering
