bio: Chala Merga Abdissa is an assistant professor with research interests in robotics, machine learning, and control systems. He has experience in rehabilitation robotics and serves as an associate editor for IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering.

name: Amit Acharya
title: Professor
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
bio: Amit Acharya is a professor whose research interests span continuum mechanics, theoretical materials science, and applied mathematics. His work includes the development of advanced computational models and materials characterization methods, with applications in energy, infrastructure, and mechanics.

name: Christopher Adams
title: Operations Director
bio: Christopher Adams leads operations and management of Mill 19 facility at Carnegie Mellon's Manufacturing Futures Institute, convening faculty and students across departments to align operations with strategic directions. With over ten years of industry experience at Siemens Energy and the Advanced Robotics for Manufacturing Institute, he specializes in robotics adoption, automation, and manufacturing innovation across aerospace, automotive, energy, and textiles sectors.

name: Peter Adams
title: Professor
bio: Peter Adams is a professor whose research focuses on developing chemical transport models, particularly for simulating aerosol microphysical processes, ultrafine particles, and cloud condensation nuclei formation in global climate models. His work extends to investigating the effects of climate change on air quality, short-lived climate forcers, and particulate matter formation from various sources.

name: Franck K. Adjogble
title: Lecturer
bio: Franck Adjogble is a distinguished professional bridging industry knowledge and academic research with expertise in technology innovation management, manufacturing automation, and artificial intelligence. His research focuses on Industrial Internet of Things, Natural Language Processing, and AI applications in production planning, with extensive experience in rolling mill automation and digital transformation.

name: Shobhit Aggarwal
title: Advanced Manufacturing Engineer for Robotics
bio: Shobhit Aggarwal is the Advanced Manufacturing Engineer for Robotics at Carnegie Mellon's Manufacturing Futures Institute, where he oversees a robotic assembly/disassembly testbed equipped with industrial robot arms and autonomous mobile robots. He leverages expertise in product development, robotics, and manufacturing digitization to advance robotic assembly, disassembly, and autonomous material handling in manufacturing facilities.

name: Aisulu Aitbekova
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Aisulu Aitbekova is an assistant professor of chemical engineering who develops catalytic processes to make fuels and chemicals by coupling light and heat to drive chemical reactions efficiently. Her research spans from nanoscale materials development to microscale environment control to macroscale process development, with a focus on sustainability and decarbonization.

name: Burak Aksak
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Burak Aksak's research focuses on bioinspired engineering, specifically studying and designing engineering systems in adhesives, actuators, and sensors inspired by biological systems. His teaching interests span mechanics and design courses at both undergraduate and graduate levels, with emphasis on incorporating biomimicry, advanced materials, and entrepreneurship topics.

name: Alaine M. Allen
title: Educator
bio: Alaine Allen is an educator committed to creating inclusive excellence and uplifting voices from historically marginalized groups in STEM environments. Her work includes teaching, directing pre-college and undergraduate engineering programs, and collaborating on NSF-funded projects focused on broadening participation in engineering.

name: Magdi M. Amin
title: Adjunct Professor
bio: Magdi Amin is an adjunct professor at Carnegie Mellon University Africa teaching Responsible Digital Development, and managing partner of African Renaissance Ventures backing early-stage technology ventures. His career focuses on ethical leadership and responsibly using markets and technology to empower people, with two decades of experience at the World Bank and IFC across Africa, East Asia, and MENA.

name: Geoffrey Andrew
bio: Geoffrey Andrew is an experienced program and project manager with fourteen years of cumulative experience in health, livelihood, and program management across international NGO and World Bank-funded projects. He specializes in financial management, team leadership, monitoring and evaluation, and risk management with expertise in institutional oversight and stakeholder coordination.

name: Ellen M. Ayoob
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Ellen Ayoob is an assistant teaching professor who applies human-centered research and design methods to understand users and develop actionable design strategies. She bridges engineering, design, and communications to create empathetic, technology-focused solutions informed by user narratives and experiences.

name: James A. Bain
title: Professor
bio: James Bain is a professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department at Carnegie Mellon University with over 225 published papers in magnetic, optical, electrical, thermal, and mechanical devices for information storage. His current research focuses on heat assisted magnetic recording and resistive switches for memory and reconfigurable electronics.

name: Amir Barati Farimani
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Amir Barati Farimani directs the Mechanical and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (MAIL) at Carnegie Mellon, focusing on applying machine learning, data science, and molecular dynamics simulations to health and bio-engineering problems. His research combines physics-based principles with data-driven models to advance additive manufacturing, computational engineering, and multi-scale simulations across mechanical systems.

name: Amie Barry
bio: Amie brings over a decade of experience overseeing up to $1 billion in funding from diverse global donors and leading finance and operations functions across multiple regions. She specializes in building efficient systems for compliance, transparency, and sustainability while driving significant new business wins through partnership-building and proposal development.

name: Aaron Bartnick
title: Distinguished Policy Fellow
bio: Aaron Bartnick is a Distinguished Policy Fellow at Carnegie Mellon's Critical Technology Initiative with nearly 15 years of experience at the intersection of technology, national security, and global markets. His work focuses on economic and technology security, foreign investment risk analysis, and emerging technology sectors including AI, quantum computing, and semiconductors.

name: Lujo Bauer
title: Professor
department: Electrical and Computer Engineering Department and Software and Societal Systems Department
bio: Lujo Bauer's research spans computer security and privacy, including building usable access-control systems with sound theoretical foundations and developing languages for runtime security policy enforcement. His recent work focuses on tools to help users stay safer online and how machine learning advances can create more secure systems.

name: Jessica Becker
bio: Jessica Becker manages the Academic Affairs and Student Services team at the Institute for Software Research. She leads student enrollment, advising processes, academic policies, and student services activities.

name: Joanne Beckwith Maddock
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Joanne Beckwith Maddock is an assistant teaching professor whose primary research focus is on undergraduate engineering education, particularly sustainable workforce development and the alignment of chemical engineering curricula with industrial workforce needs. She conducts engineering education outreach to K-12 students and educators with a focus on rural communities, and develops learning modules to enhance safety, statistics, and design elements in laboratory courses.

name: Mark Bedillion
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Mark Bedillion is an associate teaching professor whose research interests span distributed manipulation, control applications in data storage and manufacturing, and STEM education. With seven years of industrial experience at Seagate Technology in servo-control architecture development, he brings practical expertise to both undergraduate and graduate-level mechatronics and control systems courses.

name: Brian Belowich
bio: Brian Belowich provides course facilitation and support for CEE lectures and laboratories, working with faculty members to manage course-related laboratory activities and equipment. He collaborates with faculty to ensure CEE students have the resources needed to succeed in their coursework and identifies areas where additional support may be necessary.

name: Theophilus A. Benson
title: Professor
bio: Theophilus Benson is an electrical and computer engineering professor whose research focuses on improving the performance and availability of computer networks, particularly by working with cloud providers to enhance their infrastructures. He has recently developed initiatives to address the digital divide in the global south and has received numerous awards including the SIGCOMM Test of Time Award and NSF CAREER Award.

name: Sarah Bergbreiter
title: Associate Professor
bio: Sarah Bergbreiter is a roboticist specializing in engineering robotic systems at sub-millimeter size scales, with a focus on bioinspired design and novel fabrication techniques. Her research spans microrobotics, soft robotics, and legged/aerial systems, with applications in manufacturing and medicine.

name: Mario Bergés
title: Professor
bio: Mario Bergés develops approximate inference techniques to extract useful information from sensor data in civil infrastructure systems, with a focus on buildings and energy efficiency. He works to make the built environment more operationally efficient and robust through information and communication technologies to address resource constraints and environmental challenges.

name: Alyssa Bernens
department: Energy Science, Technology and Policy (EST&P) Program

name: Jack Beuth
title: Professor
bio: Jack Beuth's research focuses on manufacturing, solid mechanics, and fracture mechanics, with particular expertise in additive manufacturing processes and interfacial mechanics. His work includes developing process map approaches to characterize and optimize additive manufacturing processes across their full operating ranges for improved process control and expanded capabilities.

name: Vijayakumar Bhagavatula
title: U.A. & Helen Whitaker Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Vijayakumar Bhagavatula is a distinguished professor whose research focuses on computer vision, pattern recognition algorithms, and signal processing for data storage systems. He has published extensively with over 410 conference papers and 210 journal papers, and is recognized as a Fellow of IEEE, SPIE, OSA, and IAPR among other prestigious organizations.

name: Lorenz T. Biegler
title: Covestro University Professor
bio: Lorenz Biegler is a leading expert in computer-aided process engineering (CAPE) with research spanning flowsheet optimization, optimization of differential and algebraic equation systems, and constrained nonlinear process control. His work addresses critical challenges in energy production, carbon management, and sustainability through advanced optimization methods and process systems engineering.

name: Jacobo Bielak
title: Hamerschlag University Professor Emeritus
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
bio: Jacobo Bielak is a leading expert in earthquake engineering and computational seismology, with foundational contributions to seismic provisions for soil-structure interaction now part of the National Earthquake Hazards Reduction Program. His research spans earthquake engineering, engineering seismology, computational mechanics, and structural health monitoring, with recognition including membership in the National Academy of Engineering.

name: Kathleen Bieryla
title: Associate Professor
bio: Kathleen Bieryla is an Associate Professor with expertise in biomechanics and biomedical engineering, with recent scholarly interests centered on ballet biomechanics and the development of engineering identity among undergraduate students. Her teaching experience spans engineering design, biomechanics, biomedical instrumentation, and first-year engineering courses.

name: Nancy Biwott
bio: Nancy Biwott is a seasoned diversity and inclusion professional with a passion for social justice who has worked across various organizations in roles touching on conflict transformation, inclusion, and sustainable business. Her vision is to support organizations and teams towards becoming partnership-driven spaces that embed values of diversity and inclusion.

name: Michael Blackhurst
title: Executive Director
bio: Michael Blackhurst is the Executive Director of the Open Energy Outlook Initiative, leading research and stakeholder engagement focused on the US energy system. His research spans energy modeling, carbon management, and the intersection of energy policy with climate and sustainability challenges.

name: Shawn Blanton
title: Professor
bio: Shawn Blanton is a professor specializing in integrated system tests, testable design, and test methodology development for integrated circuits. His research encompasses hardware security, IC test and diagnosis, and emerging applications of LLM-based electronic design automation.

name: Peter Boatwright
title: Allan D. Shocker Professor of Marketing and New Product Development
bio: Peter Boatwright is the director and co-founder of the Integrated Innovation Institute and an expert in innovation education who has co-authored three books on related topics. He has consulted for leading companies across diverse industries including Nissan, Apple, Procter and Gamble, and Merck on product innovation, brand strategy, and market research.

name: Michael Bockstaller
title: Professor
bio: Michael Bockstaller is a professor of materials science and engineering whose research focuses on structure-property-performance relations in polymer hybrid materials. His current interests include optical and thermal transport properties of polymer nanocomposites and the development of polymeric hybrid materials for solid state lighting and battery applications.

name: Brandon Bodily
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Brandon Bodily brings over 20 years of aerospace industry experience in developing and implementing new materials and manufacturing processes. He specializes in designing integrated systems for high-precision applications and translating customer needs into engineering solutions.

name: Ophelia Bolmin
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Ophelia Bolmin's research focuses on the interface between structural dynamics, biomechanics, and design, with a particular emphasis on bio-inspired mechanical systems for dynamic tailoring. Her group at the MIND lab designs, manufactures, and tests innovative systems with applications ranging from aerospace to micro-robotics and prosthetics.

name: Travis D. Breaux
title: Associate Professor
department: Computer Science and Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Travis Breaux develops methods and tools for creating correct software specifications and ensuring systems conform to those specifications in transparent and reliable ways. His research includes demonstrating compliance with accessibility, privacy, and security laws and standards.

name: Melissa Brown
bio: Melissa Brown manages and administrates master's programs and online certificate programs at Carnegie Mellon. She serves as academic advisor for MS programs in Computational Design & Manufacturing, AI Engineering, and Mechanical Engineering.

name: Timothy X Brown
title: Professor
bio: Timothy X Brown is a professor in the Engineering and Public Policy Department at Carnegie Mellon University Africa and director of the Kigali Collaborative Research Center, with research interests spanning adaptive network control, machine learning, and wireless communication systems. His work focuses on security, privacy, and ICT applications for development in Africa, with current funding from the Mastercard Foundation and recognition including the NSF CAREER Award.

name: Wändi Bruine de Bruin
title: Collaborating Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Wändi Bruine de Bruin is a collaborating professor at Carnegie Mellon and Provost Professor at USC who studies the psychology of risk and science communication. Her research applies behavioral science to understand and influence behavior change in health and environmental contexts.

name: Mark Budnik
title: Teaching Professor
department: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Mark Budnik is a teaching professor specializing in embedded systems and automotive and industrial applications, bringing 16 years of semiconductor industry experience including a role as engineering director at Hitachi Semiconductor. His work focuses on controls, automation, and practical applications of electrical engineering, with extensive contributions through online education reaching nearly 100,000 students globally.

name: Esther Bugaiga
bio: Esther Bugaiga is a professional with 10 years of experience in project management, operations, administration, and student life. She is passionate about women and youth empowerment, growth mindset coaching, and financial literacy.

name: Moise Busogi
bio: Moise Busogi is a researcher at CMU Africa with expertise in operations research, machine learning, and agent-based modeling and simulation of complex systems. His work focuses on advanced manufacturing, including applications to mixed-model assembly lines, automated quality monitoring, and additive manufacturing.

name: Pedro J. Bustamante
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Pedro Bustamante studies the technical, economic, and policy dimensions of telecommunications advancements, with focus on dynamic radioelectric spectrum access and decentralized systems. He applies machine learning to spectrum regulation and works across interdisciplinary research centers on wireless technologies, governance, and telecommunications policy.

name: Joseph Calandrino
title: Assistant Professor
department: Engineering and Public Policy; Software and Societal Systems
school: College of Engineering; School of Computer Science
bio: Joseph Calandrino is an assistant professor with joint appointments in Engineering and Public Policy and Software and Societal Systems, focused on applying computer security, privacy, and computer science research methods to address public policy issues. His research interests span cybersecurity, privacy engineering, AI security, elections security, and the intersection of technology policy and consumer protection.

name: Sebastian Calderon Velasco
bio: Sebastian Calderon's research focuses on advancing transmission electron microscopy (TEM) techniques to investigate the atomic-scale structure and dynamic processes of novel ferroelectric materials and complex oxides. His work aims to accelerate materials discovery and enable next-generation electronic devices through advanced STEM characterization of functional materials.

name: Phillip Campbell
title: Research Professor
bio: Campbell has over 25 years of experience developing unique biomedical solutions through multidisciplinary research, with expertise in natural-based biomaterials, implant biocompatibility, and tissue engineering. His research focuses on understanding and engineering the cellular microenvironment to control cell behavior and tissue formation for musculoskeletal, cardiac, immunological, and cancer applications.

name: Rita Canton
title: Director of Engineering Outreach
bio: Rita Canton directs engineering outreach for the College of Engineering, coordinating efforts to reach K-12 teachers, students, and out-of-school time providers to build a diverse engineering community. With 25 years of experience in K-12 education and administration in underserved communities, she is a champion for equitable educational access.

name: Kathleen M. Carley
title: Professor
bio: Kathleen Carley combines cognitive science, sociology, organization science, and computer science to address complex social and organizational issues, with her most notable contribution being the establishment of Dynamic Network Analysis (DNA) for examining large high-dimensional time variant networks. She has developed premier tools and systems including ORA for network analysis and visualization, AutoMap for text mining, and various multi-agent simulation systems used globally for applications ranging from counter-terrorism to social media analytics.

name: L. Richard Carley
title: Professor
bio: Carley is an internationally recognized researcher in CAD for analog circuit synthesis, high-speed analog signal processing, RF front-end circuits, and the design of MEMS and NEMS devices. He has authored or co-authored over 200 publications and holds 23 patents, while having mentored over 70 graduate students who pursue careers in academia and industry.

name: Stephanie Caruso
title: Student Affairs Consultant
bio: Stephanie Caruso is a seasoned student affairs professional with over a decade of experience supporting graduate students across academic advising, student engagement, and operations. She specializes in student data strategy, process design, and creating student-centered solutions for complex administrative systems.

name: Sarah H. Cen
title: Assistant Professor
department: Electrical & Computer Engineering and Engineering & Public Policy
bio: Sarah Cen is an assistant professor whose research lies at the intersection of machine learning, statistics, economics, law, and public policy, with recent work on AI audits, AI supply chains, algorithmic fairness, and procedural due process for AI-driven decisions. She was previously an HAI postdoctoral researcher at Stanford, jointly affiliated with Stanford Law School's RegLab and the Department of Computer Science.

name: Lenah Chacha
title: Research Lab Manager
bio: Lenah Chacha is the research lab manager for CyLab-Africa with over seven years of experience in networking, security engineering, and application security. Her expertise spans software engineering, secure application design, and network security analysis for organizations including SMBs and financial institutions.

name: Patjanaporn Chalacheva
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Sang Chalacheva's research focuses on computational modeling of cardiovascular autonomic control in sickle cell disease, an inherited blood disorder characterized by painful vaso-occlusive crises. She also investigates sleep-related breathing disorders and is an active member of the IEEE Engineering in Medicine and Biology Society.

name: Daphne Wui Yarn Chan
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Daphne Chan's research focuses on designing polymers to address global sustainability challenges, including engineering plastics from renewable protein feedstock and waste tire rubber recycling. Her work spans self-assembled block polymers and their applications in ultrafiltration membranes.

name: Justin Chan
title: Assistant Professor
department: Electrical and Computer Engineering Department and Software and Societal Systems Department
bio: Justin Chan is an assistant professor building intelligent mobile and embedded systems for computational health and large-scale environmental sensing. His research has led to FDA-listed medical applications, international health initiatives, and licensed technologies acquired by major companies.

name: Steven Chase
title: Associate Professor
bio: Steven Chase designs brain-computer interfaces to study cognitive processes such as learning, memory, and skill acquisition. His work provides understanding of how movement information is represented in neural networks and informs the development of neural prosthetics.

name: Vanessa Chen
title: Associate Professor
bio: Vanessa Chen is an associate professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering whose research focuses on hardware-algorithm co-design for machine learning, security, and neural interfaces. Her work spans biometrics, hardware security, sensors, and trusted wireless systems, with recognition from the NSF CAREER Award and IEEE Solid-State Circuits Society.

name: Sarah Christian
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Sarah Christian is an associate teaching professor specializing in structural engineering, sustainable materials, and building envelope design. She teaches courses in structural engineering, materials, soil mechanics, and design, with a focus on developing socially conscious engineers who understand real-world applications and community impact.

name: Nicolas Christin
title: Professor
bio: Nicolas Christin is a professor jointly appointed in the School of Computer Science and the Department of Engineering & Public Policy, specializing in cybersecurity, privacy, and the application of AI/ML to security challenges. His research focuses on understanding cybercrime, fraud, malware, and developing solutions for security and privacy in both technical and policy contexts.

name: Mark Ciotola
bio: Mark Ciotola's research focuses on telepresence and long-distance human-to-human communications, with applications ranging from space exploration to remote academic environments. He investigates best practices for facilitating effective real-time communications across continents and explores how remote participants can better experience distant environments like spacecraft and lunar operations.

name: Betsy Clark
bio: Betsy Clark is the X-Ray facilities specialist at Carnegie Mellon, managing access to and training for X-Ray facilities. She provides technical support for MSE undergraduate laboratories and equipment including XRD, microscopy, and AFM, with a focus on student training and assistance.

name: Matthew Cline
title: Undergraduate Lab Instructor
bio: Matthew Cline serves as the Undergraduate Lab Instructor, developing and delivering lab course materials while overseeing the Robert Rothfus Undergraduate Lab facilities and equipment. He supervises student workers, co-advises independent research projects, and actively participates in engineering education communities focused on laboratory instruction.

name: Doron Cohen
bio: Doron Cohen is a behavioral scientist who focuses on the experimental and computational analysis of behavior, behavioral contexts, and learning. His research clarifies decision-making processes and their shaping by incentive systems, with applications to safety measures, taxation systems, and human-computer interactions.

name: Christophe Combemale
bio: Christophe Combemale's research focuses on the implications of technology choices and process design for skill demand, and on workforce supply chain levers to meet industry skill demand needs. He examines how regional and national labor supply may constrain economic productivity and innovation, and develops solutions that enhance outcomes for workers and firms.

name: Elizabeth Cook
bio: Dr. Cook is Vice President of the Association of Edison Illuminating Companies and a national leader in grid modernization, data strategy, and operational transformation for utilities. She also provides executive advisory services through leadership coaching and founded Integrated Being LLC, a platform focused on mind-body-spirit connection and intentional living.

name: Keith Cook
title: Professor
bio: Keith Cook's research applies biomedical engineering to cardiac and pulmonary diseases, with a focus on generating new devices, treatment strategies, and diagnostic tools for clinical translation. His laboratory develops thoracic artificial lungs, perfluorocarbon emulsions for drug delivery, biomaterial approaches to reduce coagulation, and PET-based diagnostic tools.

name: Charles Cranor
title: Senior Systems Scientist
bio: Charles Cranor is a senior systems scientist in the Parallel Data Lab who focuses on building novel systems with positive real-world impact, particularly large-scale storage systems for HPC and cloud platforms. His work spans storage architecture, data center design, and system deployment, with a background in network monitoring, embedded systems, and virtual memory management.

name: Lorrie Faith Cranor
title: Bosch Distinguished Professor
department: Computer Science and Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Lorrie Cranor is the Director of CyLab and a leading researcher in usable privacy and security, having authored over 200 papers on online privacy and security topics. She founded the Symposium on Usable Privacy and Security (SOUPS) and co-edited the seminal book 'Security and Usability', establishing herself as a key figure in building the usable privacy and security research community.

name: Audrey Kurth Cronin
title: Trustees Professor of Security and Technology
department: Carnegie Mellon Institute for Strategy & Technology
bio: Audrey Cronin is a leading expert on security, terrorism, and technological innovation who studies how emerging technologies are reshaping military strategy and terrorism. Her research examines the intersection of security policy, innovation, and the strategic implications of new technologies for national defense and counterterrorism.

name: Lisa Currin
bio: Lisa Currin oversees the admissions process and advises graduate students concerning application procedures and international student admission requirements. She serves as the departmental liaison to the Office of International Education.

name: Leonardo da Silva Sousa
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Leonardo da Silva Sousa is an assistant teaching professor in Electrical and Computer Engineering at CMU Silicon Valley with research interests in software engineering, code smells, refactoring, and software architecture. His work combines academic research published at top venues like FSE and ICSE with real-world applications, including the VazaZika platform for dengue surveillance.

name: Ismaila Dabo
title: Associate Professor
bio: Ismaila Dabo develops predictive computational models and machine-learning methods to optimize materials for energy conversion and storage applications. His research bridges materials science, condensed matter physics, applied mathematics, and computer science to advance sustainable energy technologies.

name: Marc Dandin
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Marc Dandin's research focuses on microsystems engineering and CMOS integrated circuit design for biosensing and biomedical applications, including low-noise photodetectors and portable fluorescence sensors. He is the founder of Kiskeya Microsystems LLC, commercializing point-of-care microsystems for HIV viral load monitoring.

name: Robert F. Davis
title: Emeritus Professor
bio: Robert Davis is an emeritus professor specializing in the growth and characterization of wide bandgap semiconductor thin films including SiC, Ga2O3, GaN, and AlN, with research extending to electronic device applications such as gate insulators and reactive plasma etching. He is a member of the National Academy of Engineering and has made significant contributions to materials science through over 430 peer-reviewed publications and numerous prestigious awards.

name: Justin Dawber
title: IT Operations Manager
department: Chemical Engineering and Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Justin Dawber manages all aspects of IT operations for the Chemical Engineering and Materials Science and Engineering departments, including IT staff management, infrastructure, and asset procurement. He serves as a local IT expert and resource for faculty, staff, and students in planning, deployment, and support of IT resources.

name: Kaushik Dayal
title: Professor
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
bio: Kaushik Dayal is a professor whose research focuses on theoretical and computational multiscale methods applied to materials science, bridging atomic to continuum scales in functional behavior and non-equilibrium response. His work addresses energy resilience, heterogeneous materials under extreme conditions, and the development of advanced materials for defense and civil applications.

name: Maarten de Boer
title: Professor
bio: Maarten de Boer's group explores the processing and nanomechanical behavior of new materials through the design, fabrication, and testing of micromachined platforms. His research investigates the interplay between processing, environment, and material properties such as strength, fracture, fatigue, and adhesion, with funding from the Department of Energy, National Science Foundation, NASA, and Army Research Laboratory.

name: Joseph F. DeCarolis
title: Department Head
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Joseph DeCarolis is an internationally-recognized leader with technical expertise in energy systems, climate change, computing, and public policy. His research combines domain knowledge in energy systems with numerical and mathematical modeling, life cycle assessment, and energy economics to provide practical energy-related insights to decision makers.

name: Elizabeth Dechasa
bio: Elizabeth Dechasa brings experience in student support and international education to the College of Engineering. Her background spans career advising, English composition instruction, and cultural leadership programming.

name: Marc De Graef
title: Professor
department: Department of Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Marc De Graef is a professor specializing in electron microscopy, magnetic materials characterization, and microstructural science. His research combines advanced materials processing, computation, and informatics to understand and characterize complex materials structures.

name: Mark DeSantis
title: Adjunct Professor
bio: Mark DeSantis is an adjunct professor of entrepreneurship at Carnegie Mellon who brings extensive experience founding and leading AI-driven companies including Bloomfield (specialty crop AI), RoadBotics (infrastructure assessment), and kWantix (energy hedge fund). His research and advisory work focuses on applying artificial intelligence and deep learning to solve real-world problems in agriculture, infrastructure, and energy sectors.

name: Elizabeth Dickey
title: Associate Professor
bio: Elizabeth Dickey develops processing-structure-property relationships for material systems, with particular emphasis on understanding grain boundary chemistry and its effects on electrical and chemical transport. Her research uses electron microscopy and spectroscopy to understand how microstructure and internal interfaces govern macroscopic properties in ceramics, composites, and ferroelectric materials.

name: Neil Donahue
title: Professor
department: Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, and Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Neil Donahue studies the behavior of organic compounds in Earth's atmosphere, with a focus on the origin and transformations of very small organic particles that play critical roles in climate change and human health. His research group is world-renowned for understanding how compounds from both natural and human sources affect the atmosphere, and he directs the Steinbrenner Institute for Environmental Education and Research.

name: Frederike Dümbgen
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Dümbgen's research aims to improve the efficiency and safety of robots and engineered systems through advances in optimization and machine learning, with a focus on certifiable and globally optimal methods. Her work creates principled foundations for the next generation of robotics and intelligent machines applicable to design, manufacturing, autonomous vehicles, and assistive technology.

name: Hakan Erdogmus
title: Faculty
bio: Hakan Erdogmus is a software engineering expert specializing in software measurement, quality, and engineering economics with extensive research and publications in these areas. He is a founding faculty member of ECE's M.S. in Software Engineering Program and has served as a past editor-in-chief of IEEE Software.

name: Sarah Fakhreddine
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Sarah Fakhreddine develops water management solutions that address water quantity and quality by investigating hydrologic and biogeochemical processes controlling contaminant and nutrient fate in complex systems. Her work integrates laboratory, numerical modeling, and field approaches to translate fundamental processes into actionable engineering solutions that protect water quality for human and ecosystem health.

name: Mohamed Farag
bio: Mohamed brings over 10 years of industry experience in Big Data, Cloud Computing, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, with research focused on leveraging AI to build resilient safety-critical systems. His expertise spans information technology consulting, data science, and security applications of AI and ML.

name: Gary K. Fedder
title: Professor
bio: Gary Fedder is the Howard M. Wilkoff Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and faculty director of the Manufacturing Futures Institute. His research focuses on design and manufacturing of MEMS, micro/nanosensors, stretchable electronics, and digital twin systems for manufacturing.

name: Adam Feinberg
title: Professor
department: Biomedical Engineering and Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Adam Feinberg is a professor investigating cell-material interactions and how guidance cues can be encoded in the extracellular space to organize multicellular assembly, with a focus on protein-based materials for applications in muscle tissue engineering, corneal repair, and cancer. His laboratory develops novel bioprinting and biomaterial fabrication techniques, including the FRESH 3D bioprinting platform and ECM shrink-wrapping technology, to create engineered tissue constructs.

name: Susan Finger
title: Emeritus Professor
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
bio: Susan Finger's research focuses on understanding how students learn from one another in collaborative design projects, examining how social ties between teammates affect outcomes and how design progress can be traced through document and message exchange. She has spent her career at the interface between culture and technology, serving as program director at the National Science Foundation and founding the journal Research in Engineering Design.

name: Paul Fischbeck
title: Professor
department: Engineering and Public Policy, Social and Decision Making Sciences
bio: Paul Fischbeck applies decision analysis and behavioral social science to policy problems, with particular focus on quantifying and communicating uncertainty. His research spans theoretical advances in decision analysis and applied real-world problems including risk communication, decision support systems, and organizational reliability.

name: Daniel Flaherty
bio: Daniel Flaherty provides technical support and training for advanced microscopy equipment at Carnegie Mellon, including atomic force microscopes (AFMs) and transmission electron microscopes (TEMs). He assists students and researchers with microscopy techniques and equipment operation for materials characterization and analysis.

name: Katherine Flanigan
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Katherine Flanigan develops intelligent cyber-physical systems that integrate sensing, computing, and actuation technologies to improve infrastructure performance, resilience, and sustainability. Her research extends to cyber-physical-social systems that explicitly account for human-infrastructure interactions and community-driven governance.

name: Cameron Fletcher
bio: Cameron Fletcher is an editor with 37 years of experience at the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine, where she worked with virtually every program division. She has also done freelance work in international economic policy, macroeconomics, and landscape architecture, among other areas.

name: Cameron Fouse
bio: Cameron Fouse is responsible for teaching and supporting research in metalwork. He specializes in machining, turning, water jet cutting, and welding techniques.

name: Franz Franchetti
title: Kavčić-Moura Professor of Electrical & Computer Engineering
bio: Franz Franchetti's research focuses on automatic performance tuning and program generation for emerging parallel platforms including multicore CPUs, GPUs, FPGAs, and HPC systems. He leads the SPIRAL effort to enable automatic generation of highly optimized software libraries and investigates domain-specific transformations, HPC applications in smart grids, and material sciences.

name: Whitney Friedman
bio: Whitney Friedman manages the operations of the department, including staff, human resources, policy, and finance. She serves as a resource and advisor to faculty and staff on compliance, policy, administration, and strategic planning.

name: Erica R.H. Fuchs
title: Kavčić-Moura Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Erica Fuchs is Director of the Critical Technology Initiative and researches the development, commercialization, and global manufacturing of emerging technologies with a focus on national policy implications. Her work leverages advanced analytics to help governments invest in and enact policies for technologies critical to national security, economic competitiveness, and societal well-being.

name: John Fulmer
bio: John Fulmer is responsible for teaching and supporting research in metal work. He specializes in various machining and fabrication techniques including turning, sheet metal work, water jet cutting, and welding.

name: Stefanie Garcia
title: Outreach Coordinator
bio: Stefanie Garcia is an outreach coordinator for the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering with research experience in developing MEMS implantable medical devices for radiation detection and oncology applications. She is passionate about designing interactive science and engineering lessons and helping students develop critical thinking skills about design.

name: James H. Garrett Jr.
title: Professor
bio: James Garrett's research focuses on applications of sensors and sensor systems to civil infrastructure condition assessment, and data mining and machine learning techniques for infrastructure management problems. His work includes mobile hardware/software systems for field applications and knowledge-based decision support systems for engineering practice.

name: Warren M. Garrison
title: Emeritus Professor
department: Department of Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Warren Garrison is an emeritus professor specializing in fracture, deformation, and alloy development with a focus on creating advanced materials for aerospace applications. His work has led to the development of new shaft alloys for jet engines, quench-crack-immune steels, and ultra-high strength stainless steels suitable for aircraft landing gear.

name: Andrew Gellman
title: Lord Professor of Chemical Engineering
bio: Gellman's research focuses on surface chemistry with particular emphasis on catalytic surface chemistry, enantioselective chemistry on chiral surfaces, and high throughput study of alloy surfaces. His recent work has centered on the study of enantioselectivity on naturally chiral metal surfaces and the development of methods for studying alloy surface properties such as catalysis.

name: Philip Gibbons
title: Professor
department: Computer Science Department and Electrical & Computer Engineering Department
bio: Phil Gibbons is a professor whose research spans big data, parallel computing, databases, cloud computing, and distributed systems, with publications across theory and systems in top-tier venues. He previously served as co-PI/co-director for the $15M Intel Science and Technology Center for Cloud Computing and has held research positions at AT&T Bell Laboratories, Lucent Bell Laboratories, and Intel Research Pittsburgh.

name: Gisele Gihozo
title: Academic Affairs Support Coordinator
bio: Gisele Gihozo is CMU-Africa's academic affairs support coordinator with a background in communications and experience working with international higher education institutions in Rwanda. She is passionate about transformative education and empowering the next generation of students.

name: Olga Gikundiro Buki
bio: Olga Gikundiro Buki is a higher education professional dedicated to fostering student success through innovative retention strategies and proactive advising. She manages the Mastercard Foundation Scholars program at CMU-Africa, overseeing recruitment, enrollment, student services, and career development.

name: Virgil D. Gligor
title: Professor
bio: Professor Gligor's research spans nearly four decades of work on trustworthy computing in adversarial environments, ranging from access control and cryptographic protocols to denial-of-service protection and secure Internet architecture. He has made pioneering contributions to secure operating systems, covert channel analysis, intrusion detection, and secure wireless sensor networks, recognized with the ACM Outstanding Innovation Award and IEEE Computer Society Technical Achievement Award.

name: Gabe Gomes
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Gabe Gomes develops new chemical reactions, catalysts, and materials using machine learning and automated synthesis, with research focused on autonomous reaction discovery and catalysis. His work bridges machine learning and organic chemistry to create platforms for accelerating chemical research and discovery.

name: Udo-Martin Gómez
bio: Dr. Udo-Martin Gómez has been leading Bosch Research since 2025, bringing over 25 years of experience in microstructure technology and automotive electronics. His career spans specialist and management roles across Bosch divisions, most recently as Executive Vice President of Strategy, Organization and Business Development.

name: Hamish Gordon
title: Associate Professor
bio: Hamish Gordon is an associate professor whose research focuses on the effects of air pollution and natural airborne particles on clouds and climate through the development of weather prediction and climate models. His work involves simulating atmospheric processes at high resolution and comparing results with detailed observations, while also participating in laboratory experiments and field studies such as the CLOUD experiment at CERN.

name: Chrysanthos Gounaris
title: Associate Professor
bio: Chrysanthos Gounaris is an Associate Professor of Chemical Engineering whose research focuses on advanced process decision-making, optimization techniques, and their application to energy, sustainability, and industrial systems. His work spans from theoretical nonlinear modeling and global optimization to practical applications in pharmaceutical development, carbon management, and industrial efficiency.

name: Noelia Grande Gutiérrez
title: Faculty
bio: Noelia Grande Gutiérrez develops multiphysics computational models at the intersection of engineering and cardiovascular medicine to support clinical decision making and advance understanding of cardiovascular disease. Her research includes patient-specific thrombosis modeling and computational frameworks for investigating coronary artery disease.

name: Lauren Greenwald
title: Program Coordinator
bio: Lauren Greenwald is the program coordinator for community engagement at Carnegie Mellon's College of Engineering, overseeing the EMERGE mentorship program and student fellowships. Her work focuses on student development, mentorship, and fostering collaboration between the college and student organizations to support undergraduate growth.

name: Kelvin B. Gregory
title: Professor
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
bio: Kelvin Gregory is a professor whose research explores the microbiology, ecology, and fundamental interactions between bacteria and their physical and chemical environment. His current work focuses on produced water management, environmental nanotechnology, geologic carbon sequestration, and microfluidic cell sorting.

name: Jason Griess
title: Associate Director of Partnerships
bio: Jason Griess is the associate director of partnerships at CyLab, where he maintains and strengthens relationships with existing partners to ensure they fully utilize partnership benefits. His background includes roles in technology ecosystem development, strategic consulting, and policy research focused on economic development and urban innovation.

name: Ignacio E. Grossmann
title: R. R. Dean University Professor
bio: Ignacio Grossmann is a leading expert in discrete-continuous optimization, optimal synthesis and planning of chemical processes, energy systems, and supply chain optimization. His research addresses process systems engineering challenges with applications to industrial decarbonization, energy innovation, and digital supply chains.

name: Katelyn Haas-Conrad
title: Assistant Director for Partnerships
department: Wilton E. Scott Institute for Energy Innovation
bio: Katelyn Haas-Conrad serves as assistant director for partnerships at the Scott Institute, developing and stewarding partnerships and initiatives focused on addressing critical challenges in America's energy systems. Her work supports the University Energy Institute Collaborative, a partnership of university-based energy institutes advancing a low carbon and just energy future.

name: Diana R. Haidar
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Diana Haidar leads Carnegie Mellon's Maker Ecosystem, connecting students to makerspace resources and bringing hands-on design experiences to the classroom. She is passionate about promoting diversity in STEM through active learning initiatives and youth outreach activities.

name: Eni Halilaj
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Eni Halilaj directs the CMU Musculoskeletal Biomechanics Lab, an interdisciplinary group of engineers seeking to understand and optimize human movement mechanics. Her research combines artificial intelligence, biomechanical modeling, and wearable sensors to advance clinical rehabilitation and prevent degenerative joint diseases.

name: Dena Haritos Tsamitis
title: Director
bio: Dena Haritos Tsamitis has directed Carnegie Mellon's Information Networking Institute since 2004, leading efforts to prepare information networking, security, and mobility professionals globally. Her research focuses on transnational university partnerships, cybersecurity education, and advancing diversity and women's participation in technology fields.

name: Corey Harper
title: Assistant Professor
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
bio: Corey Harper directs the Future Mobility Systems Lab and applies modeling, simulation, and data analytics to assess the equity, environmental, and policy implications of emerging transportation technologies. His research has focused on climate-resilient transportation systems, automation, and the socioeconomic impacts of technological change on mobility networks.

name: Justin Harvilla
title: TechSpark Fabrication Instructor
bio: Justin Harvilla is a TechSpark Fabrication Instructor who teaches courses in woodworking and modern making techniques. He provides guidance and expertise in fabrication technologies including laser cutting, 3D printing, mold making, and rapid prototyping.

name: Alexander Hauptmann
title: Professor
bio: Alexander Hauptmann's research focuses on integrating text, image, video, and audio analysis to process large corpora of multimedia data from the internet and open sources. His work combines speech recognition, natural language processing, and information retrieval to build intelligent systems that understand and process diverse data modalities for improved machine learning and information extraction applications.

name: Kurt Haverstock
bio: Kurt Haverstock manages the INI's distributed education centers and technical staff who support them. He oversees operations of classrooms at the INI Building and Robert Mehrabian Collaborative Innovation Center, enabling lecture delivery between Carnegie Mellon's Silicon Valley campus and the University of Hyogo in Japan.

name: Robert Heard
title: Teaching Professor
department: Department of Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Robert Heard is a teaching professor with extensive experience in materials science and engineering, specializing in the applications and commercialization of specialized new materials and technologies. His research interests span iron and steelmaking, nano-materials, plasma technologies, and innovation management, with a track record of translating academic research into industrial applications.

name: Rod Heiple
title: Research Partnership Specialist
bio: Rod Heiple is a research partnership specialist who works with the Manufacturing Futures Institute team to grow external engagement and investment in MFI research, with particular strategic emphasis on the role of data, digital twin technologies, and artificial intelligence on the future of manufacturing. He brings over 30 years of industry experience in advanced manufacturing, manufacturing technology, and R&D from his tenure at Alcoa and Arconic, where he led additive manufacturing strategy and product development for aerospace, defense, and automotive markets.

name: Chris Hendrickson
title: Hamerschlag University Professor Emeritus
bio: Chris Hendrickson is a renowned expert in engineering planning and management, with research spanning transportation systems, construction project management, and environmental life cycle assessment. His work emphasizes systems-wide perspectives and the integration of engineering and management considerations, with particular focus on sustainable design and green infrastructure.

name: Christopher Hertz
title: Academic Advisor
bio: Christopher Hertz directs the MechE Academic Team and manages mechanical engineering advisors. He advises Ph.D. students and supports them through all phases of the doctoral process.

name: Hanan Hibshi
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Hanan Hibshi is an assistant teaching professor at the Information Networking Institute who studies usable security and privacy, cybersecurity education, and the application of machine learning and AI to security challenges. Her research focuses on understanding how security experts make decisions and developing intelligent tools to better accommodate their needs, with particular expertise in security requirements, mobile/IoT security, and empirical data-driven approaches.

name: Alex Hills
title: Distinguished Service Professor
bio: Alex Hills is a pioneering researcher in wireless networking who built the world's first big Wi-Fi network, known as Wireless Andrew, which became the prototype for Wi-Fi networks globally. His current research focuses on low Earth orbiting satellite technology and its application to provide broadband service in rural and remote areas of the world.

name: Jen Hitchcock
bio: Jen Hitchcock assists students, faculty, and staff with the fabrication process at the TechSpark makerspace. She teaches courses in jewelry, welding, laser cutting, woodworking, and rapid prototyping.

name: James C. Hoe
title: Professor
bio: James Hoe is a Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering interested in computer architecture and digital hardware design, including FPGA architecture, digital signal processing hardware, and high-level hardware design synthesis. He is a Fellow of IEEE and conducts research in hardware security, network security, and energy-efficient computing systems.

name: Kenneth Holstein
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Kenneth Holstein's research focuses on participatory and expertise-driven approaches to AI design, development, and evaluation, with particular interest in AI's impacts on human workers. He develops methods and tools to incorporate diverse human expertise across the AI development lifecycle, designing systems that respect and elevate human work.

name: Trish Hredzak
title: Research Lab Manager
bio: Trish Hredzak is the research lab manager for the Department of Chemical Engineering, where she leads department-wide efforts to strengthen safe, sustainable, and high-performing research operations. She supports consistent training, compliance, and responsible research practices across the department's extensive shared lab network.

name: Olivia Hsu
title: Assistant Professor
department: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Olivia Hsu works on hardware and software systems that efficiently run complex, data-dependent applications on next-generation domain-specific hardware. Her research spans computer architecture, programming systems, compilers, and programming languages with a focus on making domain-specific hardware accessible and efficient.

name: Ellen Hughes
bio: Ellen Hughes brings extensive industry and academic experience spanning roles at major organizations including Northrop Grumman, General Motors, and VeyTel. Her research focuses on computer vision, machine learning, AI, and medical imaging, with a strong emphasis on building partnerships between government, academia, and industry.

name: Andrew Ilyas
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Andrew Ilyas works to uncover general principles that describe and predict the behavior of machine learning systems, combining statistical tools with large-scale experiments to understand the ML pipeline from data collection to deployment. His research also explores human trust in AI systems and aims to enable predictably reliable future ML systems.

name: Mika Inamahoro
title: Associate Director of Student Affairs
bio: Mika Inamahoro serves as the Associate Director of Student Affairs at CMU-Africa, managing student requests and ensuring a positive experience for students. She brings extensive experience in finance and administrative support from her work in non-governmental organizations.

name: Raissa Ineza
title: Education Network Program Manager
bio: Raissa Ineza is an education network program manager who builds and maintains relationships with African Engineering and Technology Network partners. She brings extensive project management experience from her work in higher education, including roles at the University of Global Health Equity and CMU.

name: Aharon Inspektor
title: Adjunct Professor
department: Department of Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Aharon Inspektor is an adjunct professor specializing in plasma-assisted deposition processes, hard coatings, and surface engineering technologies. His research bridges fundamental plasma physics with industrial applications in aerospace, automotive, and manufacturing sectors.

name: Sarah Jacobsen
title: Outreach Coordinator
bio: Sarah Jacobsen is the outreach coordinator for the College of Engineering at Carnegie Mellon University, responsible for organizing outreach opportunities for schools and after-school programs in Pittsburgh. She works to improve STEM achievement and exposure by collaborating with teachers and community partners to provide high-quality engineering lessons.

name: Annette Jacobson
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Annette Jacobson is a teaching professor of chemical engineering who directed the Colloids, Polymers and Surfaces Program, a joint initiative between the Mellon College of Science and the College of Engineering. Her research focuses on complex fluids engineering with applications in cosmetics formulation and polymer science.

name: Quinn Jacobson
title: Professor of the Practice
bio: Quinn Jacobson is a professor of the practice at the Information Networking Institute creating programs to teach entrepreneurship and execution to technical cofounders. His research focuses on secure distributed systems, neuromorphic computing, and domain-specific accelerators, informed by extensive industry experience at startups and technology companies.

name: Paulina Jaramillo
title: Professor
bio: Paulina Jaramillo is a professor of engineering and public policy whose research focuses on the social, economic, and environmental implications of policy-driven changes in energy systems, with particular emphasis on climate change and sustainable energy access in the Global South. Her work integrates life cycle assessment, energy planning, and development to create knowledge that addresses global energy needs while accounting for local conditions and advancing equity.

name: Reeja Jayan
title: Professor
department: Mechanical Engineering, Electrical and Computer Engineering, Materials Science & Engineering
bio: Reeja Jayan is a professor exploring how electromagnetic fields can control materials synthesis and energy storage behavior, with research spanning battery interface engineering, circular manufacturing, and AI-guided materials discovery. She founded SeaLion Energy Inc., a CMU spinout advancing polymer coatings that extend battery life and enable regeneration of lithium-ion cells, and has led a research portfolio exceeding $22 million while mentoring over 50 researchers.

name: Coty Jen
title: Associate Professor
bio: Coty Jen studies how chemical composition and physical properties of atmospheric aerosol particles influence air quality and climate by developing semi-empirical models and novel measurement techniques. Her research aims to predict how human activities will alter air quality and understand the role of natural emissions, such as from phytoplankton and seaweed, in atmospheric processes.

name: Anita Jesionowski
title: Interim Chief Partnership Officer - Research and Government Relations
bio: Anita Jesionowski is the Interim Chief Partnership Officer for Research and Government Relations in the College of Engineering, working with faculty on partnerships and funding opportunities. She brings extensive experience from twelve years at the Center for Technology Transfer and Enterprise Creation, focusing on intellectual property, licensing, and startup creation.

name: Su Jiang
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Su Jiang's research focuses on scientific machine learning, uncertainty quantification, and optimization for subsurface flow processes, integrating AI methods with domain knowledge to enable efficient prediction and management in large-scale energy and environmental systems. Her applications include geological carbon storage, hydrocarbon production, enhanced geothermal systems, and seawater intrusion.

name: Audrey Jin
title: Administrative Coordinator
bio: Audrey Jin is an Administrative Coordinator at CMU-Silicon Valley who supports campus operations and student affairs through coordination, communication, and cross-campus support. She facilitates I-9 verification processes and helps create a welcoming environment for students and staff.

name: Carlee Joe-Wong
title: Professor
bio: Carlee Joe-Wong is a professor interested in the optimization of networked systems with a focus on incentives and user behavior, including work on smart data pricing and fair resource allocation. She leads the LIONS research group and applies theoretical insights to practical system deployments in computer and information networks.

name: Aaron Johnson
title: Professor
bio: Aaron Johnson researches intelligent robot-environment interactions with a focus on deploying robots in real-world applications beyond labs and factories. His work spans novel robot design, behavior design, physics-based planning, and bio-inspired robotics, with extensive field testing in diverse environments including deserts, power plants, and military bases.

name: Ryan F. Johnson
title: Associate Professor
bio: Ryan Johnson develops predictive computational models for propulsion and reacting-flow systems, focusing on scalable prediction capabilities for complex multiscale problems. His research spans computational fluid dynamics, chemical kinetics, GPU-enabled high-performance computing, and embedded machine learning to enable accurate simulation of real propulsion and energy devices.

name: Christa Jones
bio: Christa Jones leads marketing and events strategy for the Institute for Software Research, developing and implementing communications initiatives that advance the institute's mission. She manages brand positioning, strategic messaging, and event planning to support the institute's goals and visibility.

name: Trevor J. Jones
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Trevor Jones' research applies soft matter mechanics to transform simple mechanical inputs into complex assembly and control of materials for manufacturing and robotics applications. His work harnesses interfacial fluid mechanics, flexible solid mechanics, and granular matter to develop new technologies inspired by natural phenomena.

name: Gauri Joshi
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Gauri Joshi is interested in stochastic modeling and analysis that provides sharp insights into the design of cloud and machine learning infrastructure. Her research group, affiliated with the Parallel Data Lab at Carnegie Mellon, focuses on making machine learning algorithms more robust and developing data-aware and system-aware algorithms for distributed machine learning.

name: Julie Kailath
title: Student Services Coordinator
bio: Julie supports student services and engagement at CMU-SV through creative, community-driven initiatives focused on health, wellness, and social connection. With 18 years of experience in learning and development, she creates vibrant, inclusive communities where students can thrive.

name: Rosine Kamahoro
bio: Rosine Kamahoro is passionate about education and capacity development with extensive experience in development, communication, and technology. She holds a master's degree in international studies with a major in development cooperation.

name: Inseung Kang
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Inseung Kang is an assistant professor whose research program tackles enhancing human mobility using robotics, wearables, and artificial intelligence, with a focus on developing wearable assistive devices such as robotic exoskeletons for individuals with motor impairments. His team develops versatile mechatronic systems and generalizable AI-based robot controllers, integrating mechatronic design, deep learning, control theory, gait biomechanics, motor control, and motor learning.

name: Soummya Kar
title: Buhl Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Soummya Kar is the Buhl Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering whose research focuses on decision-making in large-scale networked systems, stochastic systems, multi-agent systems and data science with applications in cyber-physical systems and smart energy systems. He is a Fellow of the IEEE and actively contributes to energy innovation and cybersecurity research.

name: Levent Burak Kara
title: Professor
bio: L. Burak Kara develops computational analysis, design, and manufacturing technologies combining machine learning, optimization, and geometric modeling for applications in CAD, topology optimization, additive manufacturing, and bioengineering. His recent work applies deep learning to replace expensive simulations, automatically generate novel products from past designs, and optimize electronic chip design using reinforcement learning.

name: Valerie Karplus
title: Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Valerie Karplus studies resource and environmental management with a focus on institutions and management practices in organizations across diverse contexts, particularly in energy and climate policy. Her research spans innovation in corporate supply chains, workforce revitalization, and integrated design of public policies for energy transitions and industrial decarbonization.

name: Amandine Kayizari
bio: Amandine Kayizari is a seasoned talent acquisition and stakeholder management professional with seven years of experience in the field. With a background in business administration, she excels at building strong relationships and ensuring the right people are in the right roles to drive success.

name: Gregory Kesden
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Gregory Kesden is an associate teaching professor specializing in software systems, including distributed systems, networks, cloud computing, and operating systems. He has extensive experience in teaching and educational leadership, recognized with multiple awards for teaching excellence.

name: Aditya Khair
title: Professor
bio: Aditya Khair is a professor of chemical engineering who uses applied mathematical techniques to investigate problems in fluid mechanics, rheology, colloid science, electrokinetics, and electrochemistry. His research has been recognized through multiple awards including the Metzner Early Career Award, NSF CAREER Award, and fellowship in the American Physical Society.

name: Taha Khan
title: Faculty
bio: Taha Khan's research focuses on usable security and privacy, measuring cybercrime, trustworthy AI, and online censorship, with an emphasis on understanding how people interact with security systems. He designs tools that are secure and robust, and brings a dynamic, active-learning approach to teaching courses ranging from introductory programming to systems, security, networks, and databases.

name: Deogratius Kiggudde
bio: Deogratius Kiggudde is a development professional with extensive experience in open data infrastructure and humanitarian projects across Africa, having led initiatives with organizations like the Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team and partners including the World Bank and USAID. His work focuses on integrating open technology and digital literacy programs to empower communities and foster sustainable development across the African continent.

name: Hyong Kim
title: Drew D. Perkins Chaired Professor
bio: Hyong Kim is a chaired professor whose research focuses on advanced switch architectures, fault-tolerant and secure network systems, and distributed computing. He has founded multiple networking startups and holds numerous patents in networking and computing technologies.

name: Wanjira Kinuthia
title: Associate Professor
bio: Wanjira Kinuthia is an associate professor dedicated to enhancing learning design technology and curriculum development through research, publications, teaching, and service. Her work focuses on leveraging technology to improve educational outcomes, with international experience in projects with organizations including the Mastercard Foundation, African Development Bank, UNICEF, and UNHCR.

name: John Kitchin
title: Professor
bio: John Kitchin works at the intersection of machine learning, data science, and scientific programming with science and engineering, developing software for modeling materials and solving engineering problems. His research focuses on modeling catalysts with applications in energy, solving inverse problems in engineering, and developing surrogate models for engineering systems.

name: Philip Koopman
title: Associate Professor
bio: Philip Koopman is an associate professor in Electrical and Computer Engineering who leads research on safe and secure embedded systems with over 20 years of experience in autonomous vehicle safety. His recent work focuses on stress testing and runtime monitoring to ensure safety for vehicle and robotic applications across research, industry, and defense sectors.

name: Amanda R. Krause
title: Associate Professor
bio: Amanda Krause is an associate professor whose research focuses on engineering grain boundaries and interfaces to improve the mechanical performance, degradation response, and thermal properties of ceramics used in extreme environments. Her work employs advanced characterization techniques including 3D X-ray imaging and electron microscopy to understand microstructural phenomena in high-performance materials.

name: Ramayya Krishnan
title: Dean of Heinz College, W. W. Cooper and Ruth F. Cooper Professor of Management Science and Information Systems
bio: Ramayya Krishnan is the dean of Heinz College and investigates risk management in business process design and information security, large scale social network analysis, and policies balancing data access with privacy protection. His research focuses on the technological, business, and policy aspects of internet-enabled systems and the use of IT to create and capture organizational value.

name: Bruce H. Krogh
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Bruce Krogh is professor emeritus of electrical and computer engineering whose career focused on designing and implementing algorithms and embedded software for control of robotic, automotive, aeronautical, energy, and manufacturing automation systems. He currently serves as digital engagement lead for the Center for Inclusive Digital Transformation of Africa at CMU-Africa, having previously founded and directed CMU-Africa and led the IEEE ContinuED initiative.

name: Jackie Kulfan
title: Research Collaborations Manager
bio: Jackie Kulfan engages with government, national lab, and industry partners to foster research collaborations with CMU faculty within the Scott Institute for Energy Innovation and the Chemical Engineering Department. With 26 years of experience at PPG in industrial coatings research, materials development, and research partnerships, she brings expertise in vehicle lightweighting, photovoltaics, building envelope technologies, and advanced manufacturing processes.

name: Cynthia Kuo
title: Associate Professor of the Practice
bio: Cynthia Kuo is an associate professor of the practice who brings industry experience as a startup founder and product lead, using sensors to invent new, impactful technologies. Her research focuses on product design, sensors, and usable security, with notable contributions including the design of Google Safe Browsing and the co-founding of Vibrado for smart apparel technology.

name: Carl D. Laird
title: Professor
bio: Carl Laird leads an internationally recognized research program in process systems engineering, known for contributions in high-performance computing techniques for large-scale nonlinear optimization and open-source software development. His research addresses non-traditional, high-impact areas including public health, homeland security, and critical infrastructure and energy systems.

name: Haoyong Lan
title: Engineering Faculty Librarian
bio: Haoyong Lan is the engineering faculty librarian who specializes in artificial intelligence literacy education and systematic review methodology, serving as co-chair of CMU Libraries' Advance AI Literacy Task Force. His work bridges engineering education, information science, and emerging technology integration to enhance research capacity and develop comprehensive AI literacy frameworks for the CMU community.

name: Kurt Larsen
title: Assistant Dean of Undergraduate Studies
bio: Kurt Larsen serves as Assistant Dean of Undergraduate Studies, advising approximately 180 first-year engineering students annually and coordinating experiential learning initiatives. He teaches courses in professional writing, first-year seminar, and leadership development, focusing on student acclimation, major exploration, and skill-building.

name: David E. Laughlin
title: Emeritus Professor
bio: David Laughlin's research focuses on investigating material structure through transmission electron microscopy and x-ray diffraction, with particular emphasis on phase transformations and microstructure analysis. For the past thirty years, he has concentrated on magnetic properties and microstructure of soft magnets, hard magnets, and magnetic thin films for recording media applications.

name: Polycarp Laurent
title: Data Center Engineer
bio: Polycarp Laurent is a data center engineer joining CMU-Africa with extensive experience in IT data center technologies, network and systems engineering. He has hands-on expertise in designing, deploying, and troubleshooting complex computing environments including IP networks, security devices, and Linux servers across multiple vendors.

name: Philip LeDuc
title: William J. Brown Professor
bio: Philip LeDuc investigates cells and molecules as mechanical systems, applying fundamental engineering approaches to understand biological phenomena ranging from mammalian cells to developmental biology. His research spans basic science and applied work in biomechanics, tissue engineering, and renewable energy systems including algae and bacterial fuel cells.

name: Liza Lepczyk
title: Program Manager
bio: Liza Lepczyk is the program manager for the Next Manufacturing Center, one of the world's leading research centers for additive manufacturing and 3D printing. She also manages the Institute for Model-based Qualification and Certification of Additive Manufacturing (IMQCAM), a NASA Space Technology Research Institute project focused on developing digital twin platforms for metal additive manufacturing quality assurance.

name: Jiaoyang Li
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Jiaoyang Li develops fundamental algorithms that enable large teams of autonomous agents to accomplish collaborative tasks intelligently in dynamic environments. Her research applications include mobile robot coordination for automated warehousing, drone swarm control, multi-arm assembly, and traffic management systems.

name: Qing Li
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Qing Li develops optical signal processing technologies and photonic devices, including chip-scale quantum frequency conversion and microresonator frequency combs. His research spans silicon and silicon nitride platforms for optical frequency synthesis and photonic interfaces.

name: Fernando V. Lima
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Fernando Lima's research group develops and implements process systems engineering methods for process design, intensification, and advanced control with applications to energy systems and sustainability. His work focuses on dynamic optimization and modular energy systems to address challenges in decarbonization and environmental sustainability.

name: Michael Lisanti
title: Senior Director of Partnerships
bio: Michael Lisanti is the senior director of partnerships at CyLab, leading the business engagement program that builds relationships between corporate and government partners with CMU faculty and researchers. He has designed engagement models for strategic initiatives including CyLab-Africa, CyLab Robotics Security and Privacy, and the CyLab Venture Network.

name: Shawn Litster
title: Professor
bio: Shawn Litster is a professor of mechanical engineering whose research focuses on micro- and nanoscale transport phenomena in energy conversion technologies, particularly fuel cells, batteries, and ultra-capacitors where electrochemistry and electrokinetics are critical. His work addresses sustainable energy technologies for decarbonization and electrification of transportation, including hydrogen fuel systems and advanced vehicle powertrains.

name: Tze Meng Low
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Tze Meng Low's research focuses on the systematic derivation and implementation of high-performance algorithms through formal methods and analytical models. His work aims to achieve performance portability across architectures and domains by understanding the interaction between software algorithms and hardware features.

name: Gregory Lowry
title: Hamerschlag University Professor
bio: Greg Lowry is a Hamerschlag University Professor whose environmental nanotechnology and chemistry research aims to improve the efficiency and resilience of crop agriculture, environmental remediation, and water treatment.
