He is a highly cited scientist in ecology and environment with over 230 publications and is a Fellow of both the AAAS and the Association of Environmental Engineering and Science Professors.

name: Simon Lucey
title: Associate Research Professor
bio: Simon Lucey is an associate research professor in the Robotics Institute at Carnegie Mellon University and leader of the CI2CV Lab, focusing on computer vision and robotics research. His work emphasizes deep understanding of vision systems and their practical applications, informed by his prior experience as a Principal Research Scientist at Australia's CSIRO.

name: Edith Luhanga
title: Assistant Research Professor
bio: Edith Luhanga designs theory-based behavior change systems that consider people's personal and socio-cultural contexts through a human-centered approach. Her research focuses on health, financial inclusion, and privacy and security, with particular emphasis on digital interventions in African contexts.

name: Kenneth Mai
title: Principal Systems Scientist
bio: Kenneth Mai's research focuses on scaling process technologies into the nanometer regime and designing efficient, high-performance digital blocks such as memories and functional units for future generation technologies. His work addresses technology constraints like interconnect delay and device leakage while building reliable computation systems that can handle emerging applications in sensor networks and computational biology.

name: Carmel Majidi
title: Professor
bio: Carmel Majidi's research focuses on discovering materials, hardware architectures, and fabrication methods that enable robots and machines to behave like soft biological organisms while remaining safe for human contact. His group develops filled-elastomer composites and soft microfluidic systems that function as artificial skin, nervous tissue, and muscle for soft robotics and wearables applications.

name: Sandra Malaika
bio: Sandra Malaika brings over a decade of experience in international development program management, having led economic and governance initiatives at the Foreign and Commonwealth Development Office. She plays a key role at CMU-Africa in impact measurement, evaluation, and the Mastercard Foundation Scholars program, driving transformative education and meaningful change.

name: Jonathan Malen
title: Professor
bio: Jonathan Malen seeks fundamental understanding of thermal transport processes from atomistic to macroscopic scales in advanced materials and technologies. The Malen Laboratory leverages ultrafast laser techniques, micro/nanofabrication, and thermal imaging approaches to measure thermal properties and processes in applications ranging from thermal management in high-powered electronics to evaporative cooling and organic-inorganic materials.

name: Stacy Marshall
title: Facilities and Events Manager
bio: Stacy manages building maintenance and campus spaces at CMU-SV, ensuring everything runs smoothly behind the scenes. She supports students by providing logistical assistance for events they plan on campus, ensuring seamless execution from setup to completion.

name: Deanna Matthews
title: Teaching Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Deanna Matthews is a teaching professor who educates students on the complexity of real-world problems related to technology, policy, and society. She teaches courses in engineering, science and public policy, project management, and analysis methods, while also serving as associate department head for undergraduate affairs.

name: Theresa S. Mayer
title: Vice President for Research
department: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering; Department of Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Theresa Mayer is Carnegie Mellon's vice president for research and holds joint faculty appointments in Electrical and Computer Engineering and Materials Science and Engineering. She is internationally recognized for her research in nanotechnology applications to electronic and photonic devices, with expertise in directed and self-assembly of nanoparticles for novel material integration and device structures.

name: Christopher McComb
title: Faculty Member
bio: Christopher McComb's research focuses on human social systems in design and engineering, machine learning for engineering design, and human-AI collaboration and teaming. His work is funded by NSF, DARPA, and private corporations, with applications ranging from STEM education to advanced manufacturing.

name: Alan McGaughey
title: Professor
bio: Alan McGaughey leads the Nanoscale Transport Phenomena Laboratory, which seeks to understand the transport of mass, momentum, and energy at the atomic level by studying phonons, photons, electrons, and fluid particles. His research uses molecular- and meso-scale simulation techniques and collaborates with experimental groups to advance thermal transport science and energy-efficient materials.

name: Margaret Harding McGill
title: Director of Policy Engagement
department: Office of the Vice President for Research
bio: Margaret McGill directs policy engagement in the Office of the Vice President for Research, connecting Carnegie Mellon research to decision-makers in the policy ecosystem with a focus on energy and technology policy. She brings extensive experience from federal service at NTIA and technology policy journalism to support research translation and impact.

name: Thomas Fox McManus
bio: Thomas McManus is an educational technology expert with over 25 years of experience in higher education across multiple countries and institutions. He specializes in improving program and course design, implementing advanced learning technologies, and collaborating with faculty to enhance teaching and learning effectiveness.

name: Gladys M. Mercier
title: Faculty
bio: Gladys Mercier brings 12 years of software engineering experience in industrial automation systems for steel manufacturing, with expertise in project management and team leadership. She has been instrumental in developing and teaching project management courses and growing the M.S. in Software Management program at Carnegie Mellon.

name: Eva B. Mergner
title: Academic Advisor
bio: Eva Mergner advises undergraduate students in the Mechanical Engineering department and serves as a resource for curriculum, courses, and university resources. She supports student success and is involved in community engagement initiatives focused on education and professional development.

name: Jeremy J. Michalek
title: Professor
department: Mechanical Engineering and Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Jeremy Michalek is a professor who focuses on vehicle electrification including technology, life cycle assessment, consumer behavior, and public policy, as well as design decision-making including systems optimization and techno-economic analysis. He is the founding director of the Design Decisions Laboratory and founding co-director of the Vehicle Electrification Group.

name: David C. Miller
bio: David Miller is a technical leader with extensive expertise in computational modeling and design of complex energy and industrial systems, having founded and led the Institute for the Design of Advanced Energy Systems (IDAES). His research focuses on next-generation approaches to accelerate the development and deployment of integrated energy systems through advanced simulation and optimization techniques.

name: Niloofar Mireshghallah
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Niloofar Mireshghallah is an assistant professor researching the intersection of privacy, natural language processing, and societal implications of machine learning. Her work explores the interplay between data, its influence on models, and the expectations of people who regulate and use these models.

name: Prasenjit Mitra
title: Professor
bio: Prasenjit Mitra's research focuses on natural language processing, resource-constrained large language models for low-resource languages, and applications of LLMs in scientific discovery and applied machine learning. With over 25 years of experience, he has worked extensively on extracting and utilizing information from text for digital libraries, disaster relief, recommendation systems, and spatial information processing.

name: Axel Moore
bio: Axel Moore specializes in biomaterials design and tissue engineering for bone and cartilage applications, with expertise in soft hydrated materials mechanics and fluid recovery. His research focuses on developing robotic approaches for the prevention and treatment of joint disorders, including in vivo models of spinal deformity correction.

name: Joe Moore
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Joe Moore teaches across the Civil and Environmental Engineering program with a focus on active and interdisciplinary engineering education. His research interests include environmental microbiology, climate-resilient environmental systems, and the interactions between engineered nanomaterials and bacteria.

name: M. Granger Morgan
title: Hamerschlag University Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: M. Granger Morgan is a Hamerschlab University Professor whose research addresses science, technology, and public policy with a focus on energy, electric power, environmental systems, climate change, and risk analysis. He co-directs the NSF Center for Climate and Energy Decision Making and has developed methods to characterize and treat uncertainty in quantitative policy analysis.

name: José M. F. Moura
title: Philip L. and Marsha Dowd University Professor
bio: José Moura is a distinguished researcher in signal processing, data science, and cyberphysical systems with significant contributions to both theoretical and practical applications. His work has impacted industry globally, including technology used in billions of hard disk drives, and he has been recognized with numerous awards from IEEE and the National Academy of Engineering.

name: Flavia Mugire
title: Admissions and Enrollment Officer
bio: Flavia Mugire is the Admissions and Enrollment Officer at Carnegie Mellon University Africa, overseeing application verification, evaluation, assessment, and enrollment services. With expertise in business administration, procurement, and logistics management, she provides administrative support and customer service to prospective students and faculty.

name: Edwin Mugume
bio: Edwin Mugume's research focuses on developing deployment strategies for green heterogeneous cellular networks, 5G technologies, and energy harvesting approaches using stochastic geometry. He combines academic expertise in wireless network optimization with industry experience in radio frequency planning and mobile cellular network deployment.

name: Fulgence Muhirwa
bio: Fulgence Muhirwa is a tech enthusiast with expertise in networks, audiovisual systems, systems design, and software development. He has led IT teams, managed network infrastructure, and overseen the implementation of campus access controls and security systems.

name: Carine Pierrette Mukamakuza
title: Lecturer
bio: Carine Mukamakuza is a lecturer and researcher focused on digital healthcare solutions and machine learning, with particular expertise in recommender systems and data science. She is developing AI-powered digital detection projects for diseases like malaria and rheumatic heart disease, with a focus on creating scalable systems for African countries.

name: Mukooza Peter Mukasa
bio: Mukooza Peter Mukasa is a seasoned data analyst with a robust background in telecommunications, network security, and machine learning. He has held pivotal roles at Bandwidth and Cloud Services Group and Eskom Limited, focusing on network security and data management.

name: Stanley Mukasa
bio: Stanley Mukasa has nearly a decade of experience designing and managing entrepreneurship development programs, with a focus on supporting African entrepreneurs in creating sustainable ventures. His work integrates systems thinking and design thinking approaches to help entrepreneurs with business strategy, market analysis, and fundraising.

name: Tamal Mukherjee
title: Research Engineer and Assistant Director
bio: Tamal Mukherjee is a research engineer and assistant director of the Center for Electronic Design Automation, specializing in CAD tools for analog circuit design and automated synthesis of MEMS structures. His research focuses on numerical optimization algorithms, circuit operating environment variations, and the development of design automation tools for various types of systems.

name: Mike Mulindwa Ssempa
bio: Mike Mulindwa Ssempa brings over 15 years of experience in higher education with a strong track record of driving innovation and expanding access across East Africa. His expertise lies in leveraging data, technology, and partnerships to connect talented students with academic opportunities and facilitate entrepreneurial initiatives.

name: Nicholas Muller
title: Lester and Judith Lave Professor of Economics, Engineering, and Public Policy
bio: Nicholas Muller is an environmental economist who studies the intersection of environmental policy and economics, with research focusing on quantifying air pollution and climate damages, estimating discount rates and risk preferences, and measuring inequality in income measures. He teaches microeconomics and environmental economics, with publications in top venues including the American Economic Review, Science, and the Proceedings of the National Academies of Science.

name: Dan Munyakazi Rwamugenza
title: IT Engineer
bio: Dan Munyakazi Rwamugenza is an IT engineer with diverse knowledge in information communication technology and experience in academic and corporate settings. He manages servers, networking infrastructure, and IT support while designing and planning new computing services infrastructure.

name: Hironobu Murata
title: Researcher
bio: Hironobu Murata is a researcher who has been working in the Russell lab since 2004 on developing innovative methods for linking synthetic compounds to biomaterials. His current work focuses on enzyme-polymer hybrid materials for therapeutic applications and the design of multifunctional cell-surface reactive polymers.

name: Andrew Armstrong Musoke
bio: Andrew Musoke is a digital infrastructure specialist with over seven years of experience architecting and scaling cloud-native, secure systems across Africa. He is passionate about sustainable and resilient digital public infrastructure and co-leads the Digital Identity Hackathon Africa.

name: William Mutero
title: Director
department: Center for Inclusive Digital Transformation of Africa
bio: William Mutero is the director of the Center for Inclusive Digital Transformation of Africa (CIDTA), bringing 15 years of experience in consulting, medical, pharmaceutical, and international civil society organizations. He is dedicated to fostering high-performing teams and driving Africa's digital transformation through strategic planning, program implementation, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

name: Ramadhan Juma Mwatima
title: Full Stack Developer
bio: Ramadhan Juma Mwatima is a full stack developer at Carnegie Mellon University Africa who designs and implements both front-end and back-end software systems. He conducts research on new technologies, manages student developers, and focuses on automating business processes to improve institutional efficiency.

name: Bill Nace
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Bill Nace is a teaching professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department where he teaches courses in Digital Logic, FPGAs, Computer Networks, and Distributed Systems. His research interests focus on digital design and verification, with extensive experience from over 25 years of service in the US Air Force.

name: Yorie Nakahira
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Yorie Nakahira is an Assistant Professor studying the fundamental theory of optimization, control, and learning with applications spanning neuroscience, cell biology, smart grids, cloud computing, finance, and autonomous robots. She directs the Control and Learning Group and holds affiliations with the Manufacturing Futures Institute.

name: Priya Narasimhan
title: Professor
bio: Priya Narasimhan is a professor of Electrical & Computer Engineering whose research focuses on dependable distributed systems, fault-tolerance, embedded systems, and mobile systems. She serves as the academic lead of the Intel Science and Technology Center in Embedded Computing and is the CEO and Founder of YinzCam, Inc., a CMU spin-off company specializing in mobile live streaming and scalable video technologies for professional sports.

name: Sneha Prabha Narra
title: Associate Professor
bio: Sneha Prabha Narra is an associate professor of mechanical engineering whose research focuses on advancing metals additive manufacturing technology through digital twins, machine learning, and materials characterization. She leads the EMIT Lab and is actively involved in organizing additive manufacturing symposia and workshops, serving as Associate Editor of the Additive Manufacturing journal.

name: Jema David Ndibwile
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Jema Ndibwile is an assistant teaching professor in cybersecurity at Carnegie Mellon University with expertise in ethical hacking, cryptography, and digital forensics. His research focuses on usable privacy and security, hacking countermeasures, and the impact of artificial and human intelligence on cybersecurity.

name: Rohit Negi
title: Professor
bio: Rohit Negi is a Professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department whose research encompasses signal processing, coding for communication systems, information theory, and networking. His work extends to power grid analysis, wireless networks, and cyberphysical systems with applications to energy and smart infrastructure.

name: Alexandra Newby
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Alexandra Newby is an assistant teaching professor in chemical engineering with research experience spanning computational and experimental work in environmental and biomedical fields. Her expertise combines chemical engineering fundamentals with applications in environmental remediation and biomedical systems.

name: Mikel K. Ngueajio
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Mikel Ngueajio is a Ph.D. candidate in computer science whose doctoral research bridges hate speech and misinformation by developing explainable AI systems to mitigate online harm. She teaches Python Programming for Data Analytics at Carnegie Mellon University Africa and has published research in top-tier venues including ACM Computing Surveys, AAAI, ACL, and EMNLP.

name: Sarah Nielsen
title: TechSpark Fabrication Instructor
bio: Sarah Nielsen is a TechSpark Fabrication Instructor who teaches woodshop principles and CNC router techniques. She provides expertise in rapid prototyping, fabrication processes, and project design guidance across multiple making technologies.

name: Tagbo H.R. Niepa
title: Associate Professor
bio: Tagbo Niepa's research focuses on three main areas: developing physical-chemical alternatives to antibiotics for drug-resistant pathogens, investigating interfacial biofilms where bacteria generate new materials, and replicating the human microbiome through microbial encapsulation. His work combines chemical engineering principles with biomedical applications to address major challenges in infection control and microbial research.

name: Aline Ngira Nkugire
bio: Aline Ngira Nkugire brings over five years of experience in logistics, operations, and procurement, with expertise in creating and implementing procurement processes. Her work spans contract negotiation, quality assurance, financial oversight, and operational leadership.

name: Kizito Nkurikiyeyezu
title: Associate Professor
bio: Kizito Nkurikiyeyezu is an associate professor focused on developing technology-driven solutions to healthcare challenges in Rwanda and across Africa. His research spans thermal comfort provision systems, intelligent air-conditioning design, and biomedical applications including early cancer detection through non-invasive methods.

name: Destenie Nock
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Destenie Nock uses mathematical modeling tools to address societal problems related to sustainability planning, energy policy, and engineering for social good, with a focus on energy equity and access. Her research spans energy systems modeling, multi-criteria decision analysis, and applied optimization to help policymakers understand energy planning options.

name: Evariste Nsengumuremyi
bio: Evariste Nsengumuremyi is an experienced professional in project monitoring, evaluation, and research with over 10 years of experience strengthening institutional capacity across multi-stakeholder programs. He currently administers collaborative research projects across the Africa Engineering and Technology Network (Afretec) and works to establish policies and governance frameworks for CMU-Africa research programs.

name: Bernard Ntezimana
bio: Bernard Ntezimana is an ACCA-certified accountant with 15+ years of experience in grants management, institutional finance, and compliance across international organizations. He specializes in budget management, expense administration, and mentoring programs for business startups.

name: Elyse Nzayinambaho
bio: Nzayinambaho brings over five years of EdTech experience focused on transformative initiatives in product development, e-learning course design, and instructional innovation. He is deeply committed to improving Africa's education systems by integrating technology-enhanced learning into course and instructional design, with emphasis on developing online and hybrid courses using technical-based approaches.

name: Johnson Nziza
title: Associate Director of IT
bio: Johnson Nziza is the associate director of IT at Carnegie Mellon University Africa with nine years of experience managing computing services policies, IT infrastructure design, and user support escalation. His background combines electrical engineering with information systems expertise, with previous experience in telecommunications and satellite communications.

name: Jason O'Connor
title: Senior Research Scientist
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Jason O'Connor is a senior research scientist whose research interests lie at the intersection of the economics of innovation and industrial organization. He works on energy storage demonstration projects and technology assessment programs, bringing experience from his prior roles at the Federal Trade Commission and Medicaid Research Center.

name: Thomas O'Connor
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Thomas O'Connor's research applies molecular and continuum modeling and simulation to design sustainable polymer materials and improve soft material manufacturing methods. His group uses computational methods including molecular dynamics and lattice-Boltzmann methods to understand nanoscale structure and dynamics of polymers, gels, and nanocomposites, with particular interest in bio-inspired polymer manufacturing.

name: Francis Ogoke
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Francis Ogoke develops physics-informed deep learning methods to accelerate simulation-based insights and creates probabilistic frameworks for uncertainty analysis in engineering processes. His research focuses on building foundational models for advanced manufacturing, sensing, cyber-physical systems, and digital twins through generalizable representation learning frameworks.

name: George Okeyo
title: Assistant Professor
bio: George Okeyo brings over 16 years of experience in teaching and research, with expertise in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and Internet of Things applications. His research focuses on semantic web technologies and their application in smart environments, with a commitment to ICT for development in Sub-Saharan Africa.

name: Leigh Oliveras
title: Career Consultant
bio: Leigh serves as a career consultant at CMU-SV, supporting graduate students in their professional development through individualized coaching, advising, and interactive workshops. She leads the Peer Career Consultant team and facilitates connections between students and mentors in their fields of interest.

name: George Ooko Abong
title: Associate Professor
bio: George Ooko Abong is a food systems and nutrition expert with extensive experience at the intersection of research, higher education, public health, and food policy in Africa. His research addresses critical challenges in African food systems including food composition, dietary exposure, aflatoxin contamination, and industrially produced trans-fatty acids.

name: Kwadwo Osseo-Asare
title: Professor
bio: Kwadwo Osseo-Asare is a globally recognized expert in interfacial phenomena, dissolution, and solvent extraction processes, with pioneering contributions to aqueous materials processing and metal extraction. His research emphasizes thermodynamic modeling, electrochemistry, colloids, interfaces, and nanoscience applications in hydrometallurgy, separation science, and materials synthesis.

name: Fethiye Ozis
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Fethiye Ozis is an assistant teaching professor who conducts research on engineering classrooms and innovative pedagogical strategies while teaching across the curriculum. Her research interests span biotechnology for air pollution control, climate-resilient environmental systems, and sustainable water treatment methods.

name: Samuel Nascimento Pagliarini
bio: Samuel Nascimento Pagliarini's research focuses on applications of security and privacy across embedded systems, hardware, and IoT devices. His work addresses cybersecurity challenges in resource-constrained and connected environments.

name: Rachelle Palchesko
title: Faculty
bio: Rachelle Palchesko is a faculty member in the Department of Biomedical Engineering at Carnegie Mellon University and a member of the Bioengineered Organs Initiative. Her research focuses on bioprinting and tissue engineering for developing engineered organs.

name: Grigorios Panagakos
bio: Grigorios Panagakos is an expert in computational fluid dynamics and transport phenomena with applications in carbon management, hydrogen technologies, energy storage, and microfluidics. His research focuses on modeling and simulating separation systems using state-of-the-art numerical methods and machine learning strategies for process optimization.

name: Amritanshu Pandey
bio: Amritanshu Pandey is a faculty member in the Electrical and Computer Engineering department at Carnegie Mellon University. His research focuses on digital transformation and AI applications for energy and climate security.

name: Jon Peha
title: Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy; Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Jon Peha is a professor and center director whose research spans technical and policy issues of information and communications technology, including broadband, spectrum, universal access, autonomous vehicles, and cybersecurity. He has extensive government and industry experience, including roles at the FCC, White House, and as CTO of multiple high-tech companies.

name: Cécile Péraire
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Cécile Péraire is a teaching professor with over 25 years of software engineering experience in both industry and academia, specializing in software development methods and practices. She is passionate about innovation in tools and practices that enable teams to more effectively develop and deliver software-intensive systems, with a focus on software engineering education and process improvement.

name: Gianluca Piazza
title: Professor
department: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Gianluca Piazza is a professor specializing in piezoelectric Micro and Nano-ElectroMechanical Systems (M/NEMS) for RF wireless communication, chemical/biological detection, and mechanical computing. He directs the John and Claire Bertucci Nanotechnology Laboratory and has over 10 years of experience with piezoelectric materials and devices.

name: Nathan Picarsic
bio: Nathan Picarsic is a co-founder of Horizon Advisory, a leading geopolitical and supply chain intelligence provider, and manages Futura, a fund investing in early stage industrial innovation. He advises startups at Carnegie Mellon's Project Olympus and serves as a Senior Fellow at the Foundation for Defense of Democracies.

name: Larry Pileggi
title: Tanoto Professor and Coraluppi Head
bio: Larry Pileggi is a leading expert in digital and analog integrated circuit design, circuit simulation, and design methodologies with over 300 publications and 39 U.S. patents. His research spans power systems, energy infrastructure, hardware security, and cyberphysical systems, with significant industrial impact through multiple successful startups and collaborations with semiconductor companies.

name: P. Chris Pistorius
title: Professor
bio: P. Chris Pistorius is a metallurgical engineer whose research focuses on production of metals and alloys, mainly steel, and corrosion. His work includes advanced materials processing, additive manufacturing, and industrial decarbonization efforts in steelmaking.

name: Lisa Porter
title: Associate Dean for Faculty and Graduate Affairs
bio: Lisa Porter is a materials science and engineering researcher focused on semiconductors for energy-efficient technologies, including wide band gap semiconductors and nano-enabled devices. Her research spans electronic materials, emerging energy technologies, industrial decarbonization, and sustainable energy production.

name: Matteo Pozzi
title: Professor
bio: Matteo Pozzi is a professor whose research focuses on risk analysis for civil infrastructure systems using engineering models and sensor data, with particular emphasis on probabilistic models for seismic vulnerability and deterioration. His work addresses optimal planning for mitigation of extreme events and maintenance and inspection scheduling.

name: Albert Presto
title: Research Professor
bio: Albert Presto is a research professor and director of the Center for Atmospheric Particle Studies (CAPS) who investigates pollutant emissions from energy extraction and consumption and their atmospheric transformations using ambient measurements, laboratory experiments, and models. His research focuses on understanding how primary and secondary pollutants from sources like oil wells, vehicles, and combustion affect air quality, climate, and human health, particularly in relation to ozone, particulate matter, and greenhouse gases.

name: Sam Pulver
bio: Sam Pulver manages recruiting and outreach for chemical engineering academic programs, including coordinating admissions and developing marketing strategies. He oversees academic event programming and serves on academic committees.

name: Guannan Qu
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Guannan Qu is an assistant professor whose research focuses on control, optimization, and machine/reinforcement learning with applications to power systems, multi-agent systems, and smart cities. His work aims to make AI more safe and reliable for engineering systems, with particular interest in industrial decarbonization and energy efficiency.

name: David Quinn
title: Director of Research Initiatives and Special Projects
bio: David Quinn is the director of research initiatives and special projects for the Office of the Provost at Carnegie Mellon University, managing strategic initiatives and new research collaborations. He has broad expertise in experimental and computational mechanics with emphasis on calibrating and validating material constitutive models for soft materials including polymers, foams, and biological cells and tissue.

name: Yoed Rabin
title: Professor
bio: Yoed Rabin directs the Biothermal Technology Laboratory and conducts research in energy modalities in biology and medicine, with a focus on cryopreservation, cryosurgery, and thermal regulation of biological systems. His work integrates sensor development, instrumentation, surgical devices, physical modeling, and numerical methods for tissue and organ preservation applications.

name: Ragunathan (Raj) Rajkumar
title: George Westinghouse Professor
bio: Raj Rajkumar is the George Westinghouse Professor in ECE at Carnegie Mellon, directing multiple research centers including the Metro21 Smart Cities Institute and the GM-CMU Connected and Autonomous Driving Collaborative Research Lab. His research spans embedded real-time systems, wireless/sensor networks, cyber-physical systems security, and autonomous vehicle technologies.

name: Bhiksha Raj Ramakrishnan
title: Professor
bio: Bhiksha Raj Ramakrishnan conducts research in speech recognition, audio processing, neural networks, and privacy/security for voice processing, with expertise in noise robustness, computational auditory scene analysis, and cryptographic techniques. His work focuses on developing privacy-preserving algorithms for speech and audio processing to protect against security threats such as speaker impersonation and unauthorized demographic inference.

name: Barry Rawn
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Barry Rawn studies stressed centralized electricity infrastructure and off-grid energy systems deployment, with a focus on circular economy innovations in battery repurposing and pack design. His research integrates renewable energy resources into electric power systems and models their control, while facilitating industrial collaboration and international research activities in Africa.

name: Xi (Charlie) Ren
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Xi Ren is a developmental biologist who specializes in engineering functional vasculature and organ-specific tissues using decellularized scaffolds and advanced manufacturing techniques. His research focuses on creating bioengineered organs and tissue constructs for improved treatment of diseases, particularly in lung and cardiovascular applications.

name: Ron Ripper
bio: Ron Ripper manages CEE facilities, laboratory instruments, and safety protocols to support faculty and student research needs. He also serves as the CEE Sustainability Coordinator, implementing initiatives to reduce environmental impact and promote sustainable practices within the department.

name: Anne Skaja Robinson
title: Trustee Professor
bio: Anne Robinson is a Trustee Professor of Chemical Engineering whose research focuses on protein folding, aggregation, and biophysics with applications to therapeutically relevant protein molecules. Her work spans biotechnology and pharmaceutical engineering, with particular emphasis on bioprocess productivity and drug discovery.

name: Gregory S. Rohrer
title: W.W. Mullins Professor of Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Gregory Rohrer is a leading materials scientist whose research focuses on quantifying the atomic, nano, and microstructural mechanisms that govern high temperature deformation of materials and materials processing. His work has been recognized with numerous awards including the Cyril Stanley Smith Award from TMS in 2025, and he is a fellow of both the American Ceramic Society and the Materials Research Society.

name: Andrea Francioni Rooney
title: Academic Advisor and Writing Instructor
bio: Andrea Francioni Rooney provides academic advising to undergraduate students in civil engineering and environmental engineering while serving as a writing instructor for the department. She is pursuing a part-time Ph.D. in Rhetoric with a focus on reflective writing in engineering education.

name: Sheryl Root
title: Associate Professor of the Practice
bio: Sheryl Root is an associate professor of the practice for the M.S. in Software Management program at Carnegie Mellon Silicon Valley, where she develops curriculum and teaches courses merging technology with entrepreneurship. With over 13 years of consulting experience and a 20-year tenure at Hewlett-Packard, she specializes in corporate strategy, software engineering practices, and business management.

name: Steven Rosenberg
title: Senior Director of Operations
bio: Steven Rosenberg is senior director of operations at Carnegie Mellon's Silicon Valley campus, overseeing planning and technology incubation initiatives. With extensive experience in technology development and research management at HP Labs and academic institutions, he has led numerous university partnerships and developed innovative solutions across cloud computing, enterprise systems, and energy infrastructure.

name: Michael Rossi
bio: Michael Rossi is a certified Project Management Professional who provides research support and project management expertise to CTI. He brings extensive experience from managing research grants and contracts at leading institutions including the NSF, NIH, and CDC.

name: David Rounce
title: Assistant Professor
bio: David Rounce's research quantifies the response of glaciers, water resources, and hazards to climate change at local, regional, and global scales. His work uses computational models informed by remote sensing and fieldwork to produce actionable information for adaptation and mitigation efforts.

name: Anthony Rowe
title: Professor
bio: Anthony Rowe is a professor whose research focuses on networked real-time embedded systems with emphasis on low-power wireless communication and cyber-physical systems security. His recent work addresses large-scale sensing for critical infrastructure monitoring, building energy efficiency, and secure distributed computing architectures through his role as director of the CONIX Research Center.

name: Edward Rubin
title: Professor Emeritus
department: Engineering and Public Policy, Mechanical Engineering
bio: Edward Rubin's research addresses technical, economic, and policy issues related to energy and the environment, with a strong focus on climate change mitigation and control of pollutants from industrial and power generation sources. His group pioneered the Integrated Environmental Control Model (IECM), a widely-used stochastic simulation model for designing cost-effective emission control systems for thermal power plants, including carbon capture and storage technologies.

name: Marie-Ange Rukundo
title: Events Manager
bio: Marie-Ange Rukundo manages institutional events from conception to execution, ensuring maximum impact for all stakeholders. With over seven years of experience in client-facing roles and expertise in career services, she has achieved a 90% job placement rate for CMU-Africa alumni and coordinated 40+ internship placements annually.

name: Tom Rusu
bio: Tom Rusu is responsible for teaching and supporting research in metalwork. He provides expertise in machining, sheet metal work, welding, water jet cutting, and turning.

name: Nikolaos Sahinidis
title: Adjunct Faculty Member
bio: Nikolaos Sahinidis is an adjunct faculty member in Chemical Engineering at Carnegie Mellon whose research focuses on the interface between computer science and operations research. His work spans theory, algorithms, and software for global optimization of mixed-integer nonlinear programs with applications in engineering, chemistry, biology, and energy systems.

name: Paul A. Salvador
title: Professor
department: Department of Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Paul Salvador is a professor of Materials Science and Engineering who develops advanced materials and heterostructures for energy, data storage, and communications technologies using thin film methods. His research group focuses on materials processing, complex oxides, catalysis, and sustainable energy applications.

name: Constantine (Costa) Samaras
title: Trustee Professor
bio: Costa Samaras is the director of the Scott Institute for Energy Innovation and studies pathways to clean, climate-safe, equitable, and secure energy and infrastructure systems. His research analyzes how technologies and policies affect energy use, climate resilience, economic outcomes, and environmental emissions across transportation, electricity, and infrastructure sectors.

name: Mark Sanders
title: Distinguished Service Professor
bio: Mark Sanders is an entrepreneur and educator passionate about working with aspiring innovators and agents of change utilizing technology for good. He directs the Engineering Design, Innovation, and Entrepreneurship (EDIE) program and brings extensive experience from founding an environmental remediation company and supporting technology commercialization at Berkeley Lab.

name: Aswin Sankaranarayanan
title: Professor
bio: Aswin Sankaranarayanan is a professor in the ECE department at CMU where he directs the Image Science Lab, focusing on computational photography and 3D shape estimation through novel imaging systems. His research spans computational imaging, computer vision, and signal processing with applications to building new kinds of imaging systems and understanding visual information.

name: Marios Savvides
title: Professor
bio: Professor Savvides develops core AI and machine-learning algorithms for biometrics, face recognition, iris detection, and object detection in unconstrained real-world conditions. His research has achieved significant milestones including first-in-world long-range iris capture systems and has been successfully deployed to millions of devices through commercial applications.

name: Lauren Schachar
title: Student Affairs Leader
bio: Dr. Lauren Schachar leads Student Affairs at CMU-Silicon Valley, designing human-centered experiences and operationally effective systems for graduate students. Her work focuses on building values-driven systems, leading with purpose, and creating meaningful support structures within higher education institutions.

name: Cathy Schaefer
title: Primary Business Officer
bio: Cathy Schaefer is the Primary Business Officer for the CEE department, overseeing all finance and operations, including financial planning and analysis and human resources. She serves as a resource and advisor to faculty and staff on compliance, policy, administration, and planning matters.

name: Sarah Scheffler
title: Assistant Professor
department: Software and Societal Systems and Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Sarah Scheffler is an applied cryptographer working at the intersection of computer science, policy, and law, with research spanning end-to-end encrypted content moderation, compelled decryption, and autonomous weapon systems. She also conducts pure applied cryptography research including zero-knowledge proofs, multi-party computation, and private set intersection.

name: James Schneider
title: Professor
bio: James Schneider develops bio-inspired materials for improved biosensing and bioseparations, including a rapid gel-free electrophoretic DNA separation method that is 10-100x faster than commercial alternatives. His research extends to synthetic nucleic acid analogues, plasmid DNA purification, and amino-acid-linked surfactants for drug delivery applications.

name: Derin Sevenler
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Derin Sevenler is an assistant professor whose research addresses problems at the interface of biotechnology and fluid mechanics, with interests in microfluidics, complex fluids, biomaterials, and molecular diagnostics. His work focuses on applications including gene and drug delivery systems, and he is developing microfluidic technologies to make gene therapies more accessible and cost-effective.

name: Karimulla Shaikh
bio: Karimulla Shaikh is a seasoned product development executive with 20 years of experience at startups and publicly-traded companies, currently leading product development at Virtual Power Systems focusing on intelligent software-defined power for data centers. He is passionate about building scalable and reliable cloud products while developing high-performance teams through simplified people and process interactions.

name: Noella Marie Shema
bio: Noella Marie Shema brings over seven years of academic and program coordination experience, with expertise in curriculum development, student coaching, and cross-functional collaboration. Her background spans roles as an academic advisor, instructor, and program coordinator focused on enhancing educational delivery and fostering student entrepreneurship.

name: John Paul Shen
title: Full Professor
bio: John Shen is a tenured full professor with expertise in computer architecture, processor design, and mobile computing systems. His research spans modern processor design, instruction-level parallelism, dependable computing, and wireless cloud infrastructure.

name: Sheng Shen
title: Professor
bio: Sheng Shen is a professor whose research focuses on nanophotonics, nanoscale energy transport and conversion, nanofabrication and advanced manufacturing, with applications in thermal management, light sources, thermal emission control, and energy conversion. His work has been recognized with numerous awards including NSF CAREER Award, DARPA Director's Fellowship, and the Raymond Viskanta Award for Spectroscopy and Radiative Transfer.

name: Elaine Shi
title: Professor
department: Computer Science and Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Elaine Shi is a professor interested in cryptography, game theory, algorithms, and foundations of blockchains with a focus on security and privacy. Her research spans distributed systems security, privacy-enhancing technologies, secure multi-party computation, and blockchain security.

name: Kenji Shimada
title: Theodore Ahrens Professor of Engineering
bio: Kenji Shimada is the Theodore Ahrens Professor of Mechanical Engineering whose research focuses on computer modeling and simulation for product design, analysis, and manufacturing, with emphasis on geometric computing and mesh generation methods. His work addresses industry needs for increased design efficiency, higher product quality, and manufacturing cost reduction through novel computational methodologies including the Bubble Mesh technique and non-manifold geometric modeling.

name: Kaz Shindle
title: Program Manager
bio: Kaz Shindle is the program manager for community engagement at the College of Engineering, focused on creating welcoming spaces through the Intergroup Dialogue program and Cultural Humility trainings. They work to develop innovative programming and ensure all students have the resources and environment to succeed at CMU.

name: Paul Sides
title: Emeritus Professor
bio: Paul Sides is an emeritus professor of chemical engineering who has published over 70 articles spanning electrochemical engineering, growth of advanced materials, and colloid science. His research includes work on electrolytic gas evolution, material characterization, colloidal particle assembly, and he founded ZetaMetrix Inc. to commercialize zeta potential measurement instrumentation.

name: Satbir Singh
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Satbir Singh is a teaching professor whose research focuses on analytical, experimental, and numerical methods in thermo-fluids, with a goal to develop accurate computer models for simulations of turbulent flows. He works closely with Carnegie Mellon's Center for Atmospheric Particle Studies to perform research in the area of efficiency and environmental impact of energy conversion technologies.

name: Ramteen Sioshansi
title: Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy, Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Ramteen Sioshansi's research focuses on integrating advanced energy technologies including renewable energy, energy storage, and electric transportation into energy systems. He works in energy policy and electricity-market design, particularly as they relate to advanced energy technologies and grid resilience.

name: Jason Smith
title: Associate Director of Partnerships
bio: Jason Smith teaches and mentors students in the MIIPS program at the Integrated Innovation Institute while serving as associate director of partnerships. His research background spans regenerative medicine, wound healing, and biosensors, with extensive experience in medical device development and translating research into clinical applications.

name: Matt Smith
title: Associate Professor
bio: Matt Smith's research focuses on systems neuroengineering and understanding the brain using advanced neurotechnologies, employing neurophysiological and computational approaches to study the visual system at the level of individual neurons and populations. His lab investigates internal brain states including motivation, attention, and arousal using brain-computer interfaces and large-scale neural models.

name: Sari Smith
bio: Sari Smith leads and supports strategic engagement initiatives to broaden the INI's impact. She serves as the staff lead for the Alumni Leadership Council and spearheads alumni engagement initiatives.

name: Stephen Smith
title: Professor
bio: Stephen Smith's research focuses on artificial intelligence with emphasis on constraint-based search, optimization, and automated planning and scheduling. His work centers on developing core technologies for coordination and control of large-scale multi-actor systems with applications in transportation, manufacturing, logistics, and energy systems.

name: Vincent Sokalski
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Vincent Sokalski's research focuses on emerging phenomena in magnetic thin films and spintronic materials for future computing applications, particularly examining skyrmions and their potential for energy-efficient computer memory. His group uses combinatorial approaches and advanced characterization techniques including high resolution Lorentz transmission electron microscopy to stabilize and study different kinds of skyrmions.

name: Andrew Spielberg
bio: Andrew Spielberg researches simulation methods, design algorithms, and digital manufacturing processes with a focus on overcoming the sim-to-real gap for inventing in both virtual and physical worlds. He is the CEO of MorphoAI, a startup translating research into products for designing robots and cyberphysical machines.

name: Evan Walter Clark Spotte-Smith
title: Adjunct Professor
bio: Evan Spotte-Smith is an adjunct professor leveraging computational simulations and data science to understand reactivity in complex systems including electrochemical interfaces and solid-state syntheses. Their research applies these methods to catalysis and sustainability, with interests in high-throughput experimentation and software development.

name: Tathagata Srimani
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Tathagata Srimani's research focuses on enhancing energy efficiency and throughput of computing systems through advancements in new technologies, heterogeneous integration, and technology-architecture co-design. His work includes developing fab-compatible processes for complementary Carbon Nanotube FETs and monolithic 3D systems that integrate CNFETs with silicon and resistive RAM.

name: Michael Starz
title: Director of Research Partnerships
bio: Michael Starz is Director of Research Partnerships for the College of Engineering, bringing extensive experience from a military career spanning seven overseas deployments across South Korea, Iraq, Afghanistan, Pakistan, and Ukraine. Most recently, he served as chief operations officer of the Army's AI Integration Center, which grew into a $50M center leveraging CMU expertise to bring AI into Army operations.

name: Paul Steif
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Paul Steif is a professor emeritus in Mechanical Engineering who focused his research on engineering education, particularly on how students learn fundamental engineering concepts like statics. His work included developing conceptual frameworks, assessment tools, and interactive courseware to help students achieve deeper understanding of mechanics subjects.

name: Stephanie Steren-Ruta
title: Advanced Manufacturing Engineer - Digitalization
bio: Stephanie Steren-Ruta is an Advanced Manufacturing Engineer at Carnegie Mellon's Manufacturing Futures Institute who integrates research projects into digital systems to support manufacturing digital transformation. With extensive experience leading global projects at companies like Bridgestone Americas and Micro-Poise Measurement Systems, she specializes in process improvement, automation, and data-driven manufacturing solutions.

name: Keith Straughan
bio: Keith Straughan is a seasoned academic leader and entrepreneur with expertise spanning medical imaging, innovation, and executive coaching. He has established companies from his research, held senior academic roles at major universities, and now focuses on innovation leadership development and capacity building across Africa.

name: Andrzej Strojwas
title: Keithley Professor
bio: Andrzej Strojwas is the Keithley Professor in Electrical and Computer Engineering at Carnegie Mellon University, with extensive industry experience in semiconductor technology and EDA. His research focuses on statistically-based CAD/CAM methods for VLSI circuits and semiconductor manufacturing optimization.

name: Eswaran Subrahmanian
title: Research Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Eswaran Subrahmanian is a research professor focused on socio-technical systems design, decision support systems, engineering informatics, and design theory and methods. His work spans collaborative design support systems, engineering design processes, and the intersection of technology, social sciences, and policy.

name: Thomas M. Sullivan
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Thomas Sullivan is interested in signal processing for audio and music systems and the creation of new musical instruments, with particular expertise in electro-acoustics and electronic circuits. He is active in teaching and course development for audio engineering, oversees independent student research projects, and has worked extensively with sound for art installations and sculptures.

name: Paul Swaga
bio: Paul Swaga brings expertise in youth skills development and capacity building, with a focus on transforming lives across Africa through sustainable career service systems and educational programs. His work emphasizes empowering vulnerable communities to achieve economic self-sufficiency through employment and entrepreneurship.

name: Jason Szafron
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Jason Szafron is an Assistant Professor of Biomedical Engineering whose research focuses on computational optimization and experimental methods for understanding cardiovascular disease progression and treatment. His work combines tissue engineering, mechanobiological modeling, and computational approaches to improve vascular graft design and cardiovascular disease therapies.

name: Patrick Tague
title: Professor
bio: Patrick Tague leads the Mobile, Embedded, and Wireless Security group at CMU Silicon Valley and is affiliated with CyLab. His research focuses on wireless communications security, mobile privacy, resilient networked systems, and sensor network data analysis.

name: S. Mohadeseh Taheri-Mousavi
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Taheri-Mousavi develops advanced structural alloys and materials through computational design and additive manufacturing techniques, with a focus on high-performance applications in extreme environments. Her research emphasizes industrial decarbonization, materials sustainability, and the use of machine learning to accelerate alloy discovery and optimization.

name: Pingbo Tang
title: Associate Professor
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
bio: Pingbo Tang explores remote sensing, data analytics, and information modeling to support spatiotemporal analyses for predictive management of constructed facilities and civil infrastructure systems. His research examines sensing and modeling methods for Human-Cyber-Physical-Systems in accelerated construction and infrastructure operations.

name: Joel A. Tarr
title: University Professor
department: Department of Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Joel Tarr is a leading scholar of environmental and urban history, studying the history and impact of technological systems in cities and using historical perspectives to understand contemporary problems. His work addresses urban infrastructure, pollution, and the relationship between technology and the urban environment.

name: Rebecca Taylor
title: Associate Professor
bio: Rebecca Taylor's research focuses on structural DNA nanotechnology to create nanoscale biosensors, actuators, and bio-inspired systems that interface across molecular, cellular, and tissue scales. Her work combines top-down microfabrication with bottom-up self-assembly methods to enable advanced manufacturing and address challenges in biomedical device design.

name: Chris Telmer
title: Associate Professor
bio: Chris Telmer is an Associate Professor of Financial Economics whose research examines government subsidies in renewable energy financing, consumption behavior, exchange rate dynamics, and the effects of labor-market risk on financial markets. His work also explores intergenerational mobility and has made him a visiting scholar across multiple countries and central banks.

name: Sarath B. Tennakoon
title: Research Fellow
bio: Sarath Tennakoon is a research fellow focused on power electronics and grid integration of renewable energy, particularly wind energy, HVDC, and DC grids. His recent research emphasizes the productive use of electrical energy in Africa and sustainable energy systems.

name: Ravi Thomas
title: Associate Professor of the Practice
bio: Ravi Thomas is an associate professor of the practice at CMU Silicon Valley with extensive experience in startup finance and executive leadership. His research and teaching focus on economics, information economics, and public finance, combined with practical expertise from founding and leading multiple technology startups.

name: Glen Thompson
bio: Glen Thompson is an English language instructor with extensive international teaching experience across multiple continents, specializing in English for academic purposes and teaching to diverse student populations. He is particularly interested in curriculum development and assessment, with a focus on supporting international students in degree programs.

name: Victor Thompson
bio: Victor Thompson brings over 40 years of experience in computer technology and software application engineering, with expertise spanning enterprise systems, cloud content distribution, mobile applications, and cybersecurity. He is recognized as a technology leader known for crafting strategic IT solutions aligned with business goals and has held significant leadership roles including NASA's chief information officer overseeing IT strategy and infrastructure.

name: Jesse Thornburg
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Jesse Thornburg researches microgrids and automation while teaching courses in electrical engineering and entrepreneurship, with a focus on energy systems and applications in Africa. His work combines technical expertise in power systems with entrepreneurial experience, having co-founded multiple companies and mentored numerous startups in the automation and energy sectors.

name: Robert Tilton
title: Chevron Professor of Chemical Engineering
bio: Robert Tilton's research addresses problems in complex fluid interfacial phenomena, including fundamental investigations of macromolecules, surfactants, and composite nanoparticles in suspension or at interfaces. His work supports application-driven research in environmental nanotechnology, aerosolized carriers for pulmonary drug delivery, and high efficiency emulsifiers.

name: Daniel Tkacik
title: Executive Director
department: Wilton E. Scott Institute for Energy Innovation
bio: Daniel Tkacik is the Executive Director of the Wilton E. Scott Institute for Energy Innovation at CMU, where he leads multidisciplinary energy research and collaboration. His research background includes studying air pollution from combustion systems, secondary organic aerosols, and greenhouse gas emissions from natural gas facilities.

name: Rahul Tongia
bio: Rahul Tongia's work focuses on sustainable energy technology, policy, and economics, especially for developing regions. His research spans smart grids, political economy of reform, and energy infrastructure.

name: Ozan Tonguz
title: Professor
department: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Ozan Tonguz is a professor specializing in telecommunications and networking, with particular expertise in optical networks, high-speed networking, wireless systems, and vehicular networks. His research encompasses AI/ML applications for security, IoT systems, cybersecurity, and transportation engineering solutions using vehicle-to-vehicle communications.

name: Kevin Topolski
bio: Kevin Topolski streamlines and implements the INI's financial processes and documentation, develops budgets and forecasts, and monitors financial activity. He also assists the director of business operations with payroll, recruiting, and other human resource-related processes.

name: Ana Inés Torres
title: Associate Professor
bio: Ana Inés Torres is an associate professor whose research focuses on process systems engineering with an emphasis on sustainability, including decarbonization of the chemical industry through electrification and biomass utilization, material recovery and circular economy initiatives, and environmentally-friendly rare earth element recovery. She has received the NSF CAREER award and serves as a consultant for the United Nations Industrial Development Organization.

name: Dennis Trumble
title: Associate Research Professor
bio: Dennis Trumble is an associate research professor focused on the design and development of implantable medical devices for cardiac assist purposes, with expertise in hemodynamic monitoring and multi-scale computational modeling. His current research aims to develop innovative cardiac assist devices to realize their clinical impact, while also pursuing interests in neuroscience, consciousness, and decision-making psychology.

name: Nicholas Tsamitis
bio: Provides technical support and assistance for distance-delivered courses and events. Operates complex embedded video and audio control systems and maintains instructional equipment.

name: Conrad Tucker
title: Professor
bio: Conrad Tucker is a professor of mechanical engineering whose research focuses on the design and optimization of systems through the acquisition, integration, and mining of large-scale, disparate data. His work spans artificial intelligence, machine learning, cybersecurity, and bioengineering, with applications to security, privacy, and anomaly detection.

name: Eric Umuhoza
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Eric Umuhoza is an assistant teaching professor at Carnegie Mellon University Africa with research interests in model-driven software engineering, user interaction design, and big data management for Internet of Things. He has held postdoctoral and teaching positions at leading European universities including Polytechnic University of Milan and University of L'Aquila.

name: Honorine Uwicyubahiro
bio: Honorine Uwicyubahiro brings over five years of experience in admissions, enrollment, and recruitment within higher education, specializing in optimizing the admissions lifecycle and implementing data-driven strategies to enhance student enrollment. Her work combines strategic planning, project management, operational execution, and data-driven decision-making to achieve impactful results.

name: Olive Uwimana
title: Administrative Support Coordinator
bio: Olive Uwimana is an administrative support coordinator with a master's degree in data science specializing in econometrics and a bachelor's degree in logistics and operations. She brings five years of experience in operations and administration from higher education institutions, with a passion for data analytics and research.

name: Fils Uwitonze
bio: Fils Uwitonze is a biostatistician and epidemiology specialist with over 12 years of experience partnering with leading international organizations to meet funding and strategic program needs. At CMU-Africa, he focuses on refining qualitative and quantitative data collection methods, developing survey instruments, and upholding ethical data practices to elevate institutional research initiatives.

name: David Varodayan
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: David Varodayan is an associate teaching professor whose research focuses on the intersection of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and information security. He has developed innovative graduate courses on machine learning with adversaries in mind and AI applications in security, and was instrumental in creating CMU's Master's in AI Engineering degree program.

name: Mike Verti
title: Instructor
bio: Mike Verti is an instructor and practitioner of innovation with 20 years of industry consulting experience focused on delivering solutions from ideas to commercialization. He specializes in R&D strategy, product development, and helping organizations improve their innovation effectiveness and bring new solutions to market.

name: David Vey
bio: David Vey serves as the liaison and compliance officer for the Ph.D. program, managing office and advisor assignments while overseeing research assistantships and scholarships. He organizes graduate student orientation and supports several student organizations within the department.

name: Katie Walsh
title: Associate Director of Academic Support Services
department: Eberly Center for Teaching Excellence and Educational Innovation
bio: Katie Walsh is an educational leader who directs academic support services and peer mentoring programs at Carnegie Mellon. She works to develop teachers and learners while supporting student success through supplemental instruction and tutoring initiatives.

name: Jerry Wang
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Jerry Wang uses mechanics, statistical physics, and high-performance computing to understand nanoscale structural and transport phenomena with applications to the water-energy nexus. His research spans nanophotonics, metamaterials, fluid mechanics, and climate-resilient environmental systems.

name: Liwei Wang
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Liwei Wang directs the Computational and Physical Intelligence Laboratory, developing machine learning algorithms and multi-physics optimization methods for advanced materials systems including 3D/4D printing, smart structures, and programmable materials. His research explores applications in mechanical protective cloaks, minimally invasive surgery, flexible electronics, and soft robotics that enhance human well-being and capabilities.

name: Newell Washburn
title: Associate Professor
department: Materials Science and Engineering, Biomedical Engineering
bio: Newell Washburn develops novel materials with innovative compositions and architectures, focusing on fundamental properties, structure-function relationships, and technological applications. His research emphasizes renewable resource-based materials, including lignin-based composites, non-fouling medical interfaces, metal adsorbents, and conducting polymers for energy storage.

name: Douglas Weber
title: Professor
bio: Douglas Weber investigates how sensory feedback supports perceptual, motor, cognitive, and autonomic functions by combining neuroscience and engineering research. His work develops technologies and therapies for enhancing sensory and motor functions after injury and creates wearable devices for improving performance in healthy individuals.

name: Bryan Webler
title: Professor
department: Department of Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Bryan Webler is a Professor of Materials Science and Engineering whose research focuses on process metallurgy, including chemical reactions in steel refining, non-metallic inclusion control, continuous casting, and additive manufacturing techniques. His research group employs high temperature experiments, computational thermodynamics, and kinetic models to study fundamental processes in these areas.

name: Victoria Webster-Wood
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Victoria Webster-Wood established the CMU Biohybrid and Organic Robotics Group (B.O.R.G), which focuses on developing biohybrid and organic robots using organic materials as structures, actuators, sensors, and controllers. Her research applies these innovations toward biohybrid prosthetics and medical device development, combining biological systems with synthetic materials.

name: Damon Weiss
title: Professor of the Practice
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
bio: Damon Weiss is a professor of the practice with 28 years of experience in digital modeling, urban systems, and data-informed design who helps students connect emerging technologies with real-world infrastructure challenges. He integrates digital twin systems, AI models, and geospatial analytics with sustainable and community-centered urban design to advance climate resilience, infrastructure equity, and systems-level planning.

name: Samantha Wessel
title: Manager of Administration and External Relations
bio: Samantha Wessel serves as manager of administration and external relations for the Department of Chemical Engineering, partnering with department leadership to advance strategic initiatives and special projects. She oversees alumni and industry engagement strategy for the ChemE Council, MS Advisory Board, and the Center for Advanced Process Decision-Making.

name: Jay Whitacre
title: Trustee Professor in Energy
department: Engineering and Public Policy; Materials Science and Engineering
bio: Jay Whitacre's research focuses on the materials science of synthesizing and implementing promising materials for energy storage and generation technologies such as Li-ion batteries, fuel cells, and photovoltaics, while addressing policy implications of renewable energy implementation. He has founded companies commercializing novel battery chemistries and cathode materials, authored over 140 peer-reviewed papers, and holds more than 30 patents in energy technology.

name: Kate Whitefoot
title: Associate Professor
department: Mechanical Engineering and Engineering and Public Policy
bio: Kate Whitefoot is an internationally recognized engineer and economist specializing in sustainable transportation, energy, and manufacturing with expertise in technology development, adoption, and policy. Her research addresses the intersection of engineering innovation and economic policy to advance clean energy and transportation solutions.

name: Kathryn A. Whitehead
title: Professor
bio: Kathryn Whitehead is a professor who develops drug delivery systems for RNA and proteins with applications in maternal and infant health. Her research focuses on designing lipid nanoparticles and biomaterials to enable targeted delivery of therapeutics to specific organs.

name: Nathan Williams
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Nathan Williams researches data-driven energy policy and planning in underserved communities, focusing on how renewable and decentralized energy technologies can expand access to affordable, sustainable electricity. He applies methods from engineering, finance, social science, and data science to design and integrate infrastructure systems that support social and economic development in marginalized regions.

name: Rafał Włodarski
bio: Rafał Włodarski's research lies at the intersection of software engineering and pedagogy, focusing on bridging the gap between software engineering education and industrial needs. He currently investigates the use of generative artificial intelligence in auto grading, formative assessment, and gamification.

name: Ed Wojciechowski
bio: Ed Wojciechowski manages student and professional machine shops and shop staff at Carnegie Mellon University. He provides machining support and fabrication services for university projects and research.

name: Sandra DeVincent Wolf
title: Executive Director
bio: Sandra DeVincent Wolf is the executive director of the CMU Manufacturing Futures Institute and NextManufacturing Center, working to identify and accelerate research in manufacturing across campus and with industry partners. Her work focuses on leveraging additive manufacturing, advanced materials processing, and AI/machine learning to support innovation and drive economic development in manufacturing.

name: Jennifer Wolfeld
title: Language and Communication Specialist
bio: Jennifer Wolfeld is a language and communication specialist who supports students in developing confidence and clarity in their communication across interviews, interpersonal skills, and professional writing. Her research background includes studying computer applications for visualizing language intonation patterns to improve spoken language intelligibility.

name: Osman Yagan
title: Research Professor
bio: Osman Yagan is a research professor focused on modeling, design, and performance evaluation of engineering systems with emphasis on communication systems and networks. His research spans wireless communications, security, random graphs, social and information networks, and cyber-physical systems.

name: Shi-Chune Yao
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Shi-Chune Yao is a professor emeritus whose research focuses on multiphase flow and heat transfer with applications to energy and thermal control industries. He pioneered spray cooling technology and currently works on micro-channel systems for electronic chip cooling and numerical simulation of metal 3D printing.

name: B. Erik Ydstie
title: Emeritus Professor
bio: B. Erik Ydstie is an emeritus professor whose research focuses on modeling and control for manufacturing solar cells and optimal operation of oil and gas fields. His work includes adaptive control and self-learning control systems for process optimization.

name: Kai Yu
title: Research Scientist and Special Faculty
bio: Kai Yu is a research scientist specializing in neuromodulation, ultrasound, biomedical imaging, bioinstrumentation, and biosensors. He has received numerous awards including the IEEE Brain Initiative Neurotech Entrepreneurs' Award and recognition as a Gold Medalist at the International Exhibition of Inventions in Geneva.

name: Phillip Yu
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Phillip Yu is an associate teaching professor and executive director of Master's programs in Engineering and Public Policy and Engineering & Technology Innovation Management at Carnegie Mellon. With 30 years of industry experience at PPG Industries leading innovation, product development, and technology strategy, he brings expertise in commercializing advanced materials and managing R&D operations.

name: Tom Zajdel
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Tom Zajdel is an assistant teaching professor interested in how students become motivated to study electronics and engineering, with a technical background in fabricating bioelectronic interfaces for biosensing and medical applications. His teaching experience spans circuits, amateur radio, introductory mechanics, technical writing, and engineering design.

name: Andrea Zanette
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Andrea Zanette's research focuses on the foundations of reinforcement learning and decision-making under data uncertainty. His work addresses modern RL challenges including exploration, function approximation, adaptivity, and learning from offline data.

name: Conrad Zapanta
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Conrad Zapanta is a teaching professor in Biomedical Engineering with research interests in cardiovascular medical devices and biomedical engineering education. He has made significant contributions to biomedical engineering curricula and teaching programs, and serves as Associate Dean of Undergraduate Studies in the College of Engineering.

name: Ji Zhang
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Ji Zhang's research focuses on robotic navigation, spanning localization, mapping, planning, and exploration, with pioneering work on real-time 3D Lidar SLAM. He founded Kaarta, Inc. to commercialize 3D mapping technologies and has led autonomous navigation algorithms that earned CMU-OSU Team the Most Sectors Explored Award on DARPA Subterranean Challenge.

name: Peter Zhang
bio: Peter Zhang's research focuses on analytics and optimization with applications in health and engineering systems. His work addresses sustainability challenges through the application of advanced analytical and optimization techniques.

name: Xu Zhang
title: Associate Professor
department: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Xu Zhang is an associate professor studying advanced electronic and photonic devices based on emerging nanomaterials, with research spanning extreme transistor scaling, high frequency electronics, neuromorphic computing for AI hardware, and tunable photonic devices. His work has applications across computing, energy, communications, and sensing, and has been recognized with the NSF CAREER Award and MIT Technology Review's Innovators Under 35.

name: Jessica Zhang
title: Professor
bio: Jessica Zhang develops novel computational algorithms for biomodeling across molecular, cellular, tissue, and organ scales, with expertise in computational geometry, finite element methods, and data-driven modeling. Her research applies these techniques to computational biomedicine, materials science, and engineering, with over 240 published technical articles and a focus on bioengineered organs and medical device design.

name: Ding Zhao
title: Associate Professor
bio: Ding Zhao leads the CMU Safe AI Laboratory, developing trustworthy AI systems that are robust, safe, generalizable, and explainable with applications in intelligent autonomy and healthcare. His research aims to create fundamental theories and practical technologies for safely deploying AI to address pressing global challenges.

name: Siyang Zheng
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Siyang Zheng's research focuses on biomedical micro/nano technologies with applications to cancer and infectious disease diagnosis. He is a principal investigator of NIH R01 grants on liquid biopsy technologies and has received the NIH Director's New Innovator Award and American Cancer Society Research Scholar Award.

name: Chipiwa Zimbwa
title: Chief Operating Officer
bio: Chipiwa Zimbwa is a people-focused C-suite executive with extensive experience in corporate and non-profit leadership, spanning financial management, corporate governance, and resource mobilization. As Chief Operating Officer of CMU-Africa, she provides executive leadership to ensure financial, administrative, and operational integrity while overseeing budget, human resources, information technology, finance, and facilities.

name: Marian Aguiar
title: Associate Professor of English
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Marian Aguiar is an Associate Professor of Literary and Cultural Studies whose research focuses on postcolonial studies, border studies, mobilities, diaspora, and global gender studies. Her work examines representations of colonial and postcolonial space, South Asian diaspora narratives, and Mediterranean sea migration through literary and cultural analysis.

name: Keely Austin
title: Visiting Lecturer
school: Dietrich College of Humanities and Social Sciences
bio: Keely Austin's work focuses on genre theory, language ideologies, and diversity, equity, and inclusion in curriculum and assessment to create meaningful learning experiences.
