His research interests focus on decisionmaking processes in grantmaking and fostering civic innovation through strategic philanthropy and nonprofit management.

name: Carly Jo Harris
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Carly Jo Harris is a Senior Information Security Engineer at Nike who specializes in developing security technologies and managing cybersecurity tools across large organizations. She holds a Master's degree in Information Security, Policy, and Management from Carnegie Mellon and is actively involved in the Women in Cybersecurity organization.

name: John Harrison
title: Director/Producer/Composer
bio: John Harrison is a writer, director, and composer who has collaborated extensively with George Romero and created Emmy-winning miniseries adaptations of Frank Herbert's works. His career spans film, television, and literature, with recent publications and ongoing development of original series.

name: Chris T Hendrickson
title: Hamerschlag University Professor Emeritus
bio: Chris Hendrickson is a Hamerschlag University Professor Emeritus and Director of the Traffic 21 Institute who has made pioneering contributions to dynamic traffic equilibrium models, construction project management, and green design. His research focuses on engineering planning and management, including design for the environment, system performance, and life cycle analysis of products and processes.

name: Lakshmi Iyengar
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Lakshmi Iyengar is an entertainment industry executive with extensive experience in film acquisitions, production development, and media finance across major studios and production companies. She serves as adjunct faculty for Carnegie Mellon's Master in Entertainment Industry Management program and brings practical industry expertise to the program.

name: Meghan Jacquot
title: Adjunct Instructor
department: Heinz College of Information Systems and Public Policy
bio: Meghan Jacquot is a Cybersecurity Engineer at Carnegie Mellon's Software Engineering Institute CERT division working on cyber risk and resilience, with expertise in emerging technologies and cybersecurity maturity models. She has led development of generative AI tools for offensive security, analyzed international cybersecurity frameworks, and conducted extensive threat intelligence research across various industry sectors.

name: Kyle Johnson
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Kyle Johnson manages machine learning teams for the US Federal practice at AWS Professional Services, helping public sector agencies and healthcare systems solve business problems using deep learning and machine learning techniques. He brings experience from IBM Watson Health and is an Adjunct Professor of Data Science at Carnegie Mellon University.

name: Daniel Kambic
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Daniel Kambic is an adjunct faculty member with over 30 years of experience in IT architecture, security, applications, project delivery, and management. He has held global IT positions at major corporations including Oracle, PPG Industries, Kraft Heinz, and U.S. Steel, and is a Certified Information Systems Security Professional.

name: Pawan Khera
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Pawan Khera is the Global Marketing Manager at IBM leading strategy, planning, and go-to-market deployment for Industrial solutions. He brings 15 years of experience in corporate strategy, management consulting, and business development, having advised Fortune 100 companies on business, IT, and analytics strategies.

name: Christine Koebley
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Christine brings 30 years of finance and operational leadership experience from both for-profit and non-profit sectors, currently serving as Senior Director of Finance and Administration for the Children's Museum of Pittsburgh. She teaches financial statements and analysis for arts and entertainment organizations, with expertise in financial sustainability and risk management.

name: Christopher A. Kowalsky
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Christopher Kowalsky is an Adjunct Professor of Information Technology Management at Heinz School with over 40 years of IT experience, including leadership roles as CIO at major healthcare and education organizations. His expertise spans healthcare IT systems, organizational technology strategy, and IT service management in complex institutional environments.

name: Michael Lamb
title: Adjunct Faculty
school: Heinz College of Public Policy and Management
bio: Michael Lamb is an attorney and former elected official who served as Pittsburgh's Controller and Allegheny County Prothonotary, implementing government transparency initiatives and financial reporting reforms. His work focuses on public sector efficiency, fiscal accountability, and civic engagement in municipal governance.

name: Ed Lammi
bio: Ed Lammi is a retired Executive Vice President of Production for Sony Pictures Television with over three decades of experience overseeing production of live-action and animated series across all day-parts. He is sought after for his expertise in problem-solving and insights on the changing television business landscape.

name: Denise Lawson
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Denise Lawson is an adjunct faculty member with over 20 years of professional experience in management consulting, business strategy, and creating innovative solutions across government, healthcare, education, and non-profit sectors. She brings expertise from her tenure at Booz Allen Hamilton and her role as Senior Vice President of Strategy at a technology startup, combined with advisory consulting focused on health and education industries.

name: Hui Li
title: Assistant Professor of Marketing
bio: Hui Li is an Assistant Professor of Marketing at Carnegie Mellon University's Heinz College. Her research focuses on dynamic pricing, consumer behavior in digital platforms, and empirical industrial organization.

name: Patrick Maher
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Patrick Maher is an Adjunct Instructor with over 20 years of professional experience in management consulting and business strategy. He leads a global consulting team at SAS Institute focusing on big data, data science, machine learning, and predictive modeling across multiple industries.

name: Susan A. Markham
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Susan Markham is an adjunct faculty member and Director of the Peace With Women Fellowship program at the Halifax International Security Forum, bringing over 50 countries of experience in women's leadership and gender equality. Her career spans USAID, the National Democratic Institute, and consulting work focused on advancing women's political participation and empowerment across democratic institutions.

name: John Mattiace
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: John Mattiace is an Adjunct Instructor at Heinz College with a Master's in Statistics from Penn State, where his thesis focused on computational methods to model interactions between breastfeeding and vaccination in conferring immunity to infants. He has extensive teaching experience in undergraduate statistics courses including probability theory, mathematical statistics, experimental methods, and stochastic processes.

name: Karl Mattson
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Karl Mattson is the Chief Information Security Officer for City National Bank, leading information security, business continuity, and risk programs. With 20 years of experience in risk and security across Fortune 500 companies and the US Army, he brings practical expertise in cybersecurity and enterprise risk management.

name: Joe Mayes
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Joe Mayes is a member of the technical staff within the CERT Program at the Software Engineering Institute, managing curriculum and training delivery platforms for cybersecurity products for U.S. government agencies including U.S. Cyber Command and the Air Force Research Laboratory. With over 25 IT certifications and extensive experience in network security, wireless networking, and information operations, he brings deep technical expertise to cybersecurity training and curriculum development.

name: Janet L. McCall
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Janet L. McCall is Executive Director of the Society for Contemporary Craft in Pittsburgh, where she oversees artistic direction, management, and fundraising for this nonprofit organization featuring galleries, education centers, and community partnerships. Her extensive background includes museum management, art curation, and arts education, with expertise in exhibition development and cultural leadership.

name: Daniel S. Nagin
title: Lester Hamburg University Professor of Public Policy and Statistics
bio: Daniel Nagin is a distinguished criminologist whose research focuses on the evolution of criminal and antisocial behaviors over the life course, the deterrent effects of criminal and non-criminal penalties, and the development of statistical methods for analyzing longitudinal data. He is a recipient of multiple prestigious awards including the Stockholm Prize in Criminology and the Edwin H. Sutherland Award, and serves as a fellow of the National Academy of Sciences and the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.

name: Holly Newman
bio: Holly Newman is a digital game studio, marketing, and production executive with more than 20 years of experience as an entrepreneurial leader in the gaming industry. She has held positions ranging from studio head and CEO to publishing EVP, leading successful teams and major gaming titles that have generated billions in revenue.

name: Stephen (Jake) Nicholas
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: An experienced software engineer and agile coach who brings real-world expertise in crafting clean, high-quality code and leading software teams. Passionate about mentoring students to become professional technical leaders through hands-on learning and problem-solving skills.

name: Paul Nielsen
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Paul Nielsen leads Optum's Strategic Programs and directs the College of Artificial Intelligence, bringing over 25 years of global technology and business leadership experience. His expertise spans designing advanced technologies for telecommunications, IT services, and healthcare while building strategic partnerships and driving digital transformation initiatives.

name: Paul D. Nielsen
title: Adjunct Faculty; Director and Chief Executive Officer of CMU's Software Engineering Institute
bio: Dr. Paul D. Nielsen is the Director and CEO of Carnegie Mellon's Software Engineering Institute, a federally funded research center advancing software and cybersecurity for the nation. His research focuses on software architecture, systems engineering integration, cyber security, and organizational resiliency, particularly for complex and autonomous systems.

name: Illah Nourbakhsh
title: Professor of Robotics
bio: Illah Nourbakhsh is Professor of Robotics and Director of the CREATE Lab, which develops community-based robotics projects to empower individuals and communities through educational and social robotics applications. His research spans from autonomous mobile robots to creative robotics fusion programs, with projects engaging over 40,000 people globally in citizen science and meaningful social change.

name: Timothy O'Loughlin
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Tim O'Loughlin is an adjunct faculty member with 30 years of senior-level experience in public service and the oil and gas industry. His expertise spans public policy, renewable energy development, urban planning, and government management, with particular focus on climate change legislation and sustainability initiatives.

name: Kyle O'Meara
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Kyle O'Meara is a Senior Member of the Technical Staff at the Software Engineering Institute's CERT Coordination Center, where he researches and analyzes current and emerging threats to national security with a focus on exploits and malware. He brings extensive experience from roles at FireEye, Stroz Friedberg, and the NSA in cyber defense, threat analysis, and digital forensics.

name: Tommy Oliver
title: CEO, Confluential Films
bio: Tommy Oliver is an Emmy and Sundance-winning filmmaker, entrepreneur, and photographer whose work spans film, television, media, and documentary. He is the Founder and CEO of Confluential Films, a production company creating culturally specific and universally resonant entertainment.

name: Madhu Pawar
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Madhu Pawar is an impact-driven leader at Google leading sales engineering and customer analytics for the $50 billion Adwords business, spearheading machine learning applications and user experience simplification. With 12 years at McKinsey & Company and experience in software development and risk advisory, she brings expertise in digital transformation, healthcare technology, and large-scale process digitization.

name: Allyce Pinchback
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Dr. Allyce Pinchback is an award-winning education scholar and Co-Founder of Black Women for Better Education, dedicated to ensuring high-quality education for Black children in Pittsburgh. With over fifteen years in non-profit and education sectors, her expertise spans professional development, organizational capacity building, and community-engaged educational advocacy.

name: Cheryl Platz
title: Creative Director
bio: Cheryl Platz is an internationally renowned designer, author, and actress known for her work on cutting-edge games and digital products including Amazon Alexa, the Sims franchise, and Pokémon TCG Live. She is a creative director, author of game development strategy guides, and founder of design education company Ideaplatz LLC.

name: Jeff M. Poulin
bio: Jeff M. Poulin is an educationalist, cultural administrator, and social entrepreneur whose work is grounded in social justice and seeks transformative local solutions to complex global challenges. He leads Creative Generation and interconnected organizations that redefine and expand creativity worldwide through artists, educators, researchers, and activists committed to using creativity for good.

name: Pat Quinn
bio: Pat Quinn is the founder and manager of Quinn Media Management, a consulting firm that helps international companies navigate Hollywood and develop entertainment projects across all media platforms. With over 20 years of experience in talent representation, production, and entertainment strategy, she specializes in packaging television series and formats for networks, streamers, and cable outlets.

name: David Radzanowski
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: David Radzanowski is an adjunct instructor at Carnegie Mellon's Heinz College with extensive experience in government and aerospace leadership, including serving as NASA's Chief Financial Officer managing a nearly $20 billion budget. His expertise spans space policy, financial operations, and strategic program management across federal agencies and defense contractors.

name: Raj Rajkumar
title: George Westinghouse Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
bio: Raj Rajkumar's research focuses on embedded real-time systems and wireless/sensor networks, with emphasis on building practical systems that can be analyzed and proven correct in terms of timeliness, power efficiency, and quality of service metrics. He was a principal contributor to Rate-Monotonic Analysis (RMA) and founded TimeSys, a company developing real-time embedded Linux solutions.

name: Henry Randall
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Dr. Randall is a transplant surgeon and adjunct instructor focusing on artificial intelligence and machine learning applications in healthcare and transplantation, with research on regulatory T cells in kidney transplant recipients. He mentors students and early-career faculty with particular emphasis on underrepresented minorities in medicine and surgery.

name: Giorleny Altamirano Rayo
title: Adjunct Faculty
department: Heinz College of Information Systems and Public Policy
bio: Giorleny Altamirano Rayo is a lawyer, political scientist, and applied researcher serving as Chief Data Scientist and Responsible Artificial Intelligence Official at the U.S. Department of State. Her research focuses on property rights, political-economic development in the Global South, and has been funded by Fulbright, NSF, and Carnegie Mellon University.

name: Rachel Rearick
title: Executive Director, Contemporary Craft
bio: Rachel Rearick is an arts and culture leader with a demonstrated commitment to creating inclusive spaces and building partnerships that support artists. She serves as Executive Director of Contemporary Craft, where she leads the organization toward strategic goals while advancing equity in the arts sector.

name: Adam Resnick
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Adam Resnick is a senior operations researcher at the RAND Corporation who applies quantitative analysis to policy issues in resource management, defense strategy, health care, and logistics. He teaches management science at Carnegie Mellon University and has expertise in military force structure, capability development, and disaster response planning.

name: Norman Sadeh
title: Professor
bio: Norman Sadeh is a Professor at Carnegie Mellon's School of Computer Science who is well known for pioneering work on AI-based privacy enhancing technologies, including privacy assistants, automated compliance tools, and NLP-based solutions that have influenced major tech companies and regulatory agencies. His research interests span cybersecurity, online privacy, human-AI interaction, AI governance, mobile computing, and the Internet of Things, with a track record of translating academic innovations into commercial products protecting tens of millions of users worldwide.

name: Costa Samaras
title: Associate Professor
bio: Costa Samaras is an associate professor who directs the Center for Engineering and Resilience for Climate Adaptation, analyzing how energy technology and infrastructure designs affect energy use, climate resilience, and environmental outcomes. His research spans energy systems, climate change, automation, and defense analysis, with expertise in low-carbon transitions, electric vehicles, and national security implications of energy policy.

name: Mario Saviddes
title: Research Professor; Director – CyLab Biometrics Center
bio: Mario Saviddes is a Research Professor and Director of the CyLab Biometrics Center whose research focuses on biometric recognition systems using face, iris, fingerprint, and palm print modalities for secure user authentication. His work develops robust algorithms to handle variations in biometric appearance and reduces computational complexity for implementation on small form-factor devices.

name: Grant Schumock
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Grant Schumock is an associate data scientist at the Software Engineering Institute whose research interests include deep learning, time series forecasting, and dynamical systems modeling. He holds a Ph.D. in Biostatistics from Johns Hopkins University and a B.S. in Nuclear Engineering from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign.

name: Moshe Schwartz
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Moshe Schwartz is a specialist in defense acquisitions at the Congressional Research Service with extensive expertise in major defense acquisition programs and acquisition reform. He has written numerous reports and testified before Congress on defense policy, contracting, and government operations.

name: Bill Sermons
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Bill Sermons is Vice President for Research & Evaluation for Volunteers of America with extensive experience in nonprofit advocacy and human services organizations. His research focuses on documenting racial disparities in public policy through data analysis and open source tools.

name: Shelley Ong Teshima
bio: Shelley Ong Teshima is a senior marketing and brand leader with over 20 years of experience building and transforming brands across technology, consumer, and social-impact organizations. Her work intersects strategy and execution, translating insights into campaigns and programs that drive business outcomes and cultural impact.

name: Josh Testa
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Josh Testa is the Senior Associate Director of Organizational Research and Data Analytics at Heinz College, overseeing strategic research efforts using data analytics and quantitative methods. His research examines higher education policy, finance, and performance, particularly how state-level funding policies influence administrative behavior in colleges and universities.

name: Seema Thakker
title: Brand Impact Director
bio: Seema Thakker is a brand impact director at Enso Collaborative with extensive experience in marketing, media partnerships, and social impact initiatives. Her career spans production at major networks, affiliate marketing leadership, and creative agency work focused on aligning business success with social outcomes.

name: Gregory J. Touhill
title: Faculty Member; Director of the Software Engineering Institute's (SEI) CERT Division
bio: Gregory Touhill is the director of the SEI's CERT Division, leading researchers and engineers to investigate software security vulnerabilities and develop cutting-edge cybersecurity training and practices. A 30-year Air Force veteran and former Chief Information Security Officer of the U.S. federal government, he brings extensive expertise in cybersecurity strategy, critical infrastructure protection, and information security leadership.

name: James Traut
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Jim Traut is an Adjunct Professor at Heinz College teaching in the Chief Risk Officer Certification and Master of Information System Management programs. His research and business initiatives focus on implementing innovative risk and reward awareness methodologies through cross-discipline data analytics and decision support systems for organizational value creation and resilience.

name: K. Craig Trout
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: K. Craig Trout is a highly experienced C-suite executive with extensive background in hospitals, managed care organizations, and physician practice management companies. He brings practical industry expertise and consulting experience to the classroom.

name: Brett Tucker
title: Adjunct Faculty; Technical Manager of Cyber Risk Management
bio: Brett Tucker is the Technical Manager of Cyber Risk Management at CMU's Software Engineering Institute, leading research and development focused on improving the security and resilience of critical infrastructure. He teaches executive and graduate level courses at Heinz College and brings extensive experience from roles at Westinghouse Electric, the CIA, and the U.S. Navy.

name: Dustin Updyke
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Dustin Updyke is a Senior Engineer and AI Team Lead at Carnegie Mellon's Software Engineering Institute who builds applied AI, modeling and simulation, and cyber mission systems. He leads cross-functional teams supporting government and defense stakeholders, bringing expertise in innovation leadership and large-scale system delivery.

name: Jordan Usdan
title: Adjunct Instructor
department: Artificial Intelligence: Business and Public Policy
bio: Jordan helps organizations navigate the impact of technology on business and society, drawing from 10 years at Microsoft where he co-founded an Innovation Studio and worked on AI, autonomous systems, and strategic growth opportunities. He previously served in government at the FCC on broadband and digital equity initiatives, and co-founded EveryoneOn, a non-profit dedicated to eliminating the digital divide.

name: Francois Vallerian
title: Vice President of Research
bio: Francois is a seasoned entertainment research executive with 20 years of global expertise in content analytics across networks, studios, and distributors. He is passionate about film and television, leveraging data-driven insights to support content sales and strategy.

name: Prem Viswanathan
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Prem Viswanathan is a Senior Data Scientist at IBM Watson Health specializing in developing machine learning and artificial intelligence solutions for healthcare and life sciences. His work encompasses patient risk modeling, acute event prediction, population health prediction, and full-stack AI solutions deployed to the cloud.

name: Paula Wagner
title: Adjunct Professor
bio: Paula Wagner is an accomplished entertainment industry executive and producer who has held leadership roles at major studios and production companies, including as co-founder of Cruise/Wagner Productions and CEO of United Artists Entertainment. She brings decades of experience in film, television, theater, and new media to her teaching at Carnegie Mellon's Master of Entertainment Industry Management program.

name: George Werbacher
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: George Werbacher is a cybersecurity professional and leader with extensive industry experience in cloud security, risk management, and program development. He currently leads the Business Information Security program at Blackbaud and is passionate about educating the next generation of cybersecurity leaders.

name: Jimmy Williams
title: Executive Director and Distinguished Service Professor
department: Engineering and Technology Innovation Management Program
bio: Dr. Williams is an Executive Director and Distinguished Service Professor with extensive industry experience in engineering, technology innovation, and business development across Fortune 500 companies including Pall Corporation, Alcoa, and Boeing. His expertise spans innovation management, manufacturing optimization, portfolio management, and strategic growth initiatives in aerospace, industrial products, and defense sectors.

name: Kevin Yoder
bio: Kevin Yoder brings extensive experience from the entertainment industry, having served as Executive Vice President of Theatrical Strategy for National Research Group and overseeing marketing research for major studios including 20th Century Fox Films. His career spans decades of work in marketing research, strategic analysis, and consumer insights applied to theatrical releases and film franchises.

name: Wiam Younes
title: Information Security Training and Awareness Coordinator
bio: Wiam Younes is an Information Security Training and Awareness Coordinator who manages comprehensive information security training programs including online courses and awareness initiatives. Her research focuses on cybersecurity education, staff development, and instructional technology, with particular emphasis on K-12 faculty and staff training.

name: Sakir Yucel
title: Adjunct Faculty
department: Heinz College of Information Systems and Public Policy

name: Phil Zarone
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Phil Zarone is an adjunct faculty member and partner at Horty, Springer & Mattern law firm, specializing in healthcare law and regulatory compliance. He works extensively with hospitals and physician leaders on federal and state regulatory requirements, peer review, and credentialing matters.

name: Mary Ellen Benedict
title: Adjunct Faculty
department: Heinz College of Information Systems and Public Policy
bio: Mary Ellen Benedict is a Professor of Economics at Bowling Green State University and an adjunct professor at Carnegie Mellon's Heinz College. Her research focuses on gender differences in self-employment, unionization effects on earnings, and income inequality across various sectors.

name: James R. Benté
title: Adjunct Faculty
school: Heinz College of Public Policy and Management
bio: James R. Benté is an adjunct assistant professor who teaches in the Master of Medical Management program at Carnegie Mellon's Heinz College. He brings extensive experience in healthcare management and quality improvement from leadership roles at major health systems, with expertise in strategic planning, operational performance, and accreditation.

name: Liz Berlin
title: Lecturer
bio: Liz Berlin is a founding member of the band Rusted Root and co-owner of Mr. Smalls Theatre and Recording Studio in Pittsburgh. She teaches in the Master of Entertainment Industry Management program and founded Creative Life Support, a nonprofit providing music education and career development to youth and young adults.

name: Michael W. Bigrigg
title: Principal Research Scientist
bio: Dr. Bigrigg is a Principal Research Scientist at General Dynamics Mission Systems managing research in data science, text and network analytics, and language systems. Previously a Project Scientist at Carnegie Mellon for over 15 years, he has collaborated across multiple departments and institutes on complex engineered systems and reliable information systems.

name: Robert Brichacek
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Bob Brichacek is a Senior Consultant with over 35 years of IT experience, specializing in enterprise healthcare data warehouses that enable patient-centric analytics and care. He brings expertise in relational database systems, business architecture, and technical consulting across Fortune 100 companies in banking, healthcare, insurance, and other industries.

name: Chris W. Brussalis
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Dr. Brussalis is an engaged scholar-practitioner with over 34 years of experience in management consulting, higher education leadership, and public service. He brings extensive expertise in advising Fortune 500 corporations, governments, and institutions while serving as president of Point Park University.

name: Mark Christopher
title: Writer, Director of Film, TV, and Stage
bio: Mark Christopher is an accomplished writer and director working across film, television, and stage in the U.S., Brazil, and Europe. His career spans feature films including the cult classic 54, television pilots for major networks, award-winning short films, and original stage musicals.

name: J. Zachary Ciccone
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Zachary teaches entrepreneurship, negotiation, and workplace communications with a focus on turning founders' vision into operational excellence. His approach combines social psychology with practical business design, emphasizing scalable ventures built on people, purpose, and sustainable profitability.

name: Jacqueline Cohen
title: Emeritus Research Professor
bio: Dr. Cohen's research analyzes crime and criminal justice policy, including demographic trends in crime, criminal careers, and incapacitative effects of incarceration. Her recent work focuses on illegal drug use, firearms in youth violence, and the effectiveness of policing and housing strategies in reducing violence.

name: Constantine Cois
title: Adjunct Faculty
department: Heinz College of Information Systems and Public Policy
bio: Constantine Cois is an adjunct professor at CMU's Heinz College teaching Object Oriented Analysis and Design. He specializes in machine learning and computational systems, with expertise spanning biomedical image analysis, software architecture, and cloud technologies.

name: Ben Collier
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Ben Collier is an Assistant Teaching Professor of Business Analytics who teaches machine learning, data science, and analytics with emphasis on applying modern AI methods to real-world problems in business and public policy. His research examines how data, algorithms, and organizational structures shape decision-making and performance, informed by extensive industry experience in applied data science at organizations like UPMC and Duolingo.

name: Lauren Cooper
title: Adjunct Faculty
bio: Lauren Cooper is a cybersecurity engineer at Carnegie Mellon's Software Engineering Institute, CERT Division, specializing in security practices and policy. As an alumna of Heinz College with an MS in information security policy and management, she brings practical expertise to her adjunct faculty role.

name: Mohamed Bouaouina
title: Associate Area Head, Biological Sciences / Associate Teaching Professor, Biological Sciences

name: Bianca Brown
title: Postdoctoral Fellow in Academic and Disciplinary Writing

name: Kira Dreher
title: Associate Dean, Community Excellence / Associate Teaching Professor, English

name: Muhammad Farooqi
title: Area Head, Business Administration / Teaching Professor, Finance

name: Hend Gedawy
title: Visiting Assistant Teaching Professor, Computer Science

name: Ki-Won Haan
title: Assistant Teaching Professor, Organizational Behavior and Theory

name: Agustín Indaco
title: Associate Area Head, Business Administration / Associate Teaching Professor, Economics

name: J Mcginnis
title: Distinguished Career Professor, Business Management Communication

name: Taeyong Park
title: Assistant Teaching Professor, Statistics and Data Science

name: Silvia Pessoa
title: Co-Area Head, Arts and Sciences / Teaching Professor, English

name: Dudley Reynolds
title: Senior Associate Dean, Education / Teaching Professor, English

name: Michael Trick
title: Dean / Harry B. and James H. Higgins Professor of Operations Research

name: Ihab Younis
title: Area Head, Biological Sciences / Teaching Professor, Biological Sciences

name: Yonatan Bisk
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Yonatan Bisk is an Assistant Professor whose research focuses on the intersection of natural language processing, robotics, and embodied AI, enabling machines to interpret and act upon language in physical and social environments. His work advances theory of mind and embodied language systems, exploring how language interacts with vision, action, and reasoning.

name: Ralf Brown
title: Principal Systems Scientist
bio: Dr. Ralf Brown is a Principal Systems Scientist at CMU's Language Technologies Institute with over three decades of experience in machine translation, language identification, and computational linguistics. His research focuses on example-based machine translation, multilingual processing, and low-resource language technologies, including the creation of widely-used open-source systems like CMU-EBMT and the LTI LangID Corpus for over 2,000 languages.

name: Carlos Busso
title: Professor
bio: Carlos Busso is a Professor and director of the Multimodal Speech Processing Laboratory at CMU, focusing on human-centered multimodal machine intelligence and its applications in speech-based interfaces. His research spans affective computing, social signal processing, multimodal learning, and language technology applications in healthcare and mental health.

name: Justine Cassell
title: Professor
bio: Justine Cassell is a professor who studies human-computer interaction and natural language technologies, with a focus on conversational and embodied agents. Her research explores how people interact with computational systems through multimodal communication and dialogue.

name: Mona Diab
title: Full Professor
bio: Mona Diab is the Director of the Language Technologies Institute at Carnegie Mellon University and a globally recognized expert in computational linguistics and natural language processing. Her research focuses on advancing trustworthy NLP and responsible AI systems, particularly in low-resource and multilingual contexts, including controllable natural language generation, cross-lingual processing, and Arabic language technologies.

name: Fernando Diaz
title: Associate Professor
researchAreas: fairness and ethics in language technology
bio: Fernando Diaz is an Associate Professor at Carnegie Mellon University's Language Technologies Institute and a globally recognized leader in information retrieval, recommender systems, and the societal impacts of artificial intelligence. His research focuses on developing advanced retrieval and ranking models while investigating fairness, ethics, and evaluation methodologies in language technology.

name: Scott Fahlman
title: Professor Emeritus
bio: Scott Fahlman is an AI researcher with broad interests spanning knowledge representation, planning, image processing, and machine learning. His current work focuses on Scone, an open-source knowledge representation system designed for efficiency and scalability in knowledge-based systems and natural language understanding.

name: Robert Frederking
title: Associate Dean for PhD Programs and Chair for MLT Program
bio: Dr. Frederking has dedicated over three decades to advancing machine translation, multilingual information processing, and computational linguistics, with a focus on innovative approaches to machine translation and rapid deployment of speech-to-speech translation systems. His work has earned recognition for real-world impact in enabling communication in low-resource languages and advancing translingual information access.

name: Daniel Fried
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Daniel Fried is an Assistant Professor at CMU's Language Technologies Institute focusing on enabling people to use language to get computers to carry out useful tasks in grounded and interactive settings, with emphasis on pragmatics and code generation. His recent work concentrates on making programming more communicative and developing LLM agents to be better collaborators.

name: Daphne Ippolito
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Daphne Ippolito is an Assistant Professor at Carnegie Mellon University focusing on the tradeoffs and limitations of generating text with large language models, particularly regarding privacy and security issues in language generation systems. Her research also examines how training data affects model capabilities and develops better evaluation systems for realistic assessment of language models.

name: Alon Lavie
title: Distinguished Career Professor
bio: Alon Lavie is a Distinguished Career Professor at the Language Technologies Institute whose research focuses on machine translation, speech translation, and multilingual natural language processing systems. His work aims to advance computational approaches to understanding and translating human languages across diverse modalities and language pairs.

name: Lori Levin
title: Research Professor
bio: Lori Levin is a Research Professor focused on computational linguistics with an emphasis on documenting endangered languages, linguistic typology, and developing multilingual NLP systems. Her work addresses language technologies for low-resource languages, leveraging linguistic universals and typology to build NLP systems for new languages on short timelines.

name: Lei Li
title: Associate Professor
bio: Lei Li is an Associate Professor at CMU's Language Technologies Institute whose research advances multilingual NLP, AI security, large language models, and AI-powered drug discovery and protein design. He is known for developing scalable machine translation systems like VolcTrans and LLM watermarking techniques for responsible AI development.

name: Teruko Mitamura
title: Research Professor
bio: Teruko Mitamura is a Research Professor at Carnegie Mellon University's Language Technologies Institute whose research spans natural language processing, machine translation, and question answering systems with a focus on developing multilingual and multimodal AI systems. She has led numerous high-impact projects including the DARPA-funded KAIROS/CHRONOS initiative for schema-based reasoning and the AIST Bridge Project for integrating textual, visual, and structured information.

name: Louis-Philippe Morency
title: Leonardo Associate Professor of Computer Science
bio: Louis-Philippe Morency's research lies at the intersection of machine learning, computer vision, and computational linguistics, focusing on enabling computers to analyze, recognize, and predict subtle human communicative behaviors during social interactions. His work addresses behavioral dynamics, multimodal dynamics, interpersonal dynamics, and societal dynamics through probabilistic models designed to learn temporal dependencies across behaviors, modalities, and interlocutors, with applications in education, healthcare, and social multimedia.

name: David Mortensen
title: Assistant Research Professor
bio: David Mortensen is an Assistant Research Professor in the Language Technologies Institute whose work sits at the intersection of natural language processing, speech processing, phonology, morphology, and language change. He has published widely in both linguistics and computational linguistics venues, including a Best Paper Award at ACL 2025.

name: Graham Neubig
title: Associate Professor
bio: Graham Neubig is an associate professor whose research focuses on natural language processing and large language models, including advances in model capabilities and applications to tasks such as software development. His overarching goal is to break down barriers in human-human and human-machine communication through NLP technologies, with particular emphasis on learning from naturally occurring data and associating language with other modalities.

name: Eric Nyberg
title: Professor
bio: Eric Nyberg is a Professor and internationally recognized leader in natural language processing, information retrieval, and AI-powered question answering. His research focuses on intelligent systems capable of understanding and responding to human language with applications in education, healthcare, and beyond.

name: Kemal Oflazer
title: Teaching Professor
bio: Kemal Oflazer is a Teaching Professor at Carnegie Mellon University's Language Technologies Institute whose research focuses on computational morphology, natural language processing for low-resource languages, and the intersection of language technologies with large-scale AI systems. His work includes developing finite-state morphological analyzers, machine translation systems for morphologically complex languages, and exploring the capabilities of large language models in generating semantically novel forms.

name: Adam Paulisick
title: Distinguished Service Professor
bio: Adam Paulisick is a Distinguished Service Professor at the Language Technologies Institute specializing in language technologies and computational approaches to linguistic problems. His work spans natural language processing, speech processing, and the development of language technology systems.

name: Bhiksha Raj
title: Professor
bio: Bhiksha Raj is a Professor at Carnegie Mellon University's Language Technologies Institute whose research spans machine learning, speech processing, computer vision, and time-series analysis with applications from audio analysis to deep learning and quantum computing. He has contributed to fundamental advancements in robust speech recognition, privacy-preserving machine learning, and neural network architectures, with recent work exploring the intersection of large-scale machine learning models and signal processing.

name: Carolyn P. Rosé
title: Kavčić-Moura Professor of Language Technologies and Human-Computer Interaction
bio: Carolyn Rosé is the Kavčić-Moura Professor of Language Technologies and Human-Computer Interaction whose research advances Sociotechnical Artificial Intelligence by exploring human-AI complementarity and multi-agent human-AI teaming. Her work bridges computational linguistics, sociolinguistics, and learning sciences to develop AI systems that monitor, analyze, and support communication processes with applications in education, health, and workplace collaboration.

name: Alexander Rudnicky
title: Research Professor Emeritus
bio: Alexander Rudnicky is a Research Professor Emeritus whose work centers on interactive systems that use speech, focusing on implicit learning, automatic error detection and recovery, and language design principles for speech-based interfaces. His research explores how speech can be effectively integrated into computer interfaces across various applications including human-robot collaboration and information access systems.

name: Maarten Sap
title: Assistant Professor
researchAreas: fairness and ethics in language technology
bio: Maarten Sap is an Assistant Professor at CMU's Language Technologies Institute who develops natural language processing systems enhanced with social intelligence and examines social biases in language. His research focuses on ethical AI development, narrative dynamics, and improving AI moderation tools through understanding toxicity and cultural awareness in language.

name: Michael Shamos
title: Distinguished Career Professor
bio: Michael Shamos is a Distinguished Career Professor whose research focuses on generating and proving newly-discovered mathematical theorems, particularly in number theory, through computational methods. His unique approach combines mathematical rigor with computational creativity, and he also contributes expertise in the legal aspects of technology, electronic voting security, and intellectual property.

name: Rita Singh
title: Research Professor
bio: Rita Singh develops technology for automated discovery and analysis of information encoded in voice signals for voice intelligence applications. Her work focuses on speaker profiling, biomarker discovery, and AI systems that extract information about human physical, physiological, medical, and psychological parameters from voice signals.

name: Emma Strubell
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Emma Strubell is an Assistant Professor at Carnegie Mellon University's Language Technologies Institute, where their research focuses on efficient and equitable natural language processing (NLP). They investigate carbon use and sustainability in computing systems, particularly through the NSF Expeditions in Computing Awards.

name: Alexander Waibel
title: Professor (On Leave)
bio: Dr. Alexander Waibel is a Professor at Carnegie Mellon University and Karlsruhe Institute of Technology who directs the International Center for Advanced Communication Technologies (interACT). His research focuses on developing advanced machine learning algorithms to improve human-human and human-machine communication, with pioneering contributions including the Time-Delay Neural Network (first convolutional neural network) and the first speech translation systems.

name: Shinji Watanabe
title: Associate Professor
bio: Shinji Watanabe is an Associate Professor at CMU's Language Technologies Institute whose research advances speech processing, conversational AI, and end-to-end neural models for speech and language applications. He is the leader of the ESPnet open-source toolkit and has made groundbreaking contributions to speech recognition, speech translation, speaker diarization, and speech enhancement.

name: Sean Welleck
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Sean Welleck is an Assistant Professor developing learning, inference, and evaluation algorithms for large language models with emphasis on mathematical reasoning and code generation. His research spans neural text generation, formal theorem proving, and AI for mathematics, contributing foundational work featured in NeurIPS, ICLR, and EMNLP.

name: Eric P. Xing
title: Professor (On Leave)
bio: Professor Xing develops machine learning and statistical methodology for quantitative models of biological systems and intelligent systems for vision, information retrieval, and NLP. His research spans foundations of statistical learning, large-scale information systems, and computational biology with applications to genome analysis and personalized medicine.

name: Chenyan Xiong
title: Associate Professor
bio: Chenyan Xiong is an Associate Professor at CMU's Language Technologies Institute researching foundation and large language models, information retrieval, and healthcare AI. His work encompasses neural IR, dense retrieval, efficient model pretraining, and has been recognized at premier conferences like ICLR, ACL, and SIGIR.

name: Yiming Yang
title: Professor
bio: Yiming Yang is a Professor at Carnegie Mellon University's Language Technologies Institute focusing on advancing machine learning, natural language processing, and AI for Science with expertise in enhancing large language models through advanced training and reasoning. Her research spans transformer-based LLMs, neural network optimization, knowledge-enhanced graph neural networks, and has earned over 73,000 citations with an h-index of 81.

name: William Cohen
title: Consulting Professor
bio: William Cohen's research focuses on information integration and machine learning, particularly information extraction, text categorization, and learning from large datasets with non-trivial structure. He leads projects including NELL, a large-scale information extraction system, and ProPPR, a locally groundable probabilistic first-order logic.

name: Matt Gormley
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Matt Gormley is an Assistant Teaching Professor in the Language Technologies Institute at CMU. His research focuses on machine learning and natural language processing, with emphasis on structured prediction and neural network approaches.

name: Eduard Hovy
title: Professor
bio: Eduard Hovy is a leading researcher in natural language processing and computational linguistics, focusing on semantic understanding and knowledge representation from text. His work spans information extraction, question answering, and the application of machine learning to language understanding tasks.

name: Florian Metze
title: Adjunct Professor
bio: Florian Metze is an adjunct professor at the Language Technologies Institute focused on speech and language processing technologies. His research spans speech recognition, machine learning, and multilingual language understanding systems.

name: Jack Mostow
title: Research Professor Emeritus
bio: Jack Mostow is a Research Professor Emeritus at the Language Technologies Institute and Robotics Institute, specializing in natural language processing, machine learning, and educational technology. His research focuses on developing intelligent systems that can understand and process human language for applications in education and robotics.

name: Richard Stern
title: Professor
bio: Richard Stern's research focuses on automatic speech recognition and signal processing in the auditory system, with emphasis on making speech recognition robust to challenging acoustic conditions. His work integrates acoustic signal processing, auditory perception modeling, and practical applications including noise cancellation, microphone arrays, and hearing impairment treatment.

name: Yulia Tsvetkov
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Yulia Tsvetkov is a researcher specializing in natural language processing with a focus on multilingual systems and machine translation. Her work explores computational methods for understanding and processing human language across diverse languages and contexts.

name: Yuvraj Agarwal
title: Associate Professor
bio: Yuvraj Agarwal is a tenured Associate Professor and director of SynergyLabs who focuses on building hardware and software systems that are safe, secure, and privacy-sensitive for smart buildings, IoT, and mobile devices. His research has achieved significant real-world impact, including deployment at scale and influence on U.S. government cybersecurity policy.

name: Hammad Ahmad
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Hammad Ahmad is an Assistant Teaching Professor focused on software engineering and cognition, particularly using cognitive science to enhance developer tools and improve computer science teaching methods. His research aims to cultivate a growth mindset in students while exploring the cognitive basis of programming to support more effective learning and tool development.

name: Swarnalatha Ashok
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Swarnalatha Ashok is an Associate Teaching Professor and Associate Director of the Master of Software Engineering program at Carnegie Mellon, bringing extensive experience in software engineering education and industry practice. She has taught courses in API design, cloud computing, platform engineering, and software architecture, while supervising industry projects and consulting for organizations including Singapore's Civil Aviation Authority.

name: Andrew Begel
title: Associate Professor
bio: Andrew Begel is an Associate Professor researching how technology and AI can extend capabilities for people with disabilities, with current focus on inclusive technology workplaces and responsible remote work tools. His work applies HCI techniques to software development processes, studying topics including biometrics, affect-based engineering, collaborative development, and programming education.

name: Jim Berardone
title: Professor of Product Management
bio: Jim Berardone is a special faculty member of Carnegie Mellon's Master of Software Engineering programs who teaches product management and business strategy. With over 20 years of experience building technology businesses across startups and multinational companies, he brings practical expertise in product creation, launch, and customer development to his teaching.

name: Travis Breaux
title: Associate Professor
bio: Travis Breaux is an Associate Professor whose research develops methods and tools for creating correct software specifications and ensuring systems conform to specifications in a transparent and trustworthy manner. His work focuses on demonstrating compliance with accessibility, privacy, and security laws and standards, particularly through analyzing policies and regulations to extract software requirements.

name: Fraser Brown
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Fraser Brown is interested in security through program correctness, having worked on tools for finding bugs in security-critical systems like web browsers and verifying pieces of production systems. His current research focuses on compiler correctness and compilation for new domains.

name: Lorrie Cranor
title: Professor
bio: Lorrie Cranor is a leading researcher in online privacy and usable privacy and security who directs the CyLab Usable Privacy and Security Laboratory. She has co-authored over 150 research papers, founded the Symposium on Usable Privacy and Security, and is an ACM and IEEE Fellow recognized for her contributions to privacy research and education.

name: Fei Fang
title: Associate Professor
bio: Fei Fang is an Associate Professor whose research lies at the intersection of computational game theory and machine learning, with applications to security, sustainability, and mobility domains. Her work contributes to AI for Social Good and has been recognized with numerous awards including the NSF CAREER Award, Sloan Research Fellowship, and IJCAI-21 Computers and Thought Award.

name: Nick Frollini
title: Communications Instructor
bio: Nick Frollini is a Communications Instructor at Carnegie Mellon's Master of Software Engineering programs where he directs communications and leadership education. He brings over 14 years of experience in software research management, technology transfer, and venture capital to his teaching of presentation, negotiation, and communication skills.

name: Hana Habib
title: Associate Director of Privacy Engineering Academic Programs
bio: Hana Habib is an Associate Director of Privacy Engineering Academic Programs at Carnegie Mellon University. She works at the intersection of privacy, security, and human factors in computer systems.

name: Hoda Heidari
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Hoda's research focuses on the societal and economic aspects of artificial intelligence, particularly issues of unfairness and inexplicability in machine learning. She completed her PhD in computer and information science at the University of Pennsylvania and holds an M.Sc. in statistics from Wharton.

name: Austin Henley
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Dr. Austin Henley is an Associate Teaching Professor focused on building AI-powered developer tools with novel user interfaces to improve developer feedback loops. His teaching emphasizes experiential learning through hands-on projects and real-world problem-solving, informed by his background as a startup CTO and Microsoft researcher.

name: James Herbsleb
title: Professor
bio: James Herbsleb is a Professor studying the intersection of software engineering, computer-supported cooperative work, and socio-technical systems, with focus on geographically distributed development teams and large-scale open source development. His research examines the complex relationships between human collaboration and the software systems that humans design and use, including work on platform ecosystems, coordination requirements, and scientific software sharing.

name: Michael Hilton
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Michael Hilton is an Associate Teaching Professor focused on improving developers' lives through teaching and research on development practices and processes. His research emphasizes understanding continuous integration, developer workflows, and software engineering practices at startups.

name: Eunsuk Kang
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Eunsuk Kang is an Assistant Professor who works at the intersection of software engineering and formal methods, leveraging rigorous modeling and analysis techniques to design software systems that are safe, secure, and reliable. His research interests span software design, requirements, modeling, specification and verification, system safety, security, and cyber-physical systems.

name: Ram Konduru
title: Director of Executive Education, School of Computer Science; Director of UDL Project, Software and Societal Systems

name: Christian Kästner
title: Associate Professor
bio: Christian Kästner is an associate professor studying software engineering for systems with ML components, open-source sustainability, and software supply-chain security. His research combines empirical studies with program analysis and tool building to understand modularity limits and complexity caused by variability in software systems.

name: Claire Le Goues
title: Associate Professor
bio: Claire Le Goues is an Associate Professor whose research spans software engineering and programming languages, focusing on how to construct, maintain, evolve, and improve high-quality software systems. Her work combines program analysis and search-based techniques to enable automatic program repair, assurance, and testing across diverse software domains from open source to embedded and robotics systems.

name: Ganesh Mani
title: Adjunct Instructor
bio: Ganesh Mani is a global thought leader helping scale human expertise via AI across multiple application areas, particularly in financial services. He has pioneered innovative AI/ML techniques for asset management and founded an early AI-based asset management boutique that was acquired by SSgA.

name: Heather Miller
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Heather Miller is interested in distributed and concurrent computation from a programming languages perspective, with a focus on enabling the construction of complex distributed systems through composition of well-understood components. Her work includes programming models, type systems, and tools for functional distributed systems, asynchronous programming, and deterministic concurrent dataflow.

name: Eduardo Miranda
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Eduardo Miranda is an Associate Teaching Professor with extensive industry experience in software development, project management, and leadership at companies like Ericsson and Lockheed Martin. His research focuses on software engineering practices, project management methodologies, and formal methods including Petri Nets for software development and estimation techniques.

name: Riccardo Paccagnella
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Riccardo Paccagnella is an assistant professor at Carnegie Mellon University focusing on system and hardware security. His recent work uncovers and mitigates microarchitectural vulnerabilities and their impact on secure software.

name: Rohan Padhye
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Rohan Padhye is an Assistant Professor in the Software and Societal Systems Department at Carnegie Mellon University. His research focuses on software engineering, program analysis, and testing techniques.

name: Scott Pavetti
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Scott Pavetti is an Assistant Teaching Professor in Carnegie Mellon's Master of Software Engineering program with extensive industry experience in software development and quality assurance. His research and teaching interests span software architecture, design practices, security automation, and leadership in software engineering teams.

name: William Scherlis
title: Professor
bio: William Scherlis is a professor of computer science at Carnegie Mellon and special advisor to the Software Engineering Institute, with expertise in software assurance, cybersecurity, and software analysis. He previously served as director of DARPA's Information Innovation Office and led the Software and Societal Systems Department at CMU for 12 years.

name: Bradley Schmerl
title: Principal Systems Scientist
bio: Bradley Schmerl's research focuses on advancing software engineering to handle the challenges of composing distributed heterogeneous components, continuously running programs that must be recomposed on the fly, and integrating components written in various languages and styles. His work involves developing tools and techniques that provide reusable, analyzable, and automatic support for software composition.

name: Ashok Kumar Seetharaman
title: Director of Digital Government, Executive Education
bio: Ashok is a digital government pracademic with over 40 years of experience in government and private sector organizations across Asia, specializing in digital government transformation, public service design, and technology solutions. He is a member of the UN's eGovernment Ranking Committee and has engaged thousands of leaders through executive education programs at Carnegie Mellon and the National University of Singapore.

name: Michael Skirpan
title: Assistant Teaching Professor
bio: Michael Skirpan is a computing ethicist and educator whose work focuses on pedagogical interventions for training technical experts on ethics in practical engineering challenges, auditing and governance of AI and data systems, and communicating technological risks to the public. He serves as executive director of Community Forge and develops science fiction and immersive experiences that explore ethical challenges of technological adoption.

name: Josh Sunshine
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Josh Sunshine is an Assistant Professor interested in the intersection of programming languages and software engineering, with particular focus on understanding factors that influence the usability of reusable software components. His dissertation explored the usability of software libraries with ordering constraints and API protocols.

name: Christopher Timperley
title: Associate Teaching Professor
bio: Christopher Timperley develops techniques for automatically detecting, locating, and repairing faults in large-scale software systems using search-based approaches. His research focuses on applying these software engineering methods to robotics and autonomous vehicle systems.

name: Ben Titzer
title: Principal Researcher
bio: Ben Titzer is a Principal Researcher focusing on systems programming languages, compilers, virtual machines, and managed runtime systems. He co-founded WebAssembly in 2014 and is developing the Wizard Engine, a new WebAssembly engine designed for research, education, and competitive performance.

name: Bogdan Vasilescu
title: Associate Professor
bio: Bogdan is an Associate Professor studying how distributed and diverse teams develop software effectively, using data-driven approaches from empirical software engineering, social computing, and CSCW. His research analyzes big data from open source platforms like GitHub using mixed methods to develop theories about software engineering processes, team productivity, and code quality.

name: Steven Wu
title: Assistant Professor
bio: Steven Wu is an Assistant Professor studying the societal aspects of machine learning, with a focus on aligning machine learning with societal values such as privacy and fairness. His research examines how to make machine learning more reliable when algorithms interact with social and economic dynamics, using methods from machine learning, statistics, optimization, differential privacy, game theory, and mechanism design.

name: Thomas Bock
title: Post-Doctoral Associate
bio: Dr. Thomas Bock is a post-doctoral researcher focused on empirical software engineering, software evolution, and maintenance. His research examines coordination and communication in open-source projects, organizational patterns, developer networks, and how software is utilized in scientific research.

name: Catherine King
title: Post-Doctoral Fellow
bio: Catherine King is a Post-Doctoral Fellow in Policy at the Software and Societal Systems Department, focusing on the intersection of technology and society. Her research interests include dynamic network analysis, computational social science, social cybersecurity, and the study of elections, public opinion, and voting behavior.

name: Nandini Sharma
title: Post-Doctoral Associate
bio: Nandini Sharma is a Postdoctoral Associate studying technical and scientific work practices in distributed and decentralized environments, including open-source software communities and scientific laboratories. Her research examines how collaboration, knowledge hierarchies, and information infrastructures are shaped by socio-technical dynamics, with recent focus on accountability practices in open-source software ecosystems.
